```text
===root.go===
// Package cmd wires the cobra command tree. The root command dispatches between
// the one-shot CLI (arguments or piped stdin) and the interactive TUI (a TTY
// with no arguments); every path drives the shared engine layer.
package cmd

import (
	"context"
	"encoding/json"
	"fmt"
	"io"
	"os"
	"path/filepath"
	"strings"

	"github.com/spf13/cobra"
	"golang.org/x/term"

	tea "charm.land/bubbletea/v2"

	"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/config"
	"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/debug"
	"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/engine"
	"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/lang"
	"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/state"
	"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/store"
	"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/tui"
	"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/xdgpath"
)

var (
	flagTo           string
	flagFrom         string
	flagModel        string
	flagProvider     string
	flagEngine       string
	flagTier         string
	flagPreset       string
	flagInstructions string
	flagPair         bool
	flagPairWith     string
	flagLearn        bool
	flagJSON         bool
	flagNoHistory    bool
	flagDebug        bool
	flagSpeak        bool
	flagSpeakLang    string
)

// NewRootCmd builds the root command and its subcommands.
func NewRootCmd() *cobra.Command {
	root := &cobra.Command{
		Use:   "translate [text...]",
		Short: "Fast terminal translation (CLI + TUI)",
		Long: "translate is a fast CLI/TUI translator.\n\n" +
			"  translate \"hola mundo\" --to en    one-shot\n" +
			"  echo hola | translate --to en       pipe\n" +
			"  translate                           interactive TUI",
		Version:       buildVersion(),
		SilenceUsage:  true,
		SilenceErrors: true,
		// ArbitraryArgs lets bare text fall through to translation while exact
		// subcommands (config, lang, …) still route. Without it, cobra rejects
		// `translate "hola"` as an unknown command once subcommands exist.
		Args: cobra.ArbitraryArgs,
		RunE: runRoot,
	}
	f := root.PersistentFlags()
	f.StringVarP(&flagTo, "to", "t", "", "target language (fuzzy; e.g. en, chinese)")
	f.StringVarP(&flagFrom, "from", "f", "", "source language (\"auto\" to detect)")
	f.StringVar(&flagModel, "model", "", "LLM model id override")
	f.StringVar(&flagProvider, "provider", "", "provider name (e.g. copilot, ollama)")
	f.StringVar(&flagEngine, "engine", "", "engine: auto|<provider>|google|dict")
	f.StringVar(&flagTier, "tier", "", "model tier: default|fast|max")
	f.StringVar(&flagPreset, "preset", "", "LLM prompt style: concise|contextual|dictionary")
	f.StringVar(&flagInstructions, "instructions", "", "extra system-prompt guidance (domain focus, etc.)")
	f.BoolVar(&flagPair, "pair", false, "bidirectional mode: home-language input → --pair-with, else → --to")
	f.StringVar(&flagPairWith, "pair-with", "", "the other language for --pair (e.g. en)")
	f.BoolVar(&flagLearn, "learn", false, "learning mode: teach (native→foreign) or grammar-correct (foreign→native)")
	f.BoolVar(&flagJSON, "json", false, "emit the full result as JSON")
	f.BoolVar(&flagNoHistory, "no-history", false, "do not record this translation in history")
	f.BoolVar(&flagDebug, "debug", false, "log intermediate decisions (routing, engine choice, dict hit/miss)")
	f.BoolVarP(&flagSpeak, "speak", "s", false, "speak the foreign side of the result aloud (free TTS)")
	f.StringVar(&flagSpeakLang, "speak-lang", "", "force the spoken language (e.g. en, zh-TW)")

	root.SuggestionsMinimumDistance = 2
	root.AddCommand(newConfigCmd(), newLangCmd(), newDefineCmd(), newHistoryCmd(), newInitCmd(), newDictCmd(), newSpeakCmd())
	return root
}

// Execute runs the root command with the given (cancellable) context.
func Execute(ctx context.Context) error {
	return NewRootCmd().ExecuteContext(ctx)
}

// overrides collects the explicitly-set flag values.
func overrides() config.Overrides {
	return config.Overrides{
		Source:       flagFrom,
		Target:       flagTo,
		Engine:       flagEngine,
		Provider:     flagProvider,
		Model:        flagModel,
		Tier:         flagTier,
		Preset:       flagPreset,
		Instructions: flagInstructions,
		Pair:         flagPair,
		PairWith:     flagPairWith,
		Learn:        flagLearn,
		Debug:        flagDebug,
	}
}

// invocationMode reports whether this run is the one-shot CLI (arguments or piped
// stdin) or the interactive TUI (a TTY with no arguments). It mirrors the dispatch
// switch in runRoot and is computed before Resolve so the [cli]/[tui] overlay applies.
func invocationMode(args []string) config.Mode {
	if len(args) > 0 {
		return config.ModeCLI
	}
	if !term.IsTerminal(int(os.Stdin.Fd())) {
		return config.ModeCLI
	}
	return config.ModeTUI
}

func runRoot(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
	ctx := cmd.Context()

	cfg, created, err := config.Load()
	if err != nil {
		return err
	}
	if created {
		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: wrote default config to %s (run `translate init` to customize)\n", config.Path())
	}

	res := cfg.Resolve(overrides(), invocationMode(args))
	if res.Debug {
		// The one-shot CLI logs to stderr; the TUI logs to a file, since its
		// alt-screen would be corrupted by stderr writes.
		if invocationMode(args) == config.ModeCLI {
			debug.Enable(os.Stderr)
		} else if w := openDebugLog(); w != nil {
			debug.Enable(w)
		}
	}
	applyLastPair(cfg, &res) // remember_last_pair: seed source/target from state
	src, tgt := resolvePair(res.Source, res.Target)
	pairWith := ""
	if res.Pair {
		pwm, _ := lang.Resolve(res.PairWith)
		pairWith = pwm.Code
	}
	// Learn mode is bidirectional; ensure a distinct "away" (foreign) language so
	// direction routing has two sides to choose between.
	if res.Learn && (pairWith == "" || strings.EqualFold(pairWith, tgt)) {
		pairWith = defaultAway(tgt)
	}
	debug.Logf("resolved: engine=%s tier=%s preset=%s source=%s target=%s pair=%v pair_with=%s learn=%v",
		res.Engine, res.Tier, res.Preset, src, tgt, res.Pair, pairWith, res.Learn)
	if res.Pair && !res.Learn && (pairWith == "" || strings.EqualFold(pairWith, tgt)) {
		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: warning: pair mode is on but pair-with (%q) equals the target (%q) — pair mode is a no-op; set a different pair_with (run `translate init`)\n", pairWith, tgt)
	}

	if res.Provider == nil && res.Engine != "auto" {
		return fmt.Errorf("no provider configured; check %s", config.Path())
	}
	eng, err := buildEngine(res)
	if err != nil {
		return err
	}

	// The one-shot CLI path routes a learn request through a bare LLM engine
	// (bypassing smart-auto/dictionary); the TUI keeps its normal primary engine and
	// toggles learn at runtime against Params.LearnEngine.
	learnCLI := res.Learn && invocationMode(args) == config.ModeCLI
	if learnCLI && res.Provider == nil {
		return fmt.Errorf("learn mode requires an LLM provider; check %s", config.Path())
	}
	oneShotEng := eng
	if learnCLI {
		oneShotEng = learnEngineFromConfig(res)
	}

	st := openStore(cfg)
	if st != nil {
		defer st.Close()
	}

	switch {
	case len(args) > 0:
		text := strings.Join(args, " ")
		effTgt := effectiveTarget(res.Pair, tgt, pairWith, text)
		r, err := oneShot(ctx, oneShotEng, text, src, effTgt, res.Stream, res.Preset, res.Instructions, res.Pair, tgt, pairWith, res.Learn)
		if err != nil {
			return err
		}
		recordAndRemember(ctx, st, cfg, r, text, src, effTgt, tgt)
		if shouldSpeak(cfg) {
			speakResult(ctx, cfg, r, text, src, effTgt, speakForeign(cfg, pairWith))
		}
		return nil

	case !term.IsTerminal(int(os.Stdin.Fd())):
		b, err := io.ReadAll(os.Stdin)
		if err != nil {
			return err
		}
		text := strings.TrimSpace(string(b))
		if text == "" {
			return fmt.Errorf("no input on stdin")
		}
		effTgt := effectiveTarget(res.Pair, tgt, pairWith, text)
		r, err := oneShot(ctx, oneShotEng, text, src, effTgt, res.Stream, res.Preset, res.Instructions, res.Pair, tgt, pairWith, res.Learn)
		if err != nil {
			return err
		}
		recordAndRemember(ctx, st, cfg, r, text, src, effTgt, tgt)
		if shouldSpeak(cfg) {
			speakResult(ctx, cfg, r, text, src, effTgt, speakForeign(cfg, pairWith))
		}
		return nil

	default:
		return runTUI(ctx, eng, res, st, src, tgt, pairWith)
	}
}

// effectiveTarget applies pair mode: home-language input → away, else → home.
func effectiveTarget(pair bool, home, away, text string) string {
	if pair && away != "" {
		t := lang.PairTarget(home, away, text)
		debug.Logf("pair route: home=%s away=%s text=%q → target=%s", home, away, clip(text, 30), t)
		return t
	}
	return home
}

// clip shortens a string for a single-line debug label.
func clip(s string, n int) string {
	s = strings.TrimSpace(s)
	r := []rune(s)
	if len(r) > n {
		return string(r[:n]) + "…"
	}
	return s
}

// openDebugLog opens (append) the TUI debug log file, creating the state dir.
// Returns nil on failure (debug simply stays off).
func openDebugLog() io.Writer {
	if err := xdgpath.EnsureDirs(); err != nil {
		return nil
	}
	f, err := os.OpenFile(filepath.Join(xdgpath.StateDir(), "debug.log"),
		os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY|os.O_APPEND, 0o600)
	if err != nil {
		return nil
	}
	return f
}

// applyLastPair overrides the resolved source/target with the persisted last
// pair when remember_last_pair is on and the user did not pass flags/env.
func applyLastPair(cfg *config.Config, res *config.Resolved) {
	if !cfg.General.RememberLastPair {
		return
	}
	stt, err := state.Load()
	if err != nil || stt == nil {
		return
	}
	if flagFrom == "" && os.Getenv("TRANSLATE_SOURCE") == "" && stt.Source != "" {
		res.Source = stt.Source
		debug.Logf("remember_last_pair: source ← %s (from state)", stt.Source)
	}
	if flagTo == "" && os.Getenv("TRANSLATE_TARGET") == "" && stt.Target != "" {
		res.Target = stt.Target
		debug.Logf("remember_last_pair: target ← %s (from state)", stt.Target)
	}
}

// openStore opens the history store, or returns nil when history is disabled or
// suppressed with --no-history.
func openStore(cfg *config.Config) store.Store {
	if !cfg.History.Enabled || flagNoHistory {
		return nil
	}
	path := cfg.History.Path
	if path == "" {
		path = xdgpath.HistoryFile()
	}
	st, err := store.OpenJSONL(path)
	if err != nil {
		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: history disabled (%v)\n", err)
		return nil
	}
	return st
}

// recordAndRemember writes the translation to history and persists the last pair.
//
// target is the effective target actually used (for history); rememberTarget is
// the stable home target (== target unless pair mode redirected this one input),
// so persisting it keeps pair mode from drifting its home to the away language.
func recordAndRemember(ctx context.Context, st store.Store, cfg *config.Config, res *engine.TranslateResult, input, source, target, rememberTarget string) {
	if st != nil {
		_, _ = st.Add(ctx, toRecord(res, input, source, target))
	}
	if cfg.General.RememberLastPair {
		saveLastPair(source, rememberTarget, res.Engine)
	}
}

// toRecord builds a history Record from a translation result.
func toRecord(res *engine.TranslateResult, input, source, target string) store.Record {
	src := source
	if src == "auto" && res.DetectedSource != "" {
		src = res.DetectedSource
	}
	return store.Record{
		SourceLang:   src,
		TargetLang:   target,
		Engine:       res.Engine,
		Model:        res.Model,
		Input:        input,
		Output:       res.Translation,
		Alternatives: res.Alternatives,
		Notes:        res.Notes,
	}
}

// saveLastPair persists the source/target pair and source mode.
func saveLastPair(source, target, engineName string) {
	mode := "fixed"
	if source == "auto" {
		mode = "auto"
	}
	_ = state.Save(&state.State{
		Source:     source,
		Target:     target,
		SourceMode: mode,
		Engine:     engineName,
	})
}

// runTUI launches the interactive Bubble Tea front-end and persists the last
// pair on exit.
func runTUI(ctx context.Context, eng engine.Engine, res config.Resolved, st store.Store, source, target, pairWith string) error {
	// A model source for the ^p model picker: the resolved LLM provider (if any).
	var modelSrc engine.ModelLister
	modelProvider := ""
	if res.Provider != nil {
		modelSrc = llmFromProvider(res.Provider, res.Model)
		modelProvider = res.Provider.Name
	}
	// Learn mode (^n) runs against a bare LLM engine; nil when no provider is
	// configured, in which case the toggle refuses.
	var learnEng engine.Engine
	if res.Provider != nil {
		learnEng = learnEngineFromConfig(res)
	}
	p := tui.Params{
		Engines:       buildEngineSet(res, eng),
		ModelSource:   modelSrc,
		ModelProvider: modelProvider,
		Store:         st,
		Source:        source,
		Target:        target,
		Pair:          res.Pair,
		PairWith:      pairWith,
		Learn:         res.Learn,
		LearnEngine:   learnEng,
		Model:         res.Model,
		Preset:        res.Preset,
		Instructions:  res.Instructions,
		Live:          res.LiveTranslate,
		DebounceMs:    res.DebounceMs,
		Speaker:       tuiSpeaker(res.Cfg),
		Foreign:       speakForeign(res.Cfg, pairWith),
	}
	final, err := tea.NewProgram(tui.New(ctx, p), tea.WithContext(ctx)).Run()
	if err != nil {
		return err
	}
	if res.Cfg.General.RememberLastPair {
		if fm, ok := final.(tui.Model); ok {
			s, t := fm.Pair()
			saveLastPair(s, t, "")
		}
	}
	return nil
}

// resolvePair fuzzy-resolves the source and target languages, printing an
// "(interpreted X as Y)" note on stderr when a fuzzy correction was applied.
func resolvePair(rawSource, rawTarget string) (source, target string) {
	sm, _ := lang.Resolve(rawSource)
	tm, _ := lang.Resolve(rawTarget)
	if !sm.Exact && sm.Score > 0 && !strings.EqualFold(rawSource, sm.Code) {
		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: interpreted source %q as %s (%s)\n", rawSource, sm.Code, sm.Name)
	}
	if !tm.Exact && tm.Score > 0 && !strings.EqualFold(rawTarget, tm.Code) {
		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: interpreted target %q as %s (%s)\n", rawTarget, tm.Code, tm.Name)
	}
	return sm.Code, tm.Code
}

// oneShot translates text once and prints the result, returning it for history.
//
// Tokens stream live to stdout only when stdout is a TTY (so `translate x | pbcopy`
// stays clean) and --json was not requested. Piped output is the plain translation
// with no ANSI; --json emits the full structured result.
func oneShot(ctx context.Context, eng engine.Engine, text, source, target string, streamPref bool, preset, instructions string, pair bool, pairHome, pairAway string, learn bool) (*engine.TranslateResult, error) {
	stdoutTTY := term.IsTerminal(int(os.Stdout.Fd()))
	stream := streamPref && stdoutTTY && !flagJSON && !learn // learn output is structured (parsed at done)

	req := engine.Request{
		Text:     text,
		Source:   source,
		Target:   target,
		Mode:     engine.ModeTranslate,
		Stream:   stream,
		Preset:   preset,
		Extra:    instructions,
		Pair:     pair,
		PairHome: pairHome,
		PairAway: pairAway,
		Learn:    learn,
	}
	ch, err := eng.Translate(ctx, req)
	if err != nil {
		return nil, err
	}

	var onTok func(string)
	if stream {
		onTok = func(t string) { fmt.Print(t) }
	}
	res, err := engine.Drain(ch, onTok)
	if err != nil {
		if stream {
			fmt.Println() // terminate any partial line before the error surfaces
		}
		return nil, err
	}

	// Never downgrade silently: if the auto-chain fell back, say which engine
	// failed and why, and how to switch.
	for _, w := range res.Warnings {
		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: warning: %s\n", w)
	}
	if len(res.Warnings) > 0 && res.Learn == nil {
		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: used %q (switch with --engine, or check the model/provider)\n", res.Engine)
	}

	switch {
	case flagJSON:
		enc := json.NewEncoder(os.Stdout)
		enc.SetIndent("", "  ")
		if err := enc.Encode(res); err != nil {
			return nil, err
		}
	case res.Learn != nil:
		fmt.Print(renderLearnCLI(res))
	case stream:
		fmt.Println() // newline after the streamed tokens
	default:
		fmt.Println(res.Translation)
	}
	return res, nil
}

// defaultAway picks the "away" (foreign) language for learn/pair mode when none is
// configured: English speakers default to learning zh-TW, everyone else to en. It
// mirrors the TUI ^g heuristic so the CLI and TUI agree.
func defaultAway(home string) string {
	if strings.HasPrefix(strings.ToLower(home), "en") {
		return "zh-TW"
	}
	return "en"
}

// renderLearnCLI formats a learn-mode result as plain text (no ANSI, pipe-safe).
func renderLearnCLI(res *engine.TranslateResult) string {
	l := res.Learn
	if l == nil {
		return res.Translation + "\n"
	}
	var b strings.Builder
	if l.Direction == "correct" {
		corrected := strings.TrimSpace(l.Corrected)
		if corrected == "" {
			corrected = res.Translation
		}
		b.WriteString("✔ " + corrected + "\n")
		if s := strings.TrimSpace(l.Translation); s != "" {
			b.WriteString("  ↳ " + s + "\n")
		}
		for _, is := range l.Issues {
			frag := strings.TrimSpace(strings.TrimSuffix(strings.TrimPrefix(is.Span+" → "+is.Fix, " → "), " → "))
			expl := strings.TrimSpace(is.Explanation)
			line := expl
			switch {
			case frag != "" && expl != "":
				line = frag + ": " + expl
			case frag != "":
				line = frag
			}
			if line != "" {
				b.WriteString("  ✎ " + line + "\n")
			}
		}
	} else {
		tr := strings.TrimSpace(l.Translation)
		if tr == "" {
			tr = res.Translation
		}
		b.WriteString(tr + "\n")
		for _, v := range l.Vocab {
			head := "  • " + v.Term
			if v.Pos != "" {
				head += " (" + v.Pos + ")"
			}
			if v.Phonetic != "" {
				head += " " + v.Phonetic
			}
			if v.Meaning != "" {
				head += " — " + v.Meaning
			}
			b.WriteString(head + "\n")
		}
		for _, ex := range l.Examples {
			b.WriteString("  ✎ " + ex.Foreign + "\n")
			if s := strings.TrimSpace(ex.Native); s != "" {
				b.WriteString("    ↳ " + s + "\n")
			}
		}
	}
	if s := strings.TrimSpace(l.Notes); s != "" {
		b.WriteString("  ⓘ " + s + "\n")
	}
	return b.String()
}
```
</details></tool-use>

The output side is simpler: plain text when piped, and streamed tokens without ANSI formatting when writing to a TTY. _**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-20 04:21:50Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
I'm confirming that ANSI escape codes are currently being sent directly to the LLM since there's no stripping happening in the piped input flow — it just reads and trims the raw content. The good news is that `x/ansi` is already available as an indirect dependency through lipgloss and bubbletea, so I can use it to handle this.

Now I'm thinking through the core problem with bilingual line-by-line interleaving: when input contains soft-wrapped text or tables where a single logical unit spans multiple physical lines, translating each line independently loses context and produces fragmented translations. I'd need to either detect and reassemble logical lines or accept that the output quality degrades for wrapped content.

For tables specifically—like `ls -l` or `kubectl get`—interleaving a translation beneath each row destroys column alignment entirely, and translating individual cells or rows doesn't make semantic sense anyway. The only scenario where this works cleanly is line-oriented prose where each line is a complete logical unit, which is a narrow use case.

On the second question about detecting a line's dominant style for tldr output: the problem is that tldr uses per-word coloring rather than per-line, so the concept of a "dominant style" doesn't map cleanly to how the output is actually structured. pipe colored tldr through translate" scenario needs semantic filtering the tool can't do. The real issue is that piping `cmd | translate` creates a tension between what users expect from Unix filters versus what bilingual output provides. A filter should transform text cleanly and pass it along — doubling the line count with bilingual output breaks downstream piping to `less`, `grep`, or other tools, and violates the principle of least surprise. Bilingual mode is really a reading/study feature, not a filter mode, so it should be opt-in via a flag rather than the default behavior.

The codebase itself has a deliberate design principle: piped output should be plain translation with no ANSI codes. Making bilingual the default for colored pipes would contradict that existing contract. But there's a simpler solution being overlooked — the tool is currently sending ANSI-colored input directly to the LLM without stripping the escape codes first, so the real fix might just be to clean the input before processing. — but the real priority is fixing the input pollution first with a simple strip operation, which should ship regardless of whether we ever build a color-preserving display mode. The recommendation frames this as a display choice when the MVP is just stopping garbage from entering the pipeline.

Now I'm weighing whether this feature is worth building at all versus just documenting it — no user has actually requested it, so this is speculative work that might not justify the effort. Findings 2 and 3 make a strong case against per-span color preservation: DeepL duplicates tags by design, MediaWiki abandoned inline tags, XLIFF exists because reorder/drop/split are recognized hazards, and it would break token streaming. Immersive Translate—the most successful bilingual translator—uses uniform per-segment styling and block-level layout rather than per-word style transfer, which is battle-tested precedent. Uniform dimming is trivially cheap, honest, and gives a real UX win by clearly distinguishing translation from original.

Keeping the original colored line intact sidesteps the entire alignment problem—you preserve color where it already exists in the source and don't try to re-apply it. For someone studying colored CLI output in another language, bilingual-with-original-preserved is the right shape. Approach B is strongly correct and well-evidenced, though the recommendation misses that the immediate win—stripping ANSI on the input path—is a one-line fix with an existing dependency.

The bilingual "immersive" mode should be an opt-in flag, not the default for pipes, since it breaks on wrapped prose and tables. It's a distinct reading feature for line-oriented content, not the answer to piped colored input. Approach A (dominant color) doesn't work for the main use cases like `tldr` or `--help` where color is per-word, so if bilingual ships, use uniform dim instead.

The real priority is fixing the raw ANSI escape bug: when piping input, the code reads everything with `io.ReadAll`, trims whitespace, and passes it directly to the LLM — escape sequences in the middle survive and reach the model. That's the immediate win to ship, independent of any display mode decisions.

Stripping ANSI on input is safe because the strip function only removes escape sequences, not literal text, so even edge cases with bracketed content stay intact. I could gate it to only apply when escape bytes are actually detected, keeping behavior unchanged for normal input.

The real issue is that the recommendation doesn't acknowledge the input pollution at all—it only talks about display options for colored output. And looking at real-world cases like `kubectl get pods` or `git status`, line-by-line bilingual translation breaks table alignment and makes the output unreadable, which confirms this shouldn't be the default pipe behavior.

Now I'm structuring the findings into a formal schema with a summary, concrete facts, feasibility assessment, and potential risks. I'll skip the test finding and focus on the substantive ones. The core tension is that the recommendation gets the main decision right—rejecting per-span color preservation—but frames it around output display when the actual problem is on the input side.

The real issue is that ANSI codes are reaching the LLM unstripped, and the quickest fix is a single-line ANSI strip on stdin using an existing dependency. Bilingual mode should be opt-in rather than the default pipe behavior, since doubling output breaks standard Unix tools like less, grep, and wc. The stdin reader already reads and trims the input, but that only removes leading/trailing whitespace—ANSI escape sequences in the middle still get through to the LLM. The ansi.Strip function from the charmbracelet dependency is already available without adding anything new, and the oneShot function's own comment already documents that piped output should be plain text with no ANSI codes, so stripping on input aligns with the existing contract.

For the other approaches: bilingual output with dimmed translations would work for line-oriented text but breaks down when logical units span multiple lines; dominant-color handling doesn't make sense for per-word colored output like help text; per-span interleaving is overengineered; and wrapping or table-aware interleaving adds complexity without clear benefit. Stripping ANSI on input is the clear win—trivial to implement, fixes real pollution, and doesn't touch streaming. hard-wrapping heuristics can't distinguish soft from hard breaks in piped output, so reassembly fails. Structured formats like tables (kubectl, ls, docker, git) get mangled when lines are interleaved independently, destroying alignment and semantic meaning. Even prose mixed with code examples (man pages, tldr) gets mistranslated when treated line-by-line, turning command templates into gibberish.

The tool would need semantic awareness it doesn't have, and adding that feels like scope creep without actual user demand. Let me ground this in concrete evidence—I'll verify the line numbers in root.go where the oneShot comment explains the TTY behavior and check go.mod for the ansi dependency, then write up the findings.
</details></think>

Approach A (dominant color) is technically feasible but semantically empty for the flagship per-word cases (tldr/--help) — recommend dropping it. Approach B (per-span) is correctly judged over-engineered. A wrap/table-aware interleaver is NOT cheaply feasible (it re-opens the segmentation/reflow problem) — don't attempt. Net: 'don't chase B' is sound, but the shippable MVP is strip-on-input, and bilingual should be a scoped opt-in flag, not the default answer to 'piped colored input'.",
```text
      "risks": [
        "Framing gap: the recommendation only addresses output DISPLAY and misses the actual current bug — raw ANSI polluting the LLM input (wasted tokens, mis-segmentation, model echoing/commenting on escape codes). That 1-line fix is the real, immediate win and is under-prioritized.",
        "Default-mode error: bilingual as the pipe default breaks the Unix filter contract — doubles line count, breaks | less, | grep, | wc, chained pipes, and the tool's own documented plain-stdout behavior. Bilingual is a reading/study mode, not a filter mode.",

        "Code-in-prose: tldr/man pages interleave prose with command examples; a naive line interleaver translates the code examples too (git commit -m {{message}} -> nonsense), needing semantic filtering the tool cannot do.",
```

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The workflow finished and one agent returned a stub that the reviewer correctly identified as having no real signal. From the other four agents, I'm getting the key details—the ansi-api agent found that `charmbracelet/x/ansi` v0.11.7 provides `ansi.Strip` for direct stripping and `ansi.DecodeSequence` plus `NewParser` for tokenization.

Immersive Translate's philosophy is block-level styling with bilingual layout, not per-word inline color preservation—it renders translations in a single uniform theme and inherits block CSS rather than re-coloring individual words. The inline-tag-mt approach of preserving per-span tags across word reordering is a research-hard problem that defeats streaming since it requires buffering. The adversarial review surfaced that the real defect isn't about output styling but about how raw ANSI input is being handled on the input side. The LLM actually handled the ANSI codes fine and produced correct output, so this isn't a visible bug — but stripping on input is still a clean win for token efficiency and robustness against messier inputs. Let me verify whether tldr itself is even emitting the color codes in the first place.

Looking at the user's actual question though — they're asking if it's possible to preserve color in the *output*, not about input cleanliness. They want the translated text to keep the styling like the original, similar to how Immersive Translate handles bilingual + style-preserving workflows. So I should focus my answer on output preservation, with input stripping as a secondary optimization.

The core issue is straightforward: ANSI codes go into the LLM as a text blob, it returns plain text, and the color information is lost. There are a few technical approaches at different complexity levels, but the one that actually aligns with Immersive Translate's philosophy is block or line-level bilingual translation with uniform dimming for the translation — not per-word color manipulation, which would be over-engineering and fragile. The per-word approach breaks streaming and is research-hard; the line-level bilingual approach is the reasonable sweet spot if offered as an opt-in flag.

The immediate win is dead simple: strip ANSI codes on input with a single line of code, do that regardless. For the longer term, I'd recommend adding an opt-in bilingual reading mode with a flag like `--immersive` or `--bilingual` that handles line-oriented translation with uniform dimming, but keep it scoped and off by default — not the pipe default. It needs a warning about wrapped prose and table edge cases. Skip the per-word color entirely.

Now I'm mapping out the actual implementation scope: which files need to change, starting with the stdin pipe handling in the root command.

The real complexity lands in batched translation — either making N separate LLM calls (slow and expensive) or structuring one call to translate each line while preserving line breaks and returning the same number of lines. LLMs are unreliable at maintaining exact line counts for arbitrary input, so this either needs a numbered-line protocol or accepting some reflow. It's the same segmentation and alignment problem at line granularity — manageable but not trivial. For rendering, I'd keep the original line with its ANSI codes intact while also printing a stripped version.

The edge cases are where this gets thorny: blank lines, tables, wrapped lines, and code examples embedded in prose. The tldr use case specifically has command examples like `rg --hidden --no-ignore pattern` interleaved with prose, and a naive line translator would mangle those. I'd need heuristics to skip lines that look like commands or code — indented lines, symbol-heavy lines. That's real surface area for bugs. Given the value is mainly a reading mode nicety and the edge cases are substantial, this feels like something to backlog as an opt-in feature rather than ship now.

I should give the user a structured evaluation rather than jump into implementation. I'll present the three approaches in a table, give a clear recommendation, and offer to record this as a backlog item if they want. Stripping ANSI codes before sending to the LLM makes sense in all cases—it cleans up the prompt, reduces token count, and avoids the model echoing escape sequences, with no downside for translation quality. And looking at the actual code behavior: when stdout is a TTY like in the screenshot, it streams with color, but piped output gets plain text per the root.go logic, so the strip-on-input approach aligns with how the system already handles different output contexts.

For presenting this, I'll write it as a straightforward evaluation with a clear recommendation and concrete next steps in prose rather than forcing a question format—that feels more natural here.

Let me structure the final assessment: I'll lead with the verdict, explain why the color is dropping now by grounding it in the code behavior, then compare the three approaches with their complexity tradeoffs. The key insight is that Immersive Translate itself doesn't do per-word coloring—it works at the block level with unified styling—which suggests per-word coloring might be overengineering given how other translation tools handle this. I'll recommend a tiered approach: the immediate win is stripping ANSI codes on input, which is a one-liner and solves the core issue cleanly. I'll give my own complexity estimate based on reading the code directly, then write up the response.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-20 04:25:43Z)**_

The investigation is complete (one sub-agent returned a stub, but I'd read that code path myself and the adversarial reviewer caught the gap, so the picture is solid). Here's my evaluation.

## 結論先行

**保留顏色技術上完全可行**,但你真正想要的「Immersive Translate 哲學」其實**不包含逐字上色**——連 Immersive Translate 自己都不做那件事。所以答案分三層:

- ✅ **值得現在做(1 行)**:把 ANSI 從送進 LLM 的 input 裡 strip 掉。
- 🟡 **可選、算 reading-mode 加值**:opt-in 的「雙語」模式(原文保色 + 譯文統一淡色),**不能當 pipe 預設**。
- ❌ **確定 over-engineering**:逐字/逐 span 把譯文重新上色。這是研究級難題,別碰。

---

## 為什麼現在會掉色

`cmd/root.go:205` 的 pipe 路徑是:`io.ReadAll(stdin)` → `TrimSpace` → 整塊(含 ANSI escape)當成 `req.Text` 丟給 LLM → LLM 回純文字 → `fmt.Println`。

我實測確認了兩件事:
1. `tldr`/`ls --color=always`/`grep --color=always` 在 pipe 後**仍送出 ANSI**,所以 escape code(`^[[32m…^[[0m`)確實進了 stdin。
2. `TrimSpace` 只切頭尾,**中間的 SGR code 原封不動送進 prompt**。LLM 幫你「腦內」濾掉了(所以截圖裡譯文乾淨),但這是浪費 token + 對雜亂輸入有 mis-segmentation 風險。

`cmd/root.go:411-413` 還有一句既有設計註解:「Piped output is the plain translation with no ANSI」——這是你刻意定的 Unix filter 契約,下面會用到。

---

## 三種做法對照

| | 做法 | 保留程度 | 複雜度 | 評價 |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **A** | 偵測每行「主色」,譯文套該色 | 行級單色 | M | ❌ **丟棄**。tldr/`--help` 的顏色是**逐字**的(指令 vs placeholder),「最常出現的 SGR」是個沒有語意的隨機色。 |
| **B** | 解析成 styled runs → placeholder token → 要 LLM 帶著 token 翻 → 還原 | 逐字 | **L** | ❌ **over-engineering**。見下。 |
| **C** | 原文整行保留原色,譯文印在下方(統一淡色) | 行/區塊級 + 雙語 | S–M | 🟡 **唯一合理的「雙語」選項**,但要 opt-in。 |
| **strip** | 送 LLM 前 `ansi.Strip`,輸出照舊 | 不保色 | XS | ✅ **無腦該做**,與上面正交。 |

好消息:`github.com/charmbracelet/x/ansi`(已在 `go.mod`,只是 indirect)全部 primitives 都現成——`ansi.Strip`(strip)、`ansi.DecodeSequence`+`NewParser`(tokenize 成 styled runs,約 15 行迴圈)、`ansi.StringWidth`/`Truncate`(CJK-aware)。所以工具鏈不是障礙。

---

## 關鍵洞察:你引用的哲學 ≠ 逐字上色

我特地去查了 Immersive Translate 實際怎麼做:它 walk DOM,把譯文塞進**同一個 block 元素**(p/h1-h6/li),靠 **CSS 繼承**讓「header 譯文還是 header 大小」。但譯文本身是**單一統一主題**(灰淡/底線/highlight),它**從不**把原文裡某幾個粗體/有色的字在譯文裡重新上色——原文的逐字樣式只保留在「被留下的原文那份」。

換句話說:「header 保持 header 樣式」是**區塊級**保留;「`grep` 這個紅字譯文也是紅的」**根本不在它的哲學裡**。你要對齊的是 C(區塊級 + 雙語 + 統一淡色),不是 B。

---

## 為什麼 B(逐字上色)是 over-engineering

這在在地化界是個有名的老難題,而且結論一致——**不能信任翻譯引擎自己去搬 tag**:

- **XLIFF 2.0** 專門加了 `canReorder`/`canCopy`/`canDelete` 屬性,就是因為 reorder/複製/刪除 inline code 是公認的災難模式。
- **DeepL** 的 `tag_handling` 在語序改變時會**按設計複製 tag**;**Google** format=HTML 官方用語是「returns tags…**to the extent possible**」(有 hedge,實測有掉 `notranslate`)。
- **維基百科的 Content Translation 直接放棄**把 inline tag 交給 MT:改成 strip 成純文字翻譯,再用 subsentence alignment + n-gram 模糊比對「貼回去」,還是會在屈折/語序/斷詞上失敗。

套到你這:一個有色的來源字,翻成中文可能變多字、變零字(被吸收進其他詞),色該包誰無解。而且 B 需要**buffer 整段輸出再後處理**,直接打爛你 `prompt.go` + `engine.go` 的 token-by-token streaming 熱路徑。效益(終端機裝飾)對成本(迷你 XLIFF 引擎 + 殺 streaming)嚴重不成比例。

---

## 我的建議

**1. 現在就做(與顯示無關的乾淨勝利):** 在 pipe 分支 `TrimSpace` 之後、送進 `req.Text` 之前加一次 `ansi.Strip`。1 行、零新依賴、不動 streaming、省 token、對雜亂輸入更穩。輸出行為完全不變,不破壞任何契約。

**2. 若真的想要「雙語沉浸」→ 做成 opt-in flag(如 `--bilingual` / `--immersive`),用 C:** 原文整行保留原色,下方印 `ansi.Strip` 後的譯文(lipgloss faint 統一淡色)。但必須:
- **不能當 pipe 預設**——會把行數變兩倍,打爛 `| less`、`| grep`、`| wc`、後續 pipe,以及你自己 `root.go:411` 的 plain-stdout 契約。它是「閱讀模式」,不是 filter 模式。
- 誠實標注限制:對**軟換行的段落**、**表格**(`ls -l`、`kubectl get`、`git status`)、**prose 裡夾的指令範例行**(tldr 的 `rg pattern` 那種——逐行翻會把範例也翻成亂碼),效果都會壞。要做好得再加「這行像不像 code/該不該翻」的啟發式,這就是它從 S 漂到 M 的原因。

**3. 別做:** B(逐字上色)和 A(主色)。

---

要我直接把 **#1 的 strip-on-input** 補上(順手,獨立於任何顯示改動),還是先把 **#2 的雙語 opt-in 模式**照你 repo 的慣例記進 `TODO.md` / `backlog/`(標 `[?/M]`,附這份評估)?或兩者都做。

---

```text
1	// Package cmd wires the cobra command tree. The root command dispatches between
2	// the one-shot CLI (arguments or piped stdin) and the interactive TUI (a TTY
3	// with no arguments); every path drives the shared engine layer.
4	package cmd
5	
6	import (
7		"context"
8		"encoding/json"
9		"fmt"
10		"io"
11		"os"
12		"path/filepath"
13		"strings"
14	
15		"github.com/spf13/cobra"
16		"golang.org/x/term"
17	
18		tea "charm.land/bubbletea/v2"
19	
20		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/config"
21		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/debug"
22		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/engine"
23		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/lang"
24		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/state"
25		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/store"
26		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/tui"
27		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/xdgpath"
28	)
29	
30	var (
31		flagTo           string
32		flagFrom         string
33		flagModel        string
34		flagProvider     string
35		flagEngine       string
36		flagTier         string
37		flagPreset       string
38		flagInstructions string
39		flagPair         bool
40		flagPairWith     string
41		flagLearn        bool
42		flagJSON         bool
43		flagNoHistory    bool
44		flagDebug        bool
45		flagSpeak        bool
46		flagSpeakLang    string
47	)
48	
49	// NewRootCmd builds the root command and its subcommands.
50	func NewRootCmd() *cobra.Command {
51		root := &cobra.Command{
52			Use:   "translate [text...]",
53			Short: "Fast terminal translation (CLI + TUI)",
54			Long: "translate is a fast CLI/TUI translator.\n\n" +
55				"  translate \"hola mundo\" --to en    one-shot\n" +
56				"  echo hola | translate --to en       pipe\n" +
57				"  translate                           interactive TUI",
58			Version:       buildVersion(),
59			SilenceUsage:  true,
60			SilenceErrors: true,
61			// ArbitraryArgs lets bare text fall through to translation while exact
62			// subcommands (config, lang, …) still route. Without it, cobra rejects
63			// `translate "hola"` as an unknown command once subcommands exist.
64			Args: cobra.ArbitraryArgs,
65			RunE: runRoot,
66		}
67		f := root.PersistentFlags()
68		f.StringVarP(&flagTo, "to", "t", "", "target language (fuzzy; e.g. en, chinese)")
69		f.StringVarP(&flagFrom, "from", "f", "", "source language (\"auto\" to detect)")
70		f.StringVar(&flagModel, "model", "", "LLM model id override")
71		f.StringVar(&flagProvider, "provider", "", "provider name (e.g. copilot, ollama)")
72		f.StringVar(&flagEngine, "engine", "", "engine: auto|<provider>|google|dict")
73		f.StringVar(&flagTier, "tier", "", "model tier: default|fast|max")
74		f.StringVar(&flagPreset, "preset", "", "LLM prompt style: concise|contextual|dictionary")
75		f.StringVar(&flagInstructions, "instructions", "", "extra system-prompt guidance (domain focus, etc.)")
76		f.BoolVar(&flagPair, "pair", false, "bidirectional mode: home-language input → --pair-with, else → --to")
77		f.StringVar(&flagPairWith, "pair-with", "", "the other language for --pair (e.g. en)")
78		f.BoolVar(&flagLearn, "learn", false, "learning mode: teach (native→foreign) or grammar-correct (foreign→native)")
79		f.BoolVar(&flagJSON, "json", false, "emit the full result as JSON")
80		f.BoolVar(&flagNoHistory, "no-history", false, "do not record this translation in history")
81		f.BoolVar(&flagDebug, "debug", false, "log intermediate decisions (routing, engine choice, dict hit/miss)")
82		f.BoolVarP(&flagSpeak, "speak", "s", false, "speak the foreign side of the result aloud (free TTS)")
83		f.StringVar(&flagSpeakLang, "speak-lang", "", "force the spoken language (e.g. en, zh-TW)")
84	
85		root.SuggestionsMinimumDistance = 2
86		root.AddCommand(newConfigCmd(), newLangCmd(), newDefineCmd(), newHistoryCmd(), newInitCmd(), newDictCmd(), newSpeakCmd())
87		return root
88	}
89	
90	// Execute runs the root command with the given (cancellable) context.
91	func Execute(ctx context.Context) error {
92		return NewRootCmd().ExecuteContext(ctx)
93	}
94	
95	// overrides collects the explicitly-set flag values.
96	func overrides() config.Overrides {
97		return config.Overrides{
98			Source:       flagFrom,
99			Target:       flagTo,
100			Engine:       flagEngine,
101			Provider:     flagProvider,
102			Model:        flagModel,
103			Tier:         flagTier,
104			Preset:       flagPreset,
105			Instructions: flagInstructions,
106			Pair:         flagPair,
107			PairWith:     flagPairWith,
108			Learn:        flagLearn,
109			Debug:        flagDebug,
110		}
111	}
112	
113	// invocationMode reports whether this run is the one-shot CLI (arguments or piped
114	// stdin) or the interactive TUI (a TTY with no arguments). It mirrors the dispatch
115	// switch in runRoot and is computed before Resolve so the [cli]/[tui] overlay applies.
116	func invocationMode(args []string) config.Mode {
117		if len(args) > 0 {
118			return config.ModeCLI
119		}
120		if !term.IsTerminal(int(os.Stdin.Fd())) {
121			return config.ModeCLI
122		}
123		return config.ModeTUI
124	}
125	
126	func runRoot(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
127		ctx := cmd.Context()
128	
129		cfg, created, err := config.Load()
130		if err != nil {
131			return err
132		}
133		if created {
134			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: wrote default config to %s (run `translate init` to customize)\n", config.Path())
135		}
136	
137		res := cfg.Resolve(overrides(), invocationMode(args))
138		if res.Debug {
139			// The one-shot CLI logs to stderr; the TUI logs to a file, since its
140			// alt-screen would be corrupted by stderr writes.
141			if invocationMode(args) == config.ModeCLI {
142				debug.Enable(os.Stderr)
143			} else if w := openDebugLog(); w != nil {
144				debug.Enable(w)
145			}
146		}
147		applyLastPair(cfg, &res) // remember_last_pair: seed source/target from state
148		src, tgt := resolvePair(res.Source, res.Target)
149		pairWith := ""
150		if res.Pair {
151			pwm, _ := lang.Resolve(res.PairWith)
152			pairWith = pwm.Code
153		}
154		// Learn mode is bidirectional; ensure a distinct "away" (foreign) language so
155		// direction routing has two sides to choose between.
156		if res.Learn && (pairWith == "" || strings.EqualFold(pairWith, tgt)) {
157			pairWith = defaultAway(tgt)
158		}
159		debug.Logf("resolved: engine=%s tier=%s preset=%s source=%s target=%s pair=%v pair_with=%s learn=%v",
160			res.Engine, res.Tier, res.Preset, src, tgt, res.Pair, pairWith, res.Learn)
161		if res.Pair && !res.Learn && (pairWith == "" || strings.EqualFold(pairWith, tgt)) {
162			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: warning: pair mode is on but pair-with (%q) equals the target (%q) — pair mode is a no-op; set a different pair_with (run `translate init`)\n", pairWith, tgt)
163		}
164	
165		if res.Provider == nil && res.Engine != "auto" {
166			return fmt.Errorf("no provider configured; check %s", config.Path())
167		}
168		eng, err := buildEngine(res)
169		if err != nil {
170			return err
171		}
172	
173		// The one-shot CLI path routes a learn request through a bare LLM engine
174		// (bypassing smart-auto/dictionary); the TUI keeps its normal primary engine and
175		// toggles learn at runtime against Params.LearnEngine.
176		learnCLI := res.Learn && invocationMode(args) == config.ModeCLI
177		if learnCLI && res.Provider == nil {
178			return fmt.Errorf("learn mode requires an LLM provider; check %s", config.Path())
179		}
180		oneShotEng := eng
181		if learnCLI {
182			oneShotEng = learnEngineFromConfig(res)
183		}
184	
185		st := openStore(cfg)
186		if st != nil {
187			defer st.Close()
188		}
189	
190		switch {
191		case len(args) > 0:
192			text := strings.Join(args, " ")
193			effTgt := effectiveTarget(res.Pair, tgt, pairWith, text)
194			r, err := oneShot(ctx, oneShotEng, text, src, effTgt, res.Stream, res.Preset, res.Instructions, res.Pair, tgt, pairWith, res.Learn)
195			if err != nil {
196				return err
197			}
198			recordAndRemember(ctx, st, cfg, r, text, src, effTgt, tgt)
199			if shouldSpeak(cfg) {
200				speakResult(ctx, cfg, r, text, src, effTgt, speakForeign(cfg, pairWith))
201			}
202			return nil
203	
204		case !term.IsTerminal(int(os.Stdin.Fd())):
205			b, err := io.ReadAll(os.Stdin)
206			if err != nil {
207				return err
208			}
209			text := strings.TrimSpace(string(b))
210			if text == "" {
211				return fmt.Errorf("no input on stdin")
212			}
213			effTgt := effectiveTarget(res.Pair, tgt, pairWith, text)
214			r, err := oneShot(ctx, oneShotEng, text, src, effTgt, res.Stream, res.Preset, res.Instructions, res.Pair, tgt, pairWith, res.Learn)
215			if err != nil {
216				return err
217			}
218			recordAndRemember(ctx, st, cfg, r, text, src, effTgt, tgt)
219			if shouldSpeak(cfg) {
220				speakResult(ctx, cfg, r, text, src, effTgt, speakForeign(cfg, pairWith))
221			}
222			return nil
223	
224		default:
225			return runTUI(ctx, eng, res, st, src, tgt, pairWith)
226		}
227	}
228	
229	// effectiveTarget applies pair mode: home-language input → away, else → home.
230	func effectiveTarget(pair bool, home, away, text string) string {
231		if pair && away != "" {
232			t := lang.PairTarget(home, away, text)
233			debug.Logf("pair route: home=%s away=%s text=%q → target=%s", home, away, clip(text, 30), t)
234			return t
235		}
236		return home
237	}
238	
239	// clip shortens a string for a single-line debug label.
240	func clip(s string, n int) string {
241		s = strings.TrimSpace(s)
242		r := []rune(s)
243		if len(r) > n {
244			return string(r[:n]) + "…"
245		}
246		return s
247	}
248	
249	// openDebugLog opens (append) the TUI debug log file, creating the state dir.
250	// Returns nil on failure (debug simply stays off).
251	func openDebugLog() io.Writer {
252		if err := xdgpath.EnsureDirs(); err != nil {
253			return nil
254		}
255		f, err := os.OpenFile(filepath.Join(xdgpath.StateDir(), "debug.log"),
256			os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY|os.O_APPEND, 0o600)
257		if err != nil {
258			return nil
259		}
260		return f
261	}
262	
263	// applyLastPair overrides the resolved source/target with the persisted last
264	// pair when remember_last_pair is on and the user did not pass flags/env.
265	func applyLastPair(cfg *config.Config, res *config.Resolved) {
266		if !cfg.General.RememberLastPair {
267			return
268		}
269		stt, err := state.Load()
270		if err != nil || stt == nil {
271			return
272		}
273		if flagFrom == "" && os.Getenv("TRANSLATE_SOURCE") == "" && stt.Source != "" {
274			res.Source = stt.Source
275			debug.Logf("remember_last_pair: source ← %s (from state)", stt.Source)
276		}
277		if flagTo == "" && os.Getenv("TRANSLATE_TARGET") == "" && stt.Target != "" {
278			res.Target = stt.Target
279			debug.Logf("remember_last_pair: target ← %s (from state)", stt.Target)
280		}
281	}
282	
283	// openStore opens the history store, or returns nil when history is disabled or
284	// suppressed with --no-history.
285	func openStore(cfg *config.Config) store.Store {
286		if !cfg.History.Enabled || flagNoHistory {
287			return nil
288		}
289		path := cfg.History.Path
290		if path == "" {
291			path = xdgpath.HistoryFile()
292		}
293		st, err := store.OpenJSONL(path)
294		if err != nil {
295			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: history disabled (%v)\n", err)
296			return nil
297		}
298		return st
299	}
300	
301	// recordAndRemember writes the translation to history and persists the last pair.
302	//
303	// target is the effective target actually used (for history); rememberTarget is
304	// the stable home target (== target unless pair mode redirected this one input),
305	// so persisting it keeps pair mode from drifting its home to the away language.
306	func recordAndRemember(ctx context.Context, st store.Store, cfg *config.Config, res *engine.TranslateResult, input, source, target, rememberTarget string) {
307		if st != nil {
308			_, _ = st.Add(ctx, toRecord(res, input, source, target))
309		}
310		if cfg.General.RememberLastPair {
311			saveLastPair(source, rememberTarget, res.Engine)
312		}
313	}
314	
315	// toRecord builds a history Record from a translation result.
316	func toRecord(res *engine.TranslateResult, input, source, target string) store.Record {
317		src := source
318		if src == "auto" && res.DetectedSource != "" {
319			src = res.DetectedSource
320		}
321		return store.Record{
322			SourceLang:   src,
323			TargetLang:   target,
324			Engine:       res.Engine,
325			Model:        res.Model,
326			Input:        input,
327			Output:       res.Translation,
328			Alternatives: res.Alternatives,
329			Notes:        res.Notes,
330		}
331	}
332	
333	// saveLastPair persists the source/target pair and source mode.
334	func saveLastPair(source, target, engineName string) {
335		mode := "fixed"
336		if source == "auto" {
337			mode = "auto"
338		}
339		_ = state.Save(&state.State{
340			Source:     source,
341			Target:     target,
342			SourceMode: mode,
343			Engine:     engineName,
344		})
345	}
346	
347	// runTUI launches the interactive Bubble Tea front-end and persists the last
348	// pair on exit.
349	func runTUI(ctx context.Context, eng engine.Engine, res config.Resolved, st store.Store, source, target, pairWith string) error {
350		// A model source for the ^p model picker: the resolved LLM provider (if any).
351		var modelSrc engine.ModelLister
352		modelProvider := ""
353		if res.Provider != nil {
354			modelSrc = llmFromProvider(res.Provider, res.Model)
355			modelProvider = res.Provider.Name
356		}
357		// Learn mode (^n) runs against a bare LLM engine; nil when no provider is
358		// configured, in which case the toggle refuses.
359		var learnEng engine.Engine
360		if res.Provider != nil {
361			learnEng = learnEngineFromConfig(res)
362		}
363		p := tui.Params{
364			Engines:       buildEngineSet(res, eng),
365			ModelSource:   modelSrc,
366			ModelProvider: modelProvider,
367			Store:         st,
368			Source:        source,
369			Target:        target,
370			Pair:          res.Pair,
371			PairWith:      pairWith,
372			Learn:         res.Learn,
373			LearnEngine:   learnEng,
374			Model:         res.Model,
375			Preset:        res.Preset,
376			Instructions:  res.Instructions,
377			Live:          res.LiveTranslate,
378			DebounceMs:    res.DebounceMs,
379			Speaker:       tuiSpeaker(res.Cfg),
380			Foreign:       speakForeign(res.Cfg, pairWith),
381		}
382		final, err := tea.NewProgram(tui.New(ctx, p), tea.WithContext(ctx)).Run()
383		if err != nil {
384			return err
385		}
386		if res.Cfg.General.RememberLastPair {
387			if fm, ok := final.(tui.Model); ok {
388				s, t := fm.Pair()
389				saveLastPair(s, t, "")
390			}
391		}
392		return nil
393	}
394	
395	// resolvePair fuzzy-resolves the source and target languages, printing an
396	// "(interpreted X as Y)" note on stderr when a fuzzy correction was applied.
397	func resolvePair(rawSource, rawTarget string) (source, target string) {
398		sm, _ := lang.Resolve(rawSource)
399		tm, _ := lang.Resolve(rawTarget)
400		if !sm.Exact && sm.Score > 0 && !strings.EqualFold(rawSource, sm.Code) {
401			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: interpreted source %q as %s (%s)\n", rawSource, sm.Code, sm.Name)
402		}
403		if !tm.Exact && tm.Score > 0 && !strings.EqualFold(rawTarget, tm.Code) {
404			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: interpreted target %q as %s (%s)\n", rawTarget, tm.Code, tm.Name)
405		}
406		return sm.Code, tm.Code
407	}
408	
409	// oneShot translates text once and prints the result, returning it for history.
410	//
411	// Tokens stream live to stdout only when stdout is a TTY (so `translate x | pbcopy`
412	// stays clean) and --json was not requested. Piped output is the plain translation
413	// with no ANSI; --json emits the full structured result.
414	func oneShot(ctx context.Context, eng engine.Engine, text, source, target string, streamPref bool, preset, instructions string, pair bool, pairHome, pairAway string, learn bool) (*engine.TranslateResult, error) {
415		stdoutTTY := term.IsTerminal(int(os.Stdout.Fd()))
416		stream := streamPref && stdoutTTY && !flagJSON && !learn // learn output is structured (parsed at done)
417	
418		req := engine.Request{
419			Text:     text,
420			Source:   source,
421			Target:   target,
422			Mode:     engine.ModeTranslate,
423			Stream:   stream,
424			Preset:   preset,
425			Extra:    instructions,
426			Pair:     pair,
427			PairHome: pairHome,
428			PairAway: pairAway,
429			Learn:    learn,
430		}
431		ch, err := eng.Translate(ctx, req)
432		if err != nil {
433			return nil, err
434		}
435	
436		var onTok func(string)
437		if stream {
438			onTok = func(t string) { fmt.Print(t) }
439		}
440		res, err := engine.Drain(ch, onTok)
441		if err != nil {
442			if stream {
443				fmt.Println() // terminate any partial line before the error surfaces
444			}
445			return nil, err
446		}
447	
448		// Never downgrade silently: if the auto-chain fell back, say which engine
449		// failed and why, and how to switch.
450		for _, w := range res.Warnings {
451			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: warning: %s\n", w)
452		}
453		if len(res.Warnings) > 0 && res.Learn == nil {
454			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: used %q (switch with --engine, or check the model/provider)\n", res.Engine)
455		}
456	
457		switch {
458		case flagJSON:
459			enc := json.NewEncoder(os.Stdout)
460			enc.SetIndent("", "  ")
461			if err := enc.Encode(res); err != nil {
462				return nil, err
463			}
464		case res.Learn != nil:
465			fmt.Print(renderLearnCLI(res))
466		case stream:
467			fmt.Println() // newline after the streamed tokens
468		default:
469			fmt.Println(res.Translation)
470		}
471		return res, nil
472	}
473	
474	// defaultAway picks the "away" (foreign) language for learn/pair mode when none is
475	// configured: English speakers default to learning zh-TW, everyone else to en. It
476	// mirrors the TUI ^g heuristic so the CLI and TUI agree.
477	func defaultAway(home string) string {
478		if strings.HasPrefix(strings.ToLower(home), "en") {
479			return "zh-TW"
480		}
481		return "en"
482	}
483	
484	// renderLearnCLI formats a learn-mode result as plain text (no ANSI, pipe-safe).
485	func renderLearnCLI(res *engine.TranslateResult) string {
486		l := res.Learn
487		if l == nil {
488			return res.Translation + "\n"
489		}
490		var b strings.Builder
491		if l.Direction == "correct" {
492			corrected := strings.TrimSpace(l.Corrected)
493			if corrected == "" {
494				corrected = res.Translation
495			}
496			b.WriteString("✔ " + corrected + "\n")
497			if s := strings.TrimSpace(l.Translation); s != "" {
498				b.WriteString("  ↳ " + s + "\n")
499			}
500			for _, is := range l.Issues {
501				frag := strings.TrimSpace(strings.TrimSuffix(strings.TrimPrefix(is.Span+" → "+is.Fix, " → "), " → "))
502				expl := strings.TrimSpace(is.Explanation)
503				line := expl
504				switch {
505				case frag != "" && expl != "":
506					line = frag + ": " + expl
507				case frag != "":
508					line = frag
509				}
510				if line != "" {
511					b.WriteString("  ✎ " + line + "\n")
512				}
513			}
514		} else {
515			tr := strings.TrimSpace(l.Translation)
516			if tr == "" {
517				tr = res.Translation
518			}
519			b.WriteString(tr + "\n")
520			for _, v := range l.Vocab {
521				head := "  • " + v.Term
522				if v.Pos != "" {
523					head += " (" + v.Pos + ")"
524				}
525				if v.Phonetic != "" {
526					head += " " + v.Phonetic
527				}
528				if v.Meaning != "" {
529					head += " — " + v.Meaning
530				}
531				b.WriteString(head + "\n")
532			}
533			for _, ex := range l.Examples {
534				b.WriteString("  ✎ " + ex.Foreign + "\n")
535				if s := strings.TrimSpace(ex.Native); s != "" {
536					b.WriteString("    ↳ " + s + "\n")
537				}
538			}
539		}
540		if s := strings.TrimSpace(l.Notes); s != "" {
541			b.WriteString("  ⓘ " + s + "\n")
542		}
543		return b.String()
544	}
545
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	package tui
2	
3	import lg "charm.land/lipgloss/v2"
4	
5	// palette (fixed for now; light/dark adaptivity is a later slice).
6	var (
7		colAccent = lg.Color("#7D56F4")
8		colDim    = lg.Color("#6C6C6C")
9		colErr    = lg.Color("#FF5F87")
10		colWarn   = lg.Color("#E0A500")
11		colOK     = lg.Color("#5FD787")
12		colText   = lg.Color("#D0D0D0")
13	)
14	
15	type styles struct {
16		input    lg.Style
17		inputHi  lg.Style
18		result   lg.Style
19		resultHi lg.Style
20		footer   lg.Style
21		label    lg.Style
22		trans    lg.Style
23		alt      lg.Style
24		notes    lg.Style
25		warn     lg.Style
26		errText  lg.Style
27		dim      lg.Style
28		liveOn   lg.Style
29		liveOff  lg.Style
30	}
31	
32	func newStyles() styles {
33		box := lg.NewStyle().Border(lg.RoundedBorder()).Padding(0, 1)
34		return styles{
35			input:    box.BorderForeground(colDim),
36			inputHi:  box.BorderForeground(colAccent),
37			result:   box.BorderForeground(colDim),
38			resultHi: box.BorderForeground(colAccent),
39			footer:   lg.NewStyle().Foreground(colDim),
40			label:    lg.NewStyle().Foreground(colAccent).Bold(true),
41			trans:    lg.NewStyle().Foreground(colText).Bold(true),
42			alt:      lg.NewStyle().Foreground(colDim),
43			notes:    lg.NewStyle().Foreground(colDim).Italic(true),
44			warn:     lg.NewStyle().Foreground(colWarn),
45			errText:  lg.NewStyle().Foreground(colErr),
46			dim:      lg.NewStyle().Foreground(colDim),
47			liveOn:   lg.NewStyle().Foreground(colOK),
48			liveOff:  lg.NewStyle().Foreground(colDim),
49		}
50	}
```

```text
1	// Package engine defines the translation backend abstraction and its
2	// implementations (LLM via OpenAI-compatible APIs, free web APIs, dictionaries).
3	//
4	// The engine layer is pure Go with context.Context and imports zero TUI code:
5	// the one-shot CLI and the Bubble Tea TUI drive the identical Engine values and
6	// diverge only at presentation.
7	package engine
8	
9	import "context"
10	
11	// Mode selects what kind of lookup a Request performs.
12	type Mode int
13	
14	const (
15		// ModeTranslate is register-aware, faithful translation (the default).
16		ModeTranslate Mode = iota
17		// ModeDict is a dictionary/definition lookup.
18		ModeDict
19	)
20	
21	// Request is the single input to every engine. A Source of "" or "auto" asks
22	// the engine to detect the source language.
23	type Request struct {
24		Text    string
25		Source  string // BCP-47-ish code or "auto"/""
26		Target  string // e.g. "en", "zh"
27		Mode    Mode
28		MaxAlts int    // cap on Alternatives; 0 => engine default
29		Stream  bool   // caller wants token streaming (LLM only; others ignore)
30		Model   string // optional per-request model override (LLM engines)
31		// ModelProvider scopes Model to one provider by name; other engines ignore
32		// the override (so a copilot model id never leaks to an Ollama fallback).
33		ModelProvider string
34		Preset        string // LLM prompt style: "" => concise (LLM translate only)
35		Extra         string // extra user instructions appended to the system prompt
36	
37		// Pair marks a bidirectional "pair" request: the text is written in one of
38		// two languages (PairHome/PairAway) and must be translated into the OTHER.
39		// The LLM translate prompt uses this to detect-and-route and, critically, to
40		// never echo the input unchanged. Target still carries the caller's routed
41		// best guess (for history/display and non-LLM engines).
42		Pair     bool
43		PairHome string // one pair language (e.g. the home target, "zh-TW")
44		PairAway string // the other pair language (e.g. "en")
45	
46		// Learn marks a language-learning request. It is bidirectional (implies Pair,
47		// with PairHome=native and PairAway=foreign) and adaptive: native-language input
48		// is translated-and-taught (glosses + examples), foreign-language input is
49		// grammar-corrected-and-explained. The LLM engine answers with a strict JSON
50		// object (parsed into TranslateResult.Learn) and, because the output is
51		// structured, always runs NON-STREAMING regardless of Stream.
52		Learn bool
53	}
54	
55	// TranslateResult is the "Marvin-lite" typed result. Every engine fills what it
56	// can; zero-value fields are simply absent. It is also the history record shape.
57	type TranslateResult struct {
58		Translation    string   `json:"translation"`
59		DetectedSource string   `json:"detected_source,omitempty"`
60		Target         string   `json:"target"`
61		Alternatives   []string `json:"alternatives,omitempty"`
62		Notes          string   `json:"notes,omitempty"`
63		Confidence     float64  `json:"confidence,omitempty"`
64	
65		// Warnings records non-fatal problems, e.g. a chain engine that failed before
66		// this (fallback) engine served the result — so a downgrade is never silent.
67		Warnings []string `json:"warnings,omitempty"`
68	
69		// Truncated is set when a streamed result ended before the model finished
70		// (no terminal marker, or stop_reason=="max_tokens"). The partial text is
71		// still returned so the caller can show it, but it must not be cached or
72		// persisted as if complete. Transient: not serialized.
73		Truncated bool `json:"-"`
74	
75		// Provenance — stamped by the producing engine / chain.
76		Engine string `json:"engine,omitempty"`
77		Model  string `json:"model,omitempty"`
78	
79		// Dictionary payload (Mode == ModeDict); nil for translate mode.
80		Dictionary *DictEntry `json:"dictionary,omitempty"`
81		// Learn payload (Request.Learn); nil outside learn mode. Translation still holds
82		// the main foreign-language sentence (the corrected sentence, or the translation)
83		// so copy/history/speak keep working unchanged; the structured extras ride here.
84		Learn *LearnResult `json:"learn,omitempty"`
85		// Suggestions is a ranked "did you mean" list, set by the dictionary engine
86		// when no exact entry was found (Dictionary and Translation stay empty). The
87		// frontend decides how to present/select — the engine no longer auto-picks.
88		Suggestions []string `json:"suggestions,omitempty"`
89	
90		// SuggestDistance is the edit distance of the best entry in Suggestions (English
91		// fuzzy lookups only; 0 when unknown/not applicable, e.g. Chinese prefix matches).
92		// The smart-dict engine reads it to tell a likely typo from a match too far off.
93		// Transient: not serialized.
94		SuggestDistance int `json:"-"`
95	}
96	
97	// DictEntry is a dictionary lookup payload (populated by the dictionary engine).
98	type DictEntry struct {
99		Word      string    `json:"word"`
100		Phonetic  string    `json:"phonetic,omitempty"`
101		Meanings  []Meaning `json:"meanings,omitempty"`
102		SourceURL string    `json:"source_url,omitempty"`
103	}
104	
105	// Meaning groups definitions by part of speech.
106	type Meaning struct {
107		PartOfSpeech string       `json:"part_of_speech"`
108		Definitions  []Definition `json:"definitions"`
109		Synonyms     []string     `json:"synonyms,omitempty"`
110		Antonyms     []string     `json:"antonyms,omitempty"`
111	}
112	
113	// Definition is a single sense of a word.
114	type Definition struct {
115		Text    string `json:"definition"`
116		Example string `json:"example,omitempty"`
117	}
118	
119	// LearnResult is the structured payload for learn/tutor mode (Request.Learn). The
120	// model fills only the fields relevant to the auto-detected direction; all glosses,
121	// explanations, and notes are written in the native (home) language.
122	type LearnResult struct {
123		Direction   string         `json:"direction"`           // "teach" (native→foreign) | "correct" (foreign→native)
124		Original    string         `json:"original"`            // the user's input as received
125		Corrected   string         `json:"corrected,omitempty"` // correct-direction: the fixed foreign sentence
126		Translation string         `json:"translation"`         // teach: foreign translation; correct: native translation of intent
127		Notes       string         `json:"notes,omitempty"`     // short tip/encouragement, in the NATIVE language
128		Issues      []LearnIssue   `json:"issues,omitempty"`    // correct-direction grammar/usage fixes
129		Vocab       []LearnGloss   `json:"vocab,omitempty"`     // teach-direction glosses
130		Examples    []LearnExample `json:"examples,omitempty"`  // teach-direction usage examples
131	}
132	
133	// LearnIssue is one grammar/usage correction (correct-direction).
134	type LearnIssue struct {
135		Span        string `json:"span"`        // the problematic fragment of the input
136		Fix         string `json:"fix"`         // the corrected fragment
137		Explanation string `json:"explanation"` // why — in the NATIVE language
138	}
139	
140	// LearnGloss is one vocabulary explanation (teach-direction).
141	type LearnGloss struct {
142		Term     string `json:"term"`               // foreign word/term
143		Pos      string `json:"pos,omitempty"`      // part of speech
144		Phonetic string `json:"phonetic,omitempty"` // KK/IPA for English, pinyin for Chinese
145		Meaning  string `json:"meaning"`            // meaning in the NATIVE language
146	}
147	
148	// LearnExample is one usage example (teach-direction).
149	type LearnExample struct {
150		Foreign string `json:"foreign"` // example sentence in the foreign language
151		Native  string `json:"native"`  // its native-language translation
152	}
153	
154	// ChunkKind distinguishes streamed tokens from the terminal result/error.
155	type ChunkKind int
156	
157	const (
158		// ChunkToken is one streamed unit of translation text.
159		ChunkToken ChunkKind = iota
160		// ChunkDone carries the final structured result and ends the stream.
161		ChunkDone
162		// ChunkError carries a terminal error and ends the stream.
163		ChunkError
164	)
165	
166	// Chunk is one unit emitted on an engine's result channel.
167	type Chunk struct {
168		Kind   ChunkKind
169		Text   string           // token text (ChunkToken)
170		Result *TranslateResult // final result (ChunkDone)
171		Err    error            // terminal error (ChunkError)
172	}
173	
174	// Engine is the provider abstraction. Every method honors ctx cancellation.
175	//
176	// Translate returns a receive-only channel and MUST close it after sending
177	// exactly one terminal Chunk (ChunkDone or ChunkError). Streaming engines emit
178	// zero or more ChunkToken then one ChunkDone; non-streaming engines emit a
179	// single ChunkDone. This uniform "channel closed == finished" contract lets the
180	// TUI's self-resubscribing reader and the CLI's drain loop share one code path.
181	// The synchronous error return is only for immediate setup failures; all
182	// runtime/network errors flow as a terminal ChunkError.
183	type Engine interface {
184		Name() string
185		Translate(ctx context.Context, req Request) (<-chan Chunk, error)
186		Detect(ctx context.Context, text string) (string, error)
187		Available(ctx context.Context) bool
188		Supports(m Mode) bool
189	}
190	
191	// single is a helper for non-streaming engines: it returns a closed channel
192	// carrying exactly one terminal Chunk.
193	func single(res *TranslateResult, err error) <-chan Chunk {
194		ch := make(chan Chunk, 1)
195		if err != nil {
196			ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: err}
197		} else {
198			ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkDone, Result: res}
199		}
200		close(ch)
201		return ch
202	}
203	
204	// Drain consumes an engine channel to completion, returning the final result.
205	// It streams tokens to onToken (if non-nil) as they arrive. Used by the
206	// one-shot CLI path; the TUI drives the channel directly via its Update loop.
207	func Drain(ch <-chan Chunk, onToken func(string)) (*TranslateResult, error) {
208		for c := range ch {
209			switch c.Kind {
210			case ChunkToken:
211				if onToken != nil {
212					onToken(c.Text)
213				}
214			case ChunkDone:
215				return c.Result, nil
216			case ChunkError:
217				return nil, c.Err
218			}
219		}
220		return nil, ErrNoResult
221	}
222
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
# TODO

Long-term backlog for translate. See AGENTS.md
for the maintenance workflow that agents should follow.

> **For agents**: when the user surfaces an idea explicitly **not** being
> implemented this session (signals: "maybe later", "nice to have",
> "工程量太大需要再評估", "先記下來"), add it here with priority + effort tags.
> Do not create new `ROADMAP.md` / `IDEAS.md` / `BACKLOG.md` files —
> `TODO.md` is the single backlog index. Long-form research goes in
> [`backlog/<slug>.md`](backlog/).

<!-- Use the exact section order: P1, P2, P3, P?, Done.
     The bundled scripts/todo-kanban.sh validator only inspects top-level
     `- [ ]` and `- ✅` items inside these sections. Prose paragraphs,
     blockquotes, indented sub-bullets, HTML comments, and `---` rules are
     ignored — feel free to add inline guidance like this without breaking
     machine readability. -->

## P1

Likely next batch — items you'd reach for if you sat down to work today.

## P2

Worth doing, no rush.

- [ ] **[S] Align `just install` with `go install` / `~/.local/bin`** — the `install` recipe copies the binary to `~/.dotfiles/bin` (PATH position 11), which **shadows** the `go install` location `~/.local/bin` (position 13); a stale `just install` copy would win silently. Point the recipe at `GOBIN=$HOME/.local/bin go install .` (or drop it) so both paths agree. See [pitfalls/duplicate-translate-on-path-dotfiles-bin-shadows-local-bin.md](pitfalls/duplicate-translate-on-path-dotfiles-bin-shadows-local-bin.md).

## P3

Someday / nice-to-have.

- [ ] **[M] lingua-go detection upgrade behind the `Detector` seam** — whatlanggo is light/fast but weak on short and mixed-script text; lingua-go is more accurate but heavy (embedded n-gram models → bigger binary, slower init). Swap behind the existing `internal/lang` interface only if short-text detection proves insufficient. Detection is mostly a fallback (Google returns the source, LLM returns `DetectedSource`), so keeping it light is defensible.
- [ ] **[S] MyMemory fallback engine** — trivial flat JSON (`responseData.translatedText`, `matches[]`); wire as an easy secondary free path after Google. Limits: 5k chars/day anon (50k with `&de=email`), 500 B max per `q`, no source auto-detect.
- [ ] **[M] Azure Translator engine behind the engine seam** — the only supported Microsoft path (needs an Azure subscription key). Keyless Bing scraping (`ttranslatev3` + transient `IG`/`IID`/token) is too fragile/ToS-risky for v1. Leave the engine interface ready; add when a key is available.
- [ ] **[S] Drop the whole-request `http.Client{Timeout: 60s}` on the streaming path** — in `internal/engine/llm.go`, `NewLLM` sets a client-wide `Timeout` that Go applies to the *entire* request including reading the streamed body, so a long translation is cut at 60s mid-stream. Now surfaced as `⚠ truncated` (see [pitfalls/llm-stream-truncation-silently-rendered-as-complete.md](pitfalls/llm-stream-truncation-silently-rendered-as-complete.md)) instead of silently accepted, but the long output still can't finish. Fix by relying on `ctx` deadlines for the streaming `Do()` (keep the short timeout only for `Available`/`Models` probes).

## P?

Needs a spike before committing to a real priority. Tag as `[?/Effort]`.

- [ ] **[?/L] Ship prebuilt release binaries (goreleaser + GitHub Releases)** — cross-compile per OS/arch (pure-Go, no cgo — trivial) so hosts without a Go toolchain install via chezmoi `.chezmoiexternal` with a templated `{{ .chezmoi.os }}/{{ .chezmoi.arch }}` URL instead of `go install`. Also unlocks shipping the dictionary DB as a release asset. → [research](backlog/release-binaries.md)
- [ ] **[?/M] Bundle or prebuild the dictionary vs the 67 MB runtime `dict update`** — evaluate embedding a trimmed DB via `go:embed`, or shipping the built ECDICT sqlite + CC-CEDICT as release assets, so first run isn't a big download/build. Weigh binary-size blowup vs first-run friction. → [research](backlog/dict-bundling.md)

## Done

- ✅ [2026-07-09] [P?/L] Wire `translate` into chezmoi dotfiles as an auto-installed go tool — go_tools ansible role added to dotfiles (commit 306bfb0): go install → ~/.local/bin, mise-gated, + cat_go upgrade path. Pending chezmoi apply on normal cadence.

Recently shipped. When implementing an active item, in the same commit run:

```
scripts/promote-todo.sh --title "<substring>" --summary "<one-line shipped summary>"
```

This moves the entry here using the dated `Done` syntax and re-validates.

- ✅ [2026-07-10] [P1/M] Publish as a public repo + `go install` — renamed the module `translate` → `github.com/daviddwlee84/translate`, rewrote 22 internal imports, tagged `v0.1.0`, and confirmed `go install github.com/daviddwlee84/translate@latest` installs into `~/.local/bin` (GOBIN-pinned, XDG-clean, stable across mise Go upgrades).

<!-- Prune older entries into CHANGELOG.md once prior-year items appear here
     or this section grows past ~20 entries. -->
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./cmd/define.go`

```text
1	package cmd
2	
3	import (
4		"encoding/json"
5		"fmt"
6		"os"
7		"strings"
8	
9		"github.com/spf13/cobra"
10	
11		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/config"
12		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/engine"
13	)
14	
15	var (
16		flagDefinePlain bool
17		flagDefineSmart bool
18	)
19	
20	func newDefineCmd() *cobra.Command {
21		c := &cobra.Command{
22			Use:   "define <word>",
23			Short: "Look up a word in the dictionary (exact → fuzzy → LLM)",
24			Args:  cobra.MinimumNArgs(1),
25			RunE:  runDefine,
26		}
27		c.Flags().BoolVar(&flagDefinePlain, "plain", false, "force the offline dictionary (no LLM fallback)")
28		c.Flags().BoolVar(&flagDefineSmart, "smart", false, "force smart-dict (LLM fallback on a miss)")
29		return c
30	}
31	
32	func runDefine(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
33		cfg, _, err := config.Load()
34		if err != nil {
35			return err
36		}
37		if !cfg.Dict.Enabled {
38			return fmt.Errorf("dictionary is disabled in %s", config.Path())
39		}
40		res := cfg.Resolve(overrides(), config.ModeCLI)
41		de := defineEngine(res)
42		word := strings.Join(args, " ")
43	
44		ch, err := de.Translate(cmd.Context(), engine.Request{Text: word, Mode: engine.ModeDict, Stream: false})
45		if err != nil {
46			return err
47		}
48		res2, err := engine.Drain(ch, nil)
49		if err != nil {
50			return err
51		}
52	
53		if flagJSON {
54			enc := json.NewEncoder(os.Stdout)
55			enc.SetIndent("", "  ")
56			return enc.Encode(res2)
57		}
58		// Surface an LLM-fallback downgrade the same way the CLI translate path does.
59		for _, w := range res2.Warnings {
60			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: warning: %s\n", w)
61		}
62		fmt.Print(renderDict(res2))
63		if flagSpeak && cfg.TTS.Enabled {
64			speakDefine(cmd.Context(), cfg, res2)
65		}
66		return nil
67	}
68	
69	// defineEngine picks the dictionary engine for `translate define`: the plain
70	// offline dictionary, or smart-dict (with an LLM fallback) when a provider is
71	// available and not overridden. --plain forces plain; --smart forces smart.
72	func defineEngine(res config.Resolved) engine.Engine {
73		cfg := res.Cfg
74		if flagDefinePlain || res.Provider == nil {
75			return dictFromConfig(cfg)
76		}
77		if flagDefineSmart || cfg.SmartDict.DefineDefault {
78			return smartDictFromConfig(res)
79		}
80		return dictFromConfig(cfg)
81	}
82	
83	// renderDict formats a dictionary result as plain text (no ANSI, pipe-safe).
84	func renderDict(res *engine.TranslateResult) string {
85		d := res.Dictionary
86		if d == nil {
87			if len(res.Suggestions) > 0 {
88				return "no exact match — did you mean: " + strings.Join(res.Suggestions, ", ") + "\n"
89			}
90			return res.Translation + "\n"
91		}
92		var b strings.Builder
93		head := d.Word
94		if d.Phonetic != "" {
95			head += "  " + d.Phonetic
96		}
97		b.WriteString(head + "\n")
98		for _, m := range d.Meanings {
99			b.WriteString("  " + m.PartOfSpeech + "\n")
100			for i, def := range m.Definitions {
101				if i >= 4 { // cap definitions per part of speech
102					break
103				}
104				b.WriteString("    • " + def.Text + "\n")
105				if def.Example != "" {
106					b.WriteString("      \"" + def.Example + "\"\n")
107				}
108			}
109		}
110		return b.String()
111	}
112
```
</details></tool-use>
