<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/engine/prompt.go`

```text
1	package engine
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"strings"
6	
7		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/lang"
8	)
9	
10	// Preset selects the LLM translate prompt style. Only ModeTranslate LLM engines
11	// honor it; google/dict ignore it.
12	const (
13		PresetConcise    = "concise"    // terse direct translation (default)
14		PresetContextual = "contextual" // short list of common-sense translations
15		PresetDictionary = "dictionary" // translation + 1-2 example sentences
16	)
17	
18	// translateSystemPromptConcise drives the primary (hot-path) translation. It asks
19	// for ONLY the translated text so the reply streams token-by-token identically
20	// across every backend and is latency-optimal.
21	const translateSystemPromptConcise = `You are a precise, professional translation engine.
22	Translate the user's text into the target language faithfully and idiomatically.
23	
24	Rules:
25	- Preserve the meaning, tone, and register (formal/informal, technical/casual) of the source.
26	- The user's text may contain typos, slang, or ungrammatical phrasing. Interpret their
27	  INTENDED meaning and translate that; do not translate a typo literally, and never refuse.
28	- Output ONLY the translated text. No quotes, no explanations, no language labels,
29	  no commentary before or after.
30	- ALWAYS translate into the target language. A single word, name, technical term, or
31	  loanword written in a DIFFERENT language must still be translated — never echo it back.
32	  Only when the text is ALREADY in the target language should you return it (lightly
33	  corrected if it was misspelled).`
34	
35	// translateSystemPromptContextual lists translations across common senses.
36	const translateSystemPromptContextual = `You are a precise, professional translation engine.
37	Translate the user's text into the target language, showing how the translation changes
38	across the most common senses or contexts of the source.
39	
40	Rules:
41	- Preserve meaning, tone, and register. The text may contain typos, slang, or ungrammatical
42	  phrasing — interpret the INTENDED meaning; never refuse and never translate a typo literally.
43	- Output a SHORT list of 2-4 lines, one line per distinct common sense/context.
44	- Format each line EXACTLY as: "N. <translation> — <short context label in the TARGET language>".
45	- Order lines from the most likely/common sense to the least.
46	- If the text has only one natural sense, output a single line — do not invent senses.
47	- Output ONLY the list. No preamble, no headings, no commentary before or after.`
48	
49	// translateSystemPromptDictionary gives a translation plus example sentences.
50	const translateSystemPromptDictionary = `You are a precise, professional translation engine
51	that also gives brief usage examples.
52	Translate the user's text into the target language, then illustrate it with example sentences.
53	
54	Rules:
55	- Preserve meaning, tone, and register. Interpret the INTENDED meaning of typos/slang; never refuse.
56	- The FIRST line is ONLY the translation of the user's text — no label, no quotes.
57	- Then a blank line, then 1-2 example sentences that use the translation naturally.
58	- Format each example on two lines:
59	    line 1: the example sentence in the TARGET language,
60	    line 2: its translation in the source language (the language of the user's input),
61	            prefixed with "  ↳ ".
62	- Keep examples short and idiomatic. Output ONLY the translation and the examples —
63	  no headings, no numbering of the first line, no commentary.`
64	
65	// systemPromptFor returns the system prompt for a preset (defaults to concise).
66	func systemPromptFor(preset string) string {
67		switch preset {
68		case PresetContextual:
69			return translateSystemPromptContextual
70		case PresetDictionary:
71			return translateSystemPromptDictionary
72		default:
73			return translateSystemPromptConcise
74		}
75	}
76	
77	// pairDirective is appended to the system prompt in bidirectional pair mode. It
78	// makes the model own language detection (more robust than a pre-computed target
79	// for short/ambiguous input) and forbids echoing the input — the exact failure
80	// this fixes ("test" → "test" instead of "測試").
81	const pairDirective = `Bidirectional mode: the text is written in ONE of %s or %s. Detect which of the two it is, and translate it into the OTHER one. ALWAYS translate — never return the text unchanged, even for a single word, a proper name, a technical term, or a loanword.`
82	
83	// translateUserPrompt frames the concrete request.
84	const translateUserPrompt = `Source language: %s
85	Target language: %s
86	
87	Text:
88	%s`
89	
90	// buildTranslatePrompt returns the (system, user) messages for a plain-text
91	// translation request. Language codes are expanded to readable names so the
92	// model handles regional variants (e.g. zh-TW → "chinese (traditional)").
93	func buildTranslatePrompt(req Request) (system, user string) {
94		src := strings.TrimSpace(req.Source)
95		if src == "" || src == "auto" {
96			src = "auto"
97		} else {
98			src = fmt.Sprintf("%s (%s)", lang.Name(src), src)
99		}
100		tgt := fmt.Sprintf("%s (%s)", lang.Name(req.Target), req.Target)
101		system = systemPromptFor(req.Preset)
102		// In pair mode, let the model detect the input language and route to the other
103		// side, and never echo — this is appended to (not a replacement of) the preset
104		// so the chosen output format (concise/contextual/dictionary) is preserved.
105		if req.Pair && req.PairHome != "" && req.PairAway != "" {
106			system += "\n\n" + fmt.Sprintf(pairDirective, lang.Name(req.PairHome), lang.Name(req.PairAway))
107		}
108		if extra := strings.TrimSpace(req.Extra); extra != "" {
109			system += "\n\nUser preferences (apply where relevant, e.g. domain terminology):\n" + extra
110		}
111		return system, fmt.Sprintf(translateUserPrompt, src, tgt, req.Text)
112	}
113	
114	// learnDirection reports the learn-mode direction, decided OFFLINE via the same
115	// script-aware router as pair mode (lang.PairTarget): native (home) input → "teach"
116	// (translate + glosses); foreign (away) input → "correct" (grammar-correct + explain).
117	// Trusting this over the model's own guess keeps the two output shapes deterministic.
118	func learnDirection(req Request) string {
119		if lang.PairTarget(req.PairHome, req.PairAway, req.Text) == req.PairAway {
120			return "teach"
121		}
122		return "correct"
123	}
124	
125	// learnTeachPrompt drives native→foreign tutoring. %[1]s = native (home) language,
126	// %[2]s = foreign (away) language being learned.
127	const learnTeachPrompt = `You are a warm, encouraging language tutor helping a native %[1]s speaker learn %[2]s.
128	The user writes in %[1]s. Translate their text into natural, idiomatic %[2]s, then teach it.
129	
130	Respond with ONE JSON object and NOTHING else — no markdown code fence, no prose before or after it.
131	Schema (fill every relevant field; omit a field only when it genuinely has no content):
132	{
133	  "direction": "teach",
134	  "original": "<the user's %[1]s input, verbatim>",
135	  "translation": "<the idiomatic %[2]s translation>",
136	  "vocab": [
137	    {"term": "<key %[2]s word or phrase>", "pos": "<part of speech>", "phonetic": "<KK/IPA for English, pinyin for Chinese>", "meaning": "<concise meaning in %[1]s>"}
138	  ],
139	  "examples": [
140	    {"foreign": "<a short natural %[2]s sentence using the translation>", "native": "<its %[1]s translation>"}
141	  ],
142	  "notes": "<one short usage or register tip in %[1]s, optional>"
143	}
144	
145	Rules:
146	- Write every meaning, note, and explanation in %[1]s (the learner's language).
147	- vocab: only the key content words/phrases worth learning (at most 8); skip trivial function words.
148	- examples: 1-2, short and idiomatic.
149	- Interpret the intended meaning of typos/slang; never refuse. Output ONLY the JSON object.`
150	
151	// learnCorrectPrompt drives foreign→native correction. %[1]s = foreign (away)
152	// language being learned, %[2]s = native (home) language.
153	const learnCorrectPrompt = `You are a warm, encouraging language tutor helping a %[2]s speaker who is learning %[1]s.
154	The user writes a sentence in %[1]s that may contain mistakes. Correct it and explain, kindly.
155	
156	Respond with ONE JSON object and NOTHING else — no markdown code fence, no prose before or after it.
157	Schema (fill every relevant field; omit a field only when it genuinely has no content):
158	{
159	  "direction": "correct",
160	  "original": "<the user's %[1]s input, verbatim>",
161	  "corrected": "<the grammatically correct, idiomatic %[1]s sentence>",
162	  "translation": "<a %[2]s translation of the intended meaning>",
163	  "issues": [
164	    {"span": "<the problematic fragment of the input>", "fix": "<the corrected fragment>", "explanation": "<why, in %[2]s>"}
165	  ],
166	  "notes": "<one short encouraging tip in %[2]s, optional>"
167	}
168	
169	Rules:
170	- Write every explanation and note in %[2]s (the learner's native language).
171	- If the sentence is already correct, echo it in "corrected" and return "issues": [].
172	- List each distinct mistake as one issue; keep explanations concise and beginner-friendly.
173	- Interpret the intended meaning of typos/slang; never refuse. Output ONLY the JSON object.`
174	
175	// learnUserPrompt frames the concrete learn request. The system prompt already
176	// names both languages, so the user turn carries only the text.
177	const learnUserPrompt = `Text:
178	%s`
179	
180	// buildLearnPrompt returns the (system, user) messages for a learn-mode request,
181	// choosing the teach or correct prompt from the offline-detected direction. Codes
182	// are expanded to readable names so regional variants (zh-TW) read naturally.
183	func buildLearnPrompt(req Request) (system, user string) {
184		home := lang.Name(req.PairHome) // native
185		away := lang.Name(req.PairAway) // foreign
186		if learnDirection(req) == "teach" {
187			system = fmt.Sprintf(learnTeachPrompt, home, away)
188		} else {
189			system = fmt.Sprintf(learnCorrectPrompt, away, home)
190		}
191		if extra := strings.TrimSpace(req.Extra); extra != "" {
192			system += "\n\nUser preferences (apply where relevant, e.g. domain terminology):\n" + extra
193		}
194		return system, fmt.Sprintf(learnUserPrompt, req.Text)
195	}
196
```
</details></tool-use>

 `./internal/tui/cache.go`

```text
1	package tui
2	
3	import "github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/engine"
4	
5	// cacheKey identifies a translation result for the session cache. It is
6	// comparable, so it can be a map key. Keying on the *selected* engine name
7	// (e.g. "auto") means a chain fallback still caches under the user-visible engine.
8	// learn/pair/pairWith are included so a learn result, a pair result, and a plain
9	// translation of the same text never collide.
10	type cacheKey struct {
11		preset, engineName, model, source, target, text string
12		learn, pair                                     bool
13		pairWith                                        string
14	}
15	
16	// cacheKeyFor builds the key for the given input text under the current settings.
17	func (m Model) cacheKeyFor(text string) cacheKey {
18		return cacheKey{
19			preset:     m.preset,
20			engineName: m.active().Name,
21			model:      m.modelOverride,
22			source:     m.source,
23			target:     m.target,
24			text:       text,
25			learn:      m.learn,
26			pair:       m.pair,
27			pairWith:   m.pairWith,
28		}
29	}
30	
31	// cacheStore is the session result cache. Session-scoped and unbounded (small);
32	// LLM output is nondeterministic so it is not persisted across sessions.
33	type cacheStore map[cacheKey]*engine.TranslateResult
34
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>

 `./cmd/root.go`

```text
1	// Package cmd wires the cobra command tree. The root command dispatches between
2	// the one-shot CLI (arguments or piped stdin) and the interactive TUI (a TTY
3	// with no arguments); every path drives the shared engine layer.
4	package cmd
5	
6	import (
7		"context"
8		"encoding/json"
9		"fmt"
10		"io"
11		"os"
12		"path/filepath"
13		"strings"
14	
15		"github.com/spf13/cobra"
16		"golang.org/x/term"
17	
18		tea "charm.land/bubbletea/v2"
19	
20		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/config"
21		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/debug"
22		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/engine"
23		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/lang"
24		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/state"
25		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/store"
26		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/tui"
27		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/xdgpath"
28	)
29	
30	var (
31		flagTo           string
32		flagFrom         string
33		flagModel        string
34		flagProvider     string
35		flagEngine       string
36		flagTier         string
37		flagPreset       string
38		flagInstructions string
39		flagPair         bool
40		flagPairWith     string
41		flagLearn        bool
42		flagJSON         bool
43		flagNoHistory    bool
44		flagDebug        bool
45		flagSpeak        bool
46		flagSpeakLang    string
47	)
48	
49	// NewRootCmd builds the root command and its subcommands.
50	func NewRootCmd() *cobra.Command {
51		root := &cobra.Command{
52			Use:   "translate [text...]",
53			Short: "Fast terminal translation (CLI + TUI)",
54			Long: "translate is a fast CLI/TUI translator.\n\n" +
55				"  translate \"hola mundo\" --to en    one-shot\n" +
56				"  echo hola | translate --to en       pipe\n" +
57				"  translate                           interactive TUI",
58			Version:       buildVersion(),
59			SilenceUsage:  true,
60			SilenceErrors: true,
61			// ArbitraryArgs lets bare text fall through to translation while exact
62			// subcommands (config, lang, …) still route. Without it, cobra rejects
63			// `translate "hola"` as an unknown command once subcommands exist.
64			Args: cobra.ArbitraryArgs,
65			RunE: runRoot,
66		}
67		f := root.PersistentFlags()
68		f.StringVarP(&flagTo, "to", "t", "", "target language (fuzzy; e.g. en, chinese)")
69		f.StringVarP(&flagFrom, "from", "f", "", "source language (\"auto\" to detect)")
70		f.StringVar(&flagModel, "model", "", "LLM model id override")
71		f.StringVar(&flagProvider, "provider", "", "provider name (e.g. copilot, ollama)")
72		f.StringVar(&flagEngine, "engine", "", "engine: auto|<provider>|google|dict")
73		f.StringVar(&flagTier, "tier", "", "model tier: default|fast|max")
74		f.StringVar(&flagPreset, "preset", "", "LLM prompt style: concise|contextual|dictionary")
75		f.StringVar(&flagInstructions, "instructions", "", "extra system-prompt guidance (domain focus, etc.)")
76		f.BoolVar(&flagPair, "pair", false, "bidirectional mode: home-language input → --pair-with, else → --to")
77		f.StringVar(&flagPairWith, "pair-with", "", "the other language for --pair (e.g. en)")
78		f.BoolVar(&flagLearn, "learn", false, "learning mode: teach (native→foreign) or grammar-correct (foreign→native)")
79		f.BoolVar(&flagJSON, "json", false, "emit the full result as JSON")
80		f.BoolVar(&flagNoHistory, "no-history", false, "do not record this translation in history")
81		f.BoolVar(&flagDebug, "debug", false, "log intermediate decisions (routing, engine choice, dict hit/miss)")
82		f.BoolVarP(&flagSpeak, "speak", "s", false, "speak the foreign side of the result aloud (free TTS)")
83		f.StringVar(&flagSpeakLang, "speak-lang", "", "force the spoken language (e.g. en, zh-TW)")
84	
85		root.SuggestionsMinimumDistance = 2
86		root.AddCommand(newConfigCmd(), newLangCmd(), newDefineCmd(), newHistoryCmd(), newInitCmd(), newDictCmd(), newSpeakCmd())
87		return root
88	}
89	
90	// Execute runs the root command with the given (cancellable) context.
91	func Execute(ctx context.Context) error {
92		return NewRootCmd().ExecuteContext(ctx)
93	}
94	
95	// overrides collects the explicitly-set flag values.
96	func overrides() config.Overrides {
97		return config.Overrides{
98			Source:       flagFrom,
99			Target:       flagTo,
100			Engine:       flagEngine,
101			Provider:     flagProvider,
102			Model:        flagModel,
103			Tier:         flagTier,
104			Preset:       flagPreset,
105			Instructions: flagInstructions,
106			Pair:         flagPair,
107			PairWith:     flagPairWith,
108			Learn:        flagLearn,
109			Debug:        flagDebug,
110		}
111	}
112	
113	// invocationMode reports whether this run is the one-shot CLI (arguments or piped
114	// stdin) or the interactive TUI (a TTY with no arguments). It mirrors the dispatch
115	// switch in runRoot and is computed before Resolve so the [cli]/[tui] overlay applies.
116	func invocationMode(args []string) config.Mode {
117		if len(args) > 0 {
118			return config.ModeCLI
119		}
120		if !term.IsTerminal(int(os.Stdin.Fd())) {
121			return config.ModeCLI
122		}
123		return config.ModeTUI
124	}
125	
126	func runRoot(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
127		ctx := cmd.Context()
128	
129		cfg, created, err := config.Load()
130		if err != nil {
131			return err
132		}
133		if created {
134			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: wrote default config to %s (run `translate init` to customize)\n", config.Path())
135		}
136	
137		res := cfg.Resolve(overrides(), invocationMode(args))
138		if res.Debug {
139			// The one-shot CLI logs to stderr; the TUI logs to a file, since its
140			// alt-screen would be corrupted by stderr writes.
141			if invocationMode(args) == config.ModeCLI {
142				debug.Enable(os.Stderr)
143			} else if w := openDebugLog(); w != nil {
144				debug.Enable(w)
145			}
146		}
147		applyLastPair(cfg, &res) // remember_last_pair: seed source/target from state
148		src, tgt := resolvePair(res.Source, res.Target)
149		pairWith := ""
150		if res.Pair {
151			pwm, _ := lang.Resolve(res.PairWith)
152			pairWith = pwm.Code
153		}
154		// Learn mode is bidirectional; ensure a distinct "away" (foreign) language so
155		// direction routing has two sides to choose between.
156		if res.Learn && (pairWith == "" || strings.EqualFold(pairWith, tgt)) {
157			pairWith = defaultAway(tgt)
158		}
159		debug.Logf("resolved: engine=%s tier=%s preset=%s source=%s target=%s pair=%v pair_with=%s learn=%v",
160			res.Engine, res.Tier, res.Preset, src, tgt, res.Pair, pairWith, res.Learn)
161		if res.Pair && !res.Learn && (pairWith == "" || strings.EqualFold(pairWith, tgt)) {
162			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: warning: pair mode is on but pair-with (%q) equals the target (%q) — pair mode is a no-op; set a different pair_with (run `translate init`)\n", pairWith, tgt)
163		}
164	
165		if res.Provider == nil && res.Engine != "auto" {
166			return fmt.Errorf("no provider configured; check %s", config.Path())
167		}
168		eng, err := buildEngine(res)
169		if err != nil {
170			return err
171		}
172	
173		// The one-shot CLI path routes a learn request through a bare LLM engine
174		// (bypassing smart-auto/dictionary); the TUI keeps its normal primary engine and
175		// toggles learn at runtime against Params.LearnEngine.
176		learnCLI := res.Learn && invocationMode(args) == config.ModeCLI
177		if learnCLI && res.Provider == nil {
178			return fmt.Errorf("learn mode requires an LLM provider; check %s", config.Path())
179		}
180		oneShotEng := eng
181		if learnCLI {
182			oneShotEng = learnEngineFromConfig(res)
183		}
184	
185		st := openStore(cfg)
186		if st != nil {
187			defer st.Close()
188		}
189	
190		switch {
191		case len(args) > 0:
192			text := strings.Join(args, " ")
193			effTgt := effectiveTarget(res.Pair, tgt, pairWith, text)
194			r, err := oneShot(ctx, oneShotEng, text, src, effTgt, res.Stream, res.Preset, res.Instructions, res.Pair, tgt, pairWith, res.Learn)
195			if err != nil {
196				return err
197			}
198			recordAndRemember(ctx, st, cfg, r, text, src, effTgt, tgt)
199			if shouldSpeak(cfg) {
200				speakResult(ctx, cfg, r, text, src, effTgt, speakForeign(cfg, pairWith))
201			}
202			return nil
203	
204		case !term.IsTerminal(int(os.Stdin.Fd())):
205			b, err := io.ReadAll(os.Stdin)
206			if err != nil {
207				return err
208			}
209			text := strings.TrimSpace(string(b))
210			if text == "" {
211				return fmt.Errorf("no input on stdin")
212			}
213			effTgt := effectiveTarget(res.Pair, tgt, pairWith, text)
214			r, err := oneShot(ctx, oneShotEng, text, src, effTgt, res.Stream, res.Preset, res.Instructions, res.Pair, tgt, pairWith, res.Learn)
215			if err != nil {
216				return err
217			}
218			recordAndRemember(ctx, st, cfg, r, text, src, effTgt, tgt)
219			if shouldSpeak(cfg) {
220				speakResult(ctx, cfg, r, text, src, effTgt, speakForeign(cfg, pairWith))
221			}
222			return nil
223	
224		default:
225			return runTUI(ctx, eng, res, st, src, tgt, pairWith)
226		}
227	}
228	
229	// effectiveTarget applies pair mode: home-language input → away, else → home.
230	func effectiveTarget(pair bool, home, away, text string) string {
231		if pair && away != "" {
232			t := lang.PairTarget(home, away, text)
233			debug.Logf("pair route: home=%s away=%s text=%q → target=%s", home, away, clip(text, 30), t)
234			return t
235		}
236		return home
237	}
238	
239	// clip shortens a string for a single-line debug label.
240	func clip(s string, n int) string {
241		s = strings.TrimSpace(s)
242		r := []rune(s)
243		if len(r) > n {
244			return string(r[:n]) + "…"
245		}
246		return s
247	}
248	
249	// openDebugLog opens (append) the TUI debug log file, creating the state dir.
250	// Returns nil on failure (debug simply stays off).
251	func openDebugLog() io.Writer {
252		if err := xdgpath.EnsureDirs(); err != nil {
253			return nil
254		}
255		f, err := os.OpenFile(filepath.Join(xdgpath.StateDir(), "debug.log"),
256			os.O_CREATE|os.O_WRONLY|os.O_APPEND, 0o600)
257		if err != nil {
258			return nil
259		}
260		return f
261	}
262	
263	// applyLastPair overrides the resolved source/target with the persisted last
264	// pair when remember_last_pair is on and the user did not pass flags/env.
265	func applyLastPair(cfg *config.Config, res *config.Resolved) {
266		if !cfg.General.RememberLastPair {
267			return
268		}
269		stt, err := state.Load()
270		if err != nil || stt == nil {
271			return
272		}
273		if flagFrom == "" && os.Getenv("TRANSLATE_SOURCE") == "" && stt.Source != "" {
274			res.Source = stt.Source
275			debug.Logf("remember_last_pair: source ← %s (from state)", stt.Source)
276		}
277		if flagTo == "" && os.Getenv("TRANSLATE_TARGET") == "" && stt.Target != "" {
278			res.Target = stt.Target
279			debug.Logf("remember_last_pair: target ← %s (from state)", stt.Target)
280		}
281	}
282	
283	// openStore opens the history store, or returns nil when history is disabled or
284	// suppressed with --no-history.
285	func openStore(cfg *config.Config) store.Store {
286		if !cfg.History.Enabled || flagNoHistory {
287			return nil
288		}
289		path := cfg.History.Path
290		if path == "" {
291			path = xdgpath.HistoryFile()
292		}
293		st, err := store.OpenJSONL(path)
294		if err != nil {
295			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: history disabled (%v)\n", err)
296			return nil
297		}
298		return st
299	}
300	
301	// recordAndRemember writes the translation to history and persists the last pair.
302	//
303	// target is the effective target actually used (for history); rememberTarget is
304	// the stable home target (== target unless pair mode redirected this one input),
305	// so persisting it keeps pair mode from drifting its home to the away language.
306	func recordAndRemember(ctx context.Context, st store.Store, cfg *config.Config, res *engine.TranslateResult, input, source, target, rememberTarget string) {
307		if st != nil {
308			_, _ = st.Add(ctx, toRecord(res, input, source, target))
309		}
310		if cfg.General.RememberLastPair {
311			saveLastPair(source, rememberTarget, res.Engine)
312		}
313	}
314	
315	// toRecord builds a history Record from a translation result.
316	func toRecord(res *engine.TranslateResult, input, source, target string) store.Record {
317		src := source
318		if src == "auto" && res.DetectedSource != "" {
319			src = res.DetectedSource
320		}
321		return store.Record{
322			SourceLang:   src,
323			TargetLang:   target,
324			Engine:       res.Engine,
325			Model:        res.Model,
326			Input:        input,
327			Output:       res.Translation,
328			Alternatives: res.Alternatives,
329			Notes:        res.Notes,
330		}
331	}
332	
333	// saveLastPair persists the source/target pair and source mode.
334	func saveLastPair(source, target, engineName string) {
335		mode := "fixed"
336		if source == "auto" {
337			mode = "auto"
338		}
339		_ = state.Save(&state.State{
340			Source:     source,
341			Target:     target,
342			SourceMode: mode,
343			Engine:     engineName,
344		})
345	}
346	
347	// runTUI launches the interactive Bubble Tea front-end and persists the last
348	// pair on exit.
349	func runTUI(ctx context.Context, eng engine.Engine, res config.Resolved, st store.Store, source, target, pairWith string) error {
350		// A model source for the ^p model picker: the resolved LLM provider (if any).
351		var modelSrc engine.ModelLister
352		modelProvider := ""
353		if res.Provider != nil {
354			modelSrc = llmFromProvider(res.Provider, res.Model)
355			modelProvider = res.Provider.Name
356		}
357		// Learn mode (^n) runs against a bare LLM engine; nil when no provider is
358		// configured, in which case the toggle refuses.
359		var learnEng engine.Engine
360		if res.Provider != nil {
361			learnEng = learnEngineFromConfig(res)
362		}
363		p := tui.Params{
364			Engines:       buildEngineSet(res, eng),
365			ModelSource:   modelSrc,
366			ModelProvider: modelProvider,
367			Store:         st,
368			Source:        source,
369			Target:        target,
370			Pair:          res.Pair,
371			PairWith:      pairWith,
372			Learn:         res.Learn,
373			LearnEngine:   learnEng,
374			Model:         res.Model,
375			Preset:        res.Preset,
376			Instructions:  res.Instructions,
377			Live:          res.LiveTranslate,
378			DebounceMs:    res.DebounceMs,
379			Speaker:       tuiSpeaker(res.Cfg),
380			Foreign:       speakForeign(res.Cfg, pairWith),
381		}
382		final, err := tea.NewProgram(tui.New(ctx, p), tea.WithContext(ctx)).Run()
383		if err != nil {
384			return err
385		}
386		if res.Cfg.General.RememberLastPair {
387			if fm, ok := final.(tui.Model); ok {
388				s, t := fm.Pair()
389				saveLastPair(s, t, "")
390			}
391		}
392		return nil
393	}
394	
395	// resolvePair fuzzy-resolves the source and target languages, printing an
396	// "(interpreted X as Y)" note on stderr when a fuzzy correction was applied.
397	func resolvePair(rawSource, rawTarget string) (source, target string) {
398		sm, _ := lang.Resolve(rawSource)
399		tm, _ := lang.Resolve(rawTarget)
400		if !sm.Exact && sm.Score > 0 && !strings.EqualFold(rawSource, sm.Code) {
401			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: interpreted source %q as %s (%s)\n", rawSource, sm.Code, sm.Name)
402		}
403		if !tm.Exact && tm.Score > 0 && !strings.EqualFold(rawTarget, tm.Code) {
404			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: interpreted target %q as %s (%s)\n", rawTarget, tm.Code, tm.Name)
405		}
406		return sm.Code, tm.Code
407	}
408	
409	// oneShot translates text once and prints the result, returning it for history.
410	//
411	// Tokens stream live to stdout only when stdout is a TTY (so `translate x | pbcopy`
412	// stays clean) and --json was not requested. Piped output is the plain translation
413	// with no ANSI; --json emits the full structured result.
414	func oneShot(ctx context.Context, eng engine.Engine, text, source, target string, streamPref bool, preset, instructions string, pair bool, pairHome, pairAway string, learn bool) (*engine.TranslateResult, error) {
415		stdoutTTY := term.IsTerminal(int(os.Stdout.Fd()))
416		stream := streamPref && stdoutTTY && !flagJSON && !learn // learn output is structured (parsed at done)
417	
418		req := engine.Request{
419			Text:     text,
420			Source:   source,
421			Target:   target,
422			Mode:     engine.ModeTranslate,
423			Stream:   stream,
424			Preset:   preset,
425			Extra:    instructions,
426			Pair:     pair,
427			PairHome: pairHome,
428			PairAway: pairAway,
429			Learn:    learn,
430		}
431		ch, err := eng.Translate(ctx, req)
432		if err != nil {
433			return nil, err
434		}
435	
436		var onTok func(string)
437		if stream {
438			onTok = func(t string) { fmt.Print(t) }
439		}
440		res, err := engine.Drain(ch, onTok)
441		if err != nil {
442			if stream {
443				fmt.Println() // terminate any partial line before the error surfaces
444			}
445			return nil, err
446		}
447	
448		// Never downgrade silently: if the auto-chain fell back, say which engine
449		// failed and why, and how to switch.
450		for _, w := range res.Warnings {
451			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: warning: %s\n", w)
452		}
453		if len(res.Warnings) > 0 && res.Learn == nil {
454			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: used %q (switch with --engine, or check the model/provider)\n", res.Engine)
455		}
456	
457		switch {
458		case flagJSON:
459			enc := json.NewEncoder(os.Stdout)
460			enc.SetIndent("", "  ")
461			if err := enc.Encode(res); err != nil {
462				return nil, err
463			}
464		case res.Learn != nil:
465			fmt.Print(renderLearnCLI(res))
466		case stream:
467			fmt.Println() // newline after the streamed tokens
468		default:
469			fmt.Println(res.Translation)
470		}
471		return res, nil
472	}
473	
474	// defaultAway picks the "away" (foreign) language for learn/pair mode when none is
475	// configured: English speakers default to learning zh-TW, everyone else to en. It
476	// mirrors the TUI ^g heuristic so the CLI and TUI agree.
477	func defaultAway(home string) string {
478		if strings.HasPrefix(strings.ToLower(home), "en") {
479			return "zh-TW"
480		}
481		return "en"
482	}
483	
484	// renderLearnCLI formats a learn-mode result as plain text (no ANSI, pipe-safe).
485	func renderLearnCLI(res *engine.TranslateResult) string {
486		l := res.Learn
487		if l == nil {
488			return res.Translation + "\n"
489		}
490		var b strings.Builder
491		if l.Direction == "correct" {
492			corrected := strings.TrimSpace(l.Corrected)
493			if corrected == "" {
494				corrected = res.Translation
495			}
496			b.WriteString("✔ " + corrected + "\n")
497			if s := strings.TrimSpace(l.Translation); s != "" {
498				b.WriteString("  ↳ " + s + "\n")
499			}
500			for _, is := range l.Issues {
501				frag := strings.TrimSpace(strings.TrimSuffix(strings.TrimPrefix(is.Span+" → "+is.Fix, " → "), " → "))
502				expl := strings.TrimSpace(is.Explanation)
503				line := expl
504				switch {
505				case frag != "" && expl != "":
506					line = frag + ": " + expl
507				case frag != "":
508					line = frag
509				}
510				if line != "" {
511					b.WriteString("  ✎ " + line + "\n")
512				}
513			}
514		} else {
515			tr := strings.TrimSpace(l.Translation)
516			if tr == "" {
517				tr = res.Translation
518			}
519			b.WriteString(tr + "\n")
520			for _, v := range l.Vocab {
521				head := "  • " + v.Term
522				if v.Pos != "" {
523					head += " (" + v.Pos + ")"
524				}
525				if v.Phonetic != "" {
526					head += " " + v.Phonetic
527				}
528				if v.Meaning != "" {
529					head += " — " + v.Meaning
530				}
531				b.WriteString(head + "\n")
532			}
533			for _, ex := range l.Examples {
534				b.WriteString("  ✎ " + ex.Foreign + "\n")
535				if s := strings.TrimSpace(ex.Native); s != "" {
536					b.WriteString("    ↳ " + s + "\n")
537				}
538			}
539		}
540		if s := strings.TrimSpace(l.Notes); s != "" {
541			b.WriteString("  ⓘ " + s + "\n")
542		}
543		return b.String()
544	}
545
```

```text
1	// Package config defines translate's on-disk configuration (config.toml) and the
2	// resolution of effective settings (flags > config > env > defaults).
3	package config
4	
5	import (
6		"errors"
7		"fmt"
8		"os"
9		"path/filepath"
10	
11		"github.com/pelletier/go-toml/v2"
12	
13		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/xdgpath"
14	)
15	
16	// Config is the top-level config.toml schema.
17	type Config struct {
18		General   General    `toml:"general"`
19		CLI       *Overlay   `toml:"cli,omitempty"` // per-front-end overrides on [general]
20		TUI       *Overlay   `toml:"tui,omitempty"` // (nil unless the user adds the table)
21		Chain     Chain      `toml:"chain"`
22		Providers []Provider `toml:"provider"` // array-of-tables: [[provider]]
23		Google    Google     `toml:"google"`
24		Dict      Dict       `toml:"dict"`
25		SmartDict SmartDict  `toml:"smartdict"`
26		TTS       TTS        `toml:"tts"`
27		History   History    `toml:"history"`
28	}
29	
30	// Overlay is a partial [general] applied for one front-end ([cli] or [tui]). Every
31	// field is a pointer so a nil means "inherit from [general]"; only keys the user
32	// actually wrote override. Precedence: flag > env > [cli]/[tui] > [general] > default.
33	type Overlay struct {
34		Engine        *string `toml:"engine,omitempty"`
35		Tier          *string `toml:"tier,omitempty"`
36		Preset        *string `toml:"preset,omitempty"`
37		Instructions  *string `toml:"instructions,omitempty"`
38		Model         *string `toml:"model,omitempty"`
39		DefaultTarget *string `toml:"default_target,omitempty"`
40		DefaultSource *string `toml:"default_source,omitempty"`
41		Pair          *bool   `toml:"pair,omitempty"`
42		PairWith      *string `toml:"pair_with,omitempty"`
43		Learn         *bool   `toml:"learn,omitempty"`
44		Stream        *bool   `toml:"stream,omitempty"`
45		LiveTranslate *bool   `toml:"live_translate,omitempty"`
46		DebounceMs    *int    `toml:"debounce_ms,omitempty"`
47		Debug         *bool   `toml:"debug,omitempty"`
48	}
49	
50	// General holds behavior settings.
51	type General struct {
52		DefaultTarget     string `toml:"default_target"`
53		DefaultSource     string `toml:"default_source"`      // "auto" or a fixed code
54		Pair              bool   `toml:"pair"`                // bidirectional: home-language input → pair_with, else → default_target
55		PairWith          string `toml:"pair_with,omitempty"` // the "away" language for pair mode
56		Learn             bool   `toml:"learn"`               // learning mode: teach (native→foreign) or grammar-correct (foreign→native)
57		RememberLastPair  bool   `toml:"remember_last_pair"`
58		LiveTranslate     bool   `toml:"live_translate"`
59		DebounceMs        int    `toml:"debounce_ms"`
60		Engine            string `toml:"engine"`                 // auto | smartauto | llm | google | <provider>
61		Tier              string `toml:"tier"`                   // default | fast | max
62		Preset            string `toml:"preset"`                 // concise | contextual | dictionary
63		Instructions      string `toml:"instructions,omitempty"` // extra system-prompt guidance (domain focus, etc.)
64		AlternativesCount int    `toml:"alternatives_count"`
65		Stream            bool   `toml:"stream"`
66		Color             string `toml:"color"` // auto | always | never
67		Debug             bool   `toml:"debug"` // verbose decision logging (also --debug / TRANSLATE_DEBUG)
68	}
69	
70	// Chain is the ordered fallback list of provider/engine names.
71	type Chain struct {
72		Order []string `toml:"order"`
73	}
74	
75	// Provider is one OpenAI-compatible LLM backend.
76	type Provider struct {
77		Name      string `toml:"name"`
78		Type      string `toml:"type"` // openai | ollama | openrouter | litellm | generic
79		BaseURL   string `toml:"base_url"`
80		Model     string `toml:"model"`
81		ModelFast string `toml:"model_fast,omitempty"`
82		ModelMax  string `toml:"model_max,omitempty"`
83		APIKeyEnv string `toml:"api_key_env,omitempty"` // "" => no Authorization header
84	}
85	
86	// Google configures the free translate_a/single endpoint.
87	type Google struct {
88		Enabled   bool     `toml:"enabled"`
89		Endpoint  string   `toml:"endpoint,omitempty"`
90		ExtraDT   []string `toml:"extra_dt,omitempty"`
91		UserAgent string   `toml:"user_agent,omitempty"`
92		TimeoutMs int      `toml:"timeout_ms,omitempty"`
93	}
94	
95	// Dict configures the dictionary lookup engine.
96	type Dict struct {
97		Enabled  bool   `toml:"enabled"`
98		Source   string `toml:"source"` // local (CC-CEDICT + ECDICT) | api (dictionaryapi.dev)
99		Endpoint string `toml:"endpoint,omitempty"`
100		Lang     string `toml:"lang,omitempty"`
101		Fuzzy    bool   `toml:"fuzzy"`
102		Wordlist string `toml:"wordlist,omitempty"` // "" => /usr/share/dict/words if present
103	
104		// Local bilingual dictionary (source=local).
105		Dir          string `toml:"dir,omitempty"` // "" => <XDG data>/dict
106		CedictURL    string `toml:"cedict_url,omitempty"`
107		EcdictURL    string `toml:"ecdict_url,omitempty"`
108		AutoDownload bool   `toml:"auto_download"` // auto-fetch CC-CEDICT (small) on first zh lookup
109		APIFallback  bool   `toml:"api_fallback"`  // fall back to dictionaryapi.dev on an English miss
110	}
111	
112	// TTS configures free, cross-platform text-to-speech (pronunciation playback).
113	// Backends are tried in Order: "native" (macOS say / Linux espeak / Windows SAPI,
114	// offline) then "google" (translate_tts MP3 + a discovered player).
115	type TTS struct {
116		Enabled       bool              `toml:"enabled"`
117		AutoSpeak     bool              `toml:"auto_speak"`        // CLI: speak every result without --speak
118		Order         []string          `toml:"order,omitempty"`   // e.g. ["native","google"]
119		Foreign       string            `toml:"foreign,omitempty"` // the 副/away language to speak; "" => derive
120		PreferForeign bool              `toml:"prefer_foreign"`    // auto-side selection prefers the foreign side
121		Rate          int               `toml:"rate,omitempty"`    // native words/min; 0 => backend default
122		Voices        map[string]string `toml:"voices,omitempty"`  // lang -> native voice override
123		GoogleTTSURL  string            `toml:"google_tts_url,omitempty"`
124		UserAgent     string            `toml:"user_agent,omitempty"`
125		TimeoutMs     int               `toml:"timeout_ms,omitempty"`
126		CacheDir      string            `toml:"cache_dir,omitempty"`
127		Player        string            `toml:"player,omitempty"` // forced audio player binary
128	}
129	
130	// History configures translation history storage.
131	type History struct {
132		Enabled bool   `toml:"enabled"`
133		Backend string `toml:"backend"` // jsonl | sqlite
134		Path    string `toml:"path,omitempty"`
135	}
136	
137	// SmartDict configures the smart-dict engine: a dictionary lookup that falls back
138	// to the LLM when it misses or the fuzzy match is too far off (see engine.SmartDict).
139	type SmartDict struct {
140		// CloseDistance is the English edit-distance at/below which a fuzzy "did you
141		// mean" is treated as a likely typo and kept as-is; beyond it (and any hard
142		// miss) the lookup falls back to the LLM.
143		CloseDistance int    `toml:"close_distance"`
144		Preset        string `toml:"preset,omitempty"` // LLM prompt style for the fallback
145		DefineDefault bool   `toml:"define_default"`   // `translate define` uses smart-dict when a provider is available
146	}
147	
148	// Recommended copilot-proxy model ids per tier. Claude models are served via the
149	// Anthropic Messages API (/v1/messages); the LLM engine routes them there
150	// automatically (they 400 on /chat/completions).
151	const (
152		ModelDefault = "claude-sonnet-5"  // balanced
153		ModelFast    = "claude-haiku-4-5" // snappy — the out-of-box default tier
154		ModelMax     = "claude-opus-4-8"  // highest quality
155	)
156	
157	// Default returns a config with sensible defaults, written on first run.
158	func Default() *Config {
159		return &Config{
160			General: General{
161				DefaultTarget:     "en",
162				DefaultSource:     "auto",
163				Pair:              false,
164				PairWith:          "en", // used when pair mode is enabled
165				RememberLastPair:  true,
166				LiveTranslate:     false, // off by default to avoid spamming LLM/API while typing
167				DebounceMs:        700,   // when live is on, wait longer before firing
168				Engine:            "auto",
169				Tier:              "fast", // haiku by default — snappy for short, quick translations
170				Preset:            "contextual",
171				AlternativesCount: 3,
172				Stream:            true,
173				Color:             "auto",
174			},
175			Chain: Chain{Order: []string{"copilot", "ollama", "google", "dict"}},
176			Providers: []Provider{
177				{
178					Name:      "copilot",
179					Type:      "openai",
180					BaseURL:   "http://localhost:4141/v1",
181					Model:     ModelDefault,
182					ModelFast: ModelFast,
183					ModelMax:  ModelMax,
184				},
185				{
186					Name:    "ollama",
187					Type:    "ollama",
188					BaseURL: "http://localhost:11434/v1",
189					Model:   "llama3.2:3b",
190				},
191				{
192					Name:      "openrouter",
193					Type:      "openrouter",
194					BaseURL:   "https://openrouter.ai/api/v1",
195					Model:     "anthropic/claude-sonnet-5",
196					APIKeyEnv: "OPENROUTER_API_KEY",
197				},
198			},
199			Google: Google{
200				Enabled:   true,
201				Endpoint:  "https://translate.googleapis.com/translate_a/single",
202				ExtraDT:   []string{"bd", "at"},
203				UserAgent: "Mozilla/5.0 translate-cli",
204				TimeoutMs: 4000,
205			},
206			Dict: Dict{
207				Enabled:      true,
208				Source:       "local", // CC-CEDICT (zh→en) + ECDICT (en→zh); run `translate dict update`
209				Endpoint:     "https://api.dictionaryapi.dev/api/v2/entries",
210				Lang:         "en",
211				Fuzzy:        true,
212				CedictURL:    "https://www.mdbg.net/chinese/export/cedict/cedict_1_0_ts_utf-8_mdbg.txt.gz",
213				EcdictURL:    "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/skywind3000/ECDICT/master/ecdict.csv",
214				AutoDownload: false, // explicit `dict update` is the blessed path
215				APIFallback:  true,  // English lookups still work before ECDICT is installed
216			},
217			SmartDict: SmartDict{
218				CloseDistance: 1,            // distance-1 typos stay "did you mean"; farther → LLM
219				Preset:        "dictionary", // gloss + example sentences
220				DefineDefault: true,         // `translate define` prefers smart-dict when a provider is up
221			},
222			TTS: TTS{
223				Enabled:       true,
224				AutoSpeak:     false, // opt-in per call via --speak / TUI ^s
225				Order:         []string{"native", "google"},
226				Foreign:       "", // "" => derive the non-Chinese side to speak
227				PreferForeign: true,
228				GoogleTTSURL:  "https://translate.google.com/translate_tts",
229				UserAgent:     "Mozilla/5.0 translate-cli",
230				TimeoutMs:     5000,
231			},
232			History: History{
233				Enabled: true,
234				Backend: "jsonl",
235			},
236		}
237	}
238	
239	// Path returns the config file path, honoring $TRANSLATE_CONFIG if set.
240	func Path() string {
241		if p := os.Getenv("TRANSLATE_CONFIG"); p != "" {
242			return p
243		}
244		return xdgpath.ConfigFile()
245	}
246	
247	// Load reads the config file. On first run (file missing) it writes Default() and
248	// returns it with created=true so the caller can hint the user to run `init`.
249	func Load() (cfg *Config, created bool, err error) {
250		p := Path()
251		b, err := os.ReadFile(p)
252		if errors.Is(err, os.ErrNotExist) {
253			cfg = Default()
254			if werr := Save(cfg); werr != nil {
255				return cfg, false, fmt.Errorf("write default config: %w", werr)
256			}
257			return cfg, true, nil
258		}
259		if err != nil {
260			return nil, false, err
261		}
262		cfg = Default() // start from defaults so omitted keys keep sane values
263		if err := toml.Unmarshal(b, cfg); err != nil {
264			return nil, false, fmt.Errorf("parse %s: %w", p, err)
265		}
266		return cfg, false, nil
267	}
268	
269	// Save writes the config atomically (temp file + rename).
270	func Save(cfg *Config) error {
271		if err := xdgpath.EnsureDirs(); err != nil {
272			return err
273		}
274		b, err := toml.Marshal(cfg)
275		if err != nil {
276			return err
277		}
278		p := Path()
279		tmp, err := os.CreateTemp(filepath.Dir(p), ".config-*.toml")
280		if err != nil {
281			return err
282		}
283		tmpName := tmp.Name()
284		defer os.Remove(tmpName) // no-op after successful rename
285		if _, err := tmp.Write(b); err != nil {
286			tmp.Close()
287			return err
288		}
289		if err := tmp.Close(); err != nil {
290			return err
291		}
292		return os.Rename(tmpName, p)
293	}
294	
295	// ProviderByName returns the named provider, or nil if absent.
296	func (c *Config) ProviderByName(name string) *Provider {
297		for i := range c.Providers {
298			if c.Providers[i].Name == name {
299				return &c.Providers[i]
300			}
301		}
302		return nil
303	}
304	
305	// ModelForTier returns the provider's model id for the given tier
306	// ("default"/"fast"/"max"), falling back to the default model.
307	func (p *Provider) ModelForTier(tier string) string {
308		switch tier {
309		case "fast":
310			if p.ModelFast != "" {
311				return p.ModelFast
312			}
313		case "max":
314			if p.ModelMax != "" {
315				return p.ModelMax
316			}
317		}
318		return p.Model
319	}
320
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>

 `./internal/config/resolve.go`

```text
1	package config
2	
3	import (
4		"os"
5		"strings"
6	)
7	
8	// Overrides carries explicit CLI-flag values (empty string == flag not set).
9	type Overrides struct {
10		Source       string
11		Target       string
12		Engine       string
13		Provider     string
14		Model        string
15		Tier         string
16		Preset       string
17		Instructions string
18		Pair         bool // --pair forces pair mode on
19		PairWith     string
20		Learn        bool // --learn forces learning mode on (implies pair)
21		Debug        bool // --debug forces verbose logging on
22	}
23	
24	// Mode identifies which front-end an invocation resolves for, selecting the
25	// [cli] or [tui] overlay (if present).
26	type Mode int
27	
28	const (
29		ModeCLI Mode = iota // one-shot: arguments or piped stdin
30		ModeTUI             // interactive Bubble Tea front-end
31	)
32	
33	// Resolved is the effective, merged view handed to the engine layer and TUI.
34	type Resolved struct {
35		Source       string // raw language string (fuzzy-resolved by the lang package)
36		Target       string
37		Engine       string // "auto" or a provider name
38		Provider     *Provider
39		Model        string
40		Tier         string
41		Preset       string
42		Instructions string
43		Pair         bool
44		PairWith     string
45		Learn        bool
46		Stream       bool
47		Color        string
48		Debug        bool
49	
50		// LiveTranslate/DebounceMs are TUI-only knobs, resolved here (through the
51		// per-mode overlay) so the front-end never reads c.General directly.
52		LiveTranslate bool
53		DebounceMs    int
54	
55		Cfg *Config
56	}
57	
58	// Precedence per setting is flag > env (TRANSLATE_*) > [cli]/[tui] overlay > config
59	// > built-in default. (Env is kept above config so a one-off `TRANSLATE_TARGET=fr`
60	// works without editing config.toml; flags still win over both.)
61	func envVal(name string) string { return strings.TrimSpace(os.Getenv(name)) }
62	
63	// overlayFor returns the per-front-end overlay for a mode, or nil when none is set.
64	func overlayFor(c *Config, mode Mode) *Overlay {
65		switch mode {
66		case ModeCLI:
67			return c.CLI
68		case ModeTUI:
69			return c.TUI
70		}
71		return nil
72	}
73	
74	// applyOverlay copies each set (non-nil) overlay field onto g. Model is not a
75	// General field; Resolve reads it from the overlay directly.
76	func applyOverlay(g *General, ov *Overlay) {
77		if ov == nil {
78			return
79		}
80		if ov.Engine != nil {
81			g.Engine = *ov.Engine
82		}
83		if ov.Tier != nil {
84			g.Tier = *ov.Tier
85		}
86		if ov.Preset != nil {
87			g.Preset = *ov.Preset
88		}
89		if ov.Instructions != nil {
90			g.Instructions = *ov.Instructions
91		}
92		if ov.DefaultTarget != nil {
93			g.DefaultTarget = *ov.DefaultTarget
94		}
95		if ov.DefaultSource != nil {
96			g.DefaultSource = *ov.DefaultSource
97		}
98		if ov.Pair != nil {
99			g.Pair = *ov.Pair
100		}
101		if ov.PairWith != nil {
102			g.PairWith = *ov.PairWith
103		}
104		if ov.Learn != nil {
105			g.Learn = *ov.Learn
106		}
107		if ov.Stream != nil {
108			g.Stream = *ov.Stream
109		}
110		if ov.LiveTranslate != nil {
111			g.LiveTranslate = *ov.LiveTranslate
112		}
113		if ov.DebounceMs != nil {
114			g.DebounceMs = *ov.DebounceMs
115		}
116		if ov.Debug != nil {
117			g.Debug = *ov.Debug
118		}
119	}
120	
121	// Resolve merges flag overrides, environment variables, the per-mode overlay, and
122	// config into the effective settings for one invocation.
123	func (c *Config) Resolve(o Overrides, mode Mode) Resolved {
124		// g is the effective [general] after the [cli]/[tui] overlay (a value copy, so
125		// the on-disk config is untouched).
126		g := c.General
127		ov := overlayFor(c, mode)
128		applyOverlay(&g, ov)
129	
130		pick := func(flag, envName, cfgVal string) string {
131			if flag != "" {
132				return flag
133			}
134			if v := envVal(envName); v != "" {
135				return v
136			}
137			return cfgVal
138		}
139	
140		r := Resolved{
141			Cfg:           c,
142			Source:        pick(o.Source, "TRANSLATE_SOURCE", g.DefaultSource),
143			Target:        pick(o.Target, "TRANSLATE_TARGET", g.DefaultTarget),
144			Engine:        pick(o.Engine, "TRANSLATE_ENGINE", g.Engine),
145			Tier:          pick(o.Tier, "TRANSLATE_TIER", g.Tier),
146			Preset:        pick(o.Preset, "TRANSLATE_PRESET", g.Preset),
147			Instructions:  pick(o.Instructions, "TRANSLATE_INSTRUCTIONS", g.Instructions),
148			Pair:          o.Pair || g.Pair || envVal("TRANSLATE_PAIR") != "",
149			PairWith:      pick(o.PairWith, "TRANSLATE_PAIR_WITH", g.PairWith),
150			Learn:         o.Learn || g.Learn || envVal("TRANSLATE_LEARN") != "",
151			Color:         g.Color,
152			Stream:        g.Stream,
153			LiveTranslate: g.LiveTranslate,
154			DebounceMs:    g.DebounceMs,
155			Debug:         o.Debug || g.Debug || envVal("TRANSLATE_DEBUG") != "",
156		}
157	
158		// Learning mode is inherently bidirectional; it reuses pair's home/away
159		// direction routing, so enabling learn enables pair.
160		if r.Learn {
161			r.Pair = true
162		}
163	
164		// Resolve which provider backs an LLM request.
165		provName := pick(o.Provider, "TRANSLATE_PROVIDER", "")
166		if provName == "" {
167			if c.ProviderByName(r.Engine) != nil {
168				provName = r.Engine // engine was itself a provider name (e.g. "copilot")
169			} else {
170				provName = c.firstLLMProvider()
171			}
172		}
173		r.Provider = c.ProviderByName(provName)
174	
175		// Resolve the model: explicit override, else the overlay's model, else the
176		// provider's tier model.
177		cfgModel := ""
178		if ov != nil && ov.Model != nil {
179			cfgModel = *ov.Model
180		}
181		r.Model = pick(o.Model, "TRANSLATE_MODEL", cfgModel)
182		if r.Model == "" && r.Provider != nil {
183			r.Model = r.Provider.ModelForTier(r.Tier)
184		}
185		return r
186	}
187	
188	// firstLLMProvider returns the first provider named in chain.Order that exists,
189	// else the first configured provider, else "".
190	func (c *Config) firstLLMProvider() string {
191		for _, name := range c.Chain.Order {
192			if c.ProviderByName(name) != nil {
193				return name
194			}
195		}
196		if len(c.Providers) > 0 {
197			return c.Providers[0].Name
198		}
199		return ""
200	}
201
```

 `./cmd/root.go`

```text
405		}
406		return sm.Code, tm.Code
407	}
408	
409	// oneShot translates text once and prints the result, returning it for history.
410	//
411	// Tokens stream live to stdout only when stdout is a TTY (so `translate x | pbcopy`
412	// stays clean) and --json was not requested. Piped output is the plain translation
413	// with no ANSI; --json emits the full structured result.
414	func oneShot(ctx context.Context, eng engine.Engine, text, source, target string, streamPref bool, preset, instructions string, pair bool, pairHome, pairAway string, learn bool) (*engine.TranslateResult, error) {
415		stdoutTTY := term.IsTerminal(int(os.Stdout.Fd()))
416		stream := streamPref && stdoutTTY && !flagJSON && !learn // learn output is structured (parsed at done)
417	
418		req := engine.Request{
419			Text:     text,
420			Source:   source,
421			Target:   target,
422			Mode:     engine.ModeTranslate,
423			Stream:   stream,
424			Preset:   preset,
425			Extra:    instructions,
426			Pair:     pair,
427			PairHome: pairHome,
428			PairAway: pairAway,
429			Learn:    learn,
430		}
431		ch, err := eng.Translate(ctx, req)
432		if err != nil {
433			return nil, err
434		}
435	
436		var onTok func(string)
437		if stream {
438			onTok = func(t string) { fmt.Print(t) }
439		}
440		res, err := engine.Drain(ch, onTok)
441		if err != nil {
442			if stream {
443				fmt.Println() // terminate any partial line before the error surfaces
444			}
445			return nil, err
446		}
447	
448		// Never downgrade silently: if the auto-chain fell back, say which engine
449		// failed and why, and how to switch.
450		for _, w := range res.Warnings {
451			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: warning: %s\n", w)
452		}
453		if len(res.Warnings) > 0 && res.Learn == nil {
454			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "translate: used %q (switch with --engine, or check the model/provider)\n", res.Engine)
455		}
456	
457		switch {
458		case flagJSON:
459			enc := json.NewEncoder(os.Stdout)
460			enc.SetIndent("", "  ")
461			if err := enc.Encode(res); err != nil {
462				return nil, err
463			}
464		case res.Learn != nil:
465			fmt.Print(renderLearnCLI(res))
466		case stream:
467			fmt.Println() // newline after the streamed tokens
468		default:
469			fmt.Println(res.Translation)
470		}
471		return res, nil
472	}
473	
474	// defaultAway picks the "away" (foreign) language for learn/pair mode when none is
475	// configured: English speakers default to learning zh-TW, everyone else to en. It
476	// mirrors the TUI ^g heuristic so the CLI and TUI agree.
477	func defaultAway(home string) string {
478		if strings.HasPrefix(strings.ToLower(home), "en") {
479			return "zh-TW"
480		}
481		return "en"
482	}
483	
484	// renderLearnCLI formats a learn-mode result as plain text (no ANSI, pipe-safe).
```

=== main.go ===
// Command translate is a fast CLI/TUI translation tool.
package main

import (
	"context"
	"fmt"
	"os"
	"os/signal"

	"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/cmd"
)

func main() {
	// Cancel in-flight work (including LLM requests) on Ctrl-C / SIGTERM.
	ctx, stop := signal.NotifyContext(context.Background(), os.Interrupt)
	defer stop()

	if err := cmd.Execute(ctx); err != nil {
		fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "translate:", err)
		os.Exit(1)
	}
}

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./README.md`

```text
1	# translate
2	
3	A fast CLI/TUI translation tool for the terminal — one-shot from the shell or an
4	interactive live-translating TUI. Backed by an auto fallback chain over your
5	local LLM providers (copilot-proxy → Ollama), a free web API (Google), and a
6	dictionary, with typo-tolerant language resolution and translation history.
7	
8	## Install
9	
10	```sh
11	go install github.com/daviddwlee84/translate@latest   # into $GOBIN (or ~/go/bin)
12	```
13	
14	## Build & run
15	
16	```sh
17	go build -o translate .      # Go 1.25+ (developed on 1.26)
18	./translate "hola mundo" --to en          # one-shot
19	echo "bonjour" | ./translate --to en      # pipe
20	./translate                               # interactive TUI
21	```
22	
23	First run writes a default config to `~/.config/translate/config.toml`; run
24	`./translate init` for a guided setup that probes which providers are up.
25	
26	## Usage
27	
28	| Command | What it does |
29	|---|---|
30	| `translate <text>` / `… \| translate` | One-shot translation (streams to a TTY; plain text when piped) |
31	| `translate` (no args, TTY) | Interactive TUI |
32	| `translate --to <lang> --from <lang>` | Language override; both are fuzzy (`chinees` → `zh`) |
33	| `translate --json` | Emit the full structured result |
34	| `translate define <word>` | Dictionary lookup (bilingual: zh↔en local, or English API) |
35	| `translate history` / `history search <q>` | Recent history / fuzzy search (`--tsv`, `--json`) |
36	| `translate init` | Interactive config wizard (probes providers) |
37	| `translate config path\|show` · `lang resolve <q>` | Introspection helpers |
38	
39	Flags: `--engine smartauto|auto|<provider>|google`, `--provider`, `--model`, `--tier default|fast|max`, `--preset concise|contextual|dictionary`, `--instructions`, `--pair`/`--pair-with`, `--no-history`, `--debug`.
40	Env overrides: `TRANSLATE_TARGET`, `TRANSLATE_SOURCE`, `TRANSLATE_ENGINE`, `TRANSLATE_PROVIDER`, `TRANSLATE_MODEL`, `TRANSLATE_CONFIG`, `TRANSLATE_DEBUG`.
41	Precedence: **flag > env > `[cli]`/`[tui]` > `[general]` > default**.
42	
43	### Per-front-end defaults (`[cli]` / `[tui]`)
44	
45	The CLI (arguments or piped stdin) and the interactive TUI share `[general]`, but
46	either can override any subset of it via an optional `[cli]` or `[tui]` table — e.g.
47	a snappy concise CLI and a richer, higher-tier TUI:
48	
49	```toml
50	[general]
51	preset = "contextual"
52	tier   = "fast"
53	
54	[cli]                 # one-shot / piped translation
55	preset = "concise"
56	
57	[tui]                 # interactive front-end
58	tier          = "max"
59	live_translate = true
60	```
61	
62	Only keys you list are overridden; the rest inherit `[general]`. Flags and env vars
63	still win over both.
64	
65	## TUI keys
66	
67	`↵` translate · live-debounce auto-translates ~700ms after you stop typing (off by default) ·
68	`^y` copy · `^l` toggle live · `^e` cycle engine · `^t` target language · `^p` model ·
69	`^o` style · `^g` toggle pair mode · `^u` clear · `⇥` switch input/output focus · `^r` history · `⌥↵` newline · `^c`/`esc` quit.
70	
71	`⇥` (Tab) moves keyboard focus between the input box and the result box; the focused
72	box gets an accent border. With the result focused, `↑/↓ j/k PgUp/PgDn g/G space` scroll
73	it; typing any character snaps focus back to the input. A mouse click focuses the box
74	it lands in.
75	
76	## Engines & fallback
77	
78	The `auto` engine tries `chain.order` in turn, failing over **before the first
79	token** (so a down provider switches cleanly; a mid-stream error surfaces rather
80	than restarting):
81	
82	- **copilot-proxy** — OpenAI-compatible at `http://localhost:4141/v1`, no API key.
83	  Default tier is **fast** (`claude-haiku-4-5`); `--tier default` → `claude-sonnet-5`,
84	  `--tier max` → `claude-opus-4-8`. Claude models are served only via the proxy's
85	  Anthropic Messages endpoint (`/v1/messages`) — the engine routes `claude-*` ids
86	  there automatically (they 400 on `/chat/completions`).
87	- **Ollama** — `http://localhost:11434/v1` (offline; `llama3.2:3b`).
88	- **Google** — free, keyless; also reports the detected source language.
89	- **openrouter** — configured but off the default chain; needs `OPENROUTER_API_KEY`.
90	
91	> ⚠️ Using a GitHub Copilot subscription to back non-GitHub tools (copilot-proxy)
92	> may violate Copilot's Terms of Service. Reorder `chain.order` (drop `copilot`,
93	> lead with `ollama`/`google`) in the config if that matters to you.
94	
95	Config lives at `~/.config/translate/config.toml`, history at
96	`~/.local/share/translate/history.jsonl`, last-pair state at
97	`~/.local/state/translate/state.json` (XDG-honored on macOS and Linux).
98	
99	### Smart auto (`smartauto`)
100	
101	The recommended default (choose it in `translate init`). It routes by input shape:
102	a **single word/term** is looked up in the dictionary (with the same LLM fallback as
103	`smart-dict` on a miss), while a **phrase or sentence** is translated by the LLM. Set
104	`engine = "smartauto"` in `[general]` (or `--engine smartauto`). It composes with
105	pair mode, so a single word and a full sentence both translate in the right direction.
106	
107	### Pair mode (bidirectional)
108	
109	With `pair = true` + `pair_with`, the tool detects which of the two languages your
110	input is in and translates it into the **other** — one box, both directions
111	(`你好 → hi`, `test → 測試`). For a CJK/non-CJK pair (e.g. `zh-TW`⇄`en`) direction is
112	decided by script, so even short input routes correctly; the LLM translate prompt is
113	also told to detect-and-route and to **never echo** the input. `pair_with` must differ
114	from the target (`translate init` enforces this; a degenerate config warns).
115	
116	### Debugging
117	
118	`--debug` (or `TRANSLATE_DEBUG=1`, or `[general] debug = true`) logs the intermediate
119	decisions — resolved settings, pair routing, word-vs-phrase classification, dictionary
120	hit/miss, and chain fallback. The one-shot CLI logs to **stderr**; the TUI logs to
121	`~/.local/state/translate/debug.log` (its alt-screen hides stderr).
122	
123	## Dictionary mode
124	
125	`^e` cycles to the dictionary engine (or use `translate define <word>`). It is an
126	**offline bilingual** dictionary that routes by script — Chinese → CC-CEDICT
127	(zh→en), English → ECDICT (en→zh):
128	
129	```sh
130	translate dict update all      # one-time ~67 MB download/build into ~/.local/share/translate/dict
131	```
132	
133	Until then, English lookups fall back to dictionaryapi.dev (`[dict] api_fallback`),
134	and Chinese lookups prompt you to run the update. Misses show a ranked "did you
135	mean" list. Set `[dict] source = "api"` to use only dictionaryapi.dev.
136	
137	### Smart dictionary (`smart-dict`)
138	
139	A distinct engine that keeps the offline dictionary fast and deterministic, but on
140	a miss — no entry, or a fuzzy match too far off — falls back to the LLM for a gloss
141	plus example sentences (never silently: the result carries a `⚠ … defined via <provider> (LLM)`
142	warning). Close typos (edit distance ≤ `[smartdict] close_distance`, default 1) still
143	show "did you mean" without calling the LLM.
144	
145	```sh
146	translate define serendipity        # exact ECDICT entry
147	translate define zzzznotaword       # miss → LLM definition (⚠ warning on stderr)
148	translate define helllo             # distance-1 typo → "did you mean: hello, …"
149	translate define --plain <word>     # force the offline dictionary, no LLM fallback
150	```
151	
152	`translate define` uses smart-dict whenever an LLM provider is reachable
153	(`[smartdict] define_default`, `--smart`/`--plain` to force); in the TUI, `^e`
154	cycles to a `smart-dict` engine alongside the plain `dictionary`.
155	
156	```toml
157	[smartdict]
158	close_distance = 1            # en edit-distance ≤ this stays "did you mean"; beyond → LLM
159	preset         = "dictionary" # LLM output style for the fallback
160	define_default = true         # `translate define` prefers smart-dict when a provider is up
161	```
162	
163	Data: **CC-CEDICT** (CC BY-SA 4.0, © Paul Andrew Denisowski / MDBG) and
164	**ECDICT** (MIT, © skywind3000).
165	
166	## Developer note — Charm v2 (`charm.land/*`)
167	
168	This project pins the **v2** Charm stack on the `charm.land/*` vanity module paths
169	(`charm.land/bubbletea/v2`, `bubbles/v2`, `lipgloss/v2`, `huh/v2`) — **not**
170	`github.com/charmbracelet/*`. v1 code will not compile here. When editing the TUI:
171	`View()` returns `tea.View` (wrap strings with `tea.NewView`); keys arrive as
172	`tea.KeyPressMsg`; alt-screen is the declarative `View.AltScreen` field; window
173	size is requested via `tea.RequestWindowSize`.
174	
175	## Architecture
176	
177	The `internal/engine` and `internal/store` layers are pure Go (no TUI imports);
178	the one-shot CLI and the Bubble Tea TUI drive the identical engine values and
179	diverge only at presentation. A single monotonic `seq` counter in the TUI drives
180	debounce-collapse, in-flight cancellation, and stale-result dropping.
181	
182	<!-- project-knowledge-harness:readme-roadmap -->
183	<!-- Snippet for project's README.md, placed near other meta sections like
184	     "Customization" or "Contributing". -->
185	
186	## Roadmap & lessons learned
187	
188	Forward-looking work — long-term ideas, deferred items, things needing
189	evaluation — lives in [`TODO.md`](TODO.md), prioritised P1 → P3 with effort
190	estimates (S/M/L/XL). Items with accompanying research, design notes, or paused
191	troubleshooting link to a corresponding [`backlog/<slug>.md`](backlog/) doc.
192	
193	Backward-looking knowledge — past traps and non-obvious debugging — lives in
194	[`pitfalls/`](pitfalls/), titled by symptom so future-you can grep the error
195	message and land on the root cause + workaround instead of re-debugging from
196	scratch.
197	<!-- project-knowledge-harness:readme-roadmap --> (end)
198
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./AGENTS.md`

```text
1	
2	## Releasing & versioning
3	
4	`translate --version` is derived from Go build info (`cmd/version.go` →
5	`debug.ReadBuildInfo`), so **the version *is* the git tag** — there is no version
6	constant to edit. `go install …@latest` and the chezmoi/ansible pin both resolve
7	to a published **tag**, not to `main`; a commit on `main` does **not** reach an
8	installed binary until it is pushed **and** tagged.
9	
10	SemVer, pre-1.0 (`0.y.z`):
11	
12	- backward-compatible feature (new mode/flag, e.g. `--learn`) → bump **minor** (`0.1.0 → 0.2.0`)
13	- bug fix only → bump **patch** (`0.2.0 → 0.2.1`)
14	- breaking CLI/flag/config change → still a minor bump while `0.x`; call it out in the tag message
15	- cut **`v1.0.0`** only once the CLI/flags/config surface is considered stable
16	
17	To ship the current `main` to this machine via chezmoi:
18	
19	1. `go test ./...` green, working tree clean.
20	2. `git push origin main`
21	3. `git tag -a vX.Y.Z -m "<highlights>"` && `git push origin vX.Y.Z`
22	4. verify: `go install github.com/daviddwlee84/translate@vX.Y.Z && translate --version` → `vX.Y.Z`
23	5. install on this machine (dotfiles repo): run `just upgrade-go` — it installs each go
24	   tool at `@latest` (= the new tag) into `~/.local/bin`. The `go_tools` pin in
25	   `dot_ansible/roles/go_tools/defaults/main.yml` is only the *fresh-install floor*
26	   (its header says **don't** bump it for upgrades); `chezmoi apply`/ansible is
27	   install-only and won't move an already-installed binary. Make sure no stale copy
28	   shadows `~/.local/bin` earlier on `PATH` (e.g. a hand-built one in `~/.dotfiles/bin`).
29	
30	There is no CHANGELOG; the annotated tag message is the release note. Keep commit
31	subjects in `feat(scope): …` / `fix(scope): …` form so `git log <prev-tag>..<tag>`
32	reads as a coherent changelog.
33	
34	<!-- project-knowledge-harness:agent-guidance -->
35	<!-- Snippet for the project's agent contract file (AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md /
36	     similar). The bundled scripts/init.sh appends this between sentinel
37	     markers; safe to re-run. -->
38	
39	### Long-term backlog → `TODO.md` + `backlog/`
40	
41	When the user surfaces an idea explicitly **not** being implemented this
42	session (signals: "maybe later", "nice to have", "if I'm interested",
43	"工程量太大需要再評估", "先記下來"), add an entry to [`TODO.md`](TODO.md) using
44	the priority + effort tag schema. Do **not** create new `ROADMAP.md` /
45	`IDEAS.md` / `BACKLOG.md` files — `TODO.md` is the single index.
46	
47	The bundled `scripts/todo-kanban.sh` validates the format. Run it
48	(`scripts/todo-kanban.sh --validate-only TODO.md`) after editing so syntax
49	drift is caught immediately.
50	
51	#### Three ways to add a TODO entry (preferred order)
52	
53	1. **Structured CLI — `scripts/add-todo.sh`** (default):
54	
55	   ```
56	   scripts/add-todo.sh --priority P3 --effort M \
57	     --title "Title" --description "Description"
58	   ```
59	
60	   Inserts a canonically-formatted line into the right `## P*` lane and
61	   re-runs the validator. Add `--backlog` to also scaffold
62	   `backlog/<slug>.md` from the bundled template.
63	
64	2. **Quick capture — `backlog/inbox.md`** (when priority/effort unclear):
65	
66	   ```
67	   echo "- maybe add docs versioning with mike" >> backlog/inbox.md
68	   ```
69	
70	   When the user asks "sweep the inbox", run
71	   `scripts/sweep-inbox.sh`. It prompts for the missing fields per loose
72	   line and calls `add-todo.sh`. Use `--batch` for non-interactive runs
73	   that only formalize lines with parseable `key=value` pairs.
74	
75	3. **Direct edit of `TODO.md`** — fine if the format is fresh; run
76	   `scripts/todo-kanban.sh --validate-only` afterwards.
77	
78	Add a `backlog/<slug>.md` companion doc when the item meets any of:
79	
80	- carries a `P?` tag (record what was tried so it doesn't need re-investigation)
81	- captures a paused troubleshooting session that you intend to fix later
82	  (preserve the error trace + root cause analysis before context evaporates)
83	- weighs multiple options (record trade-offs, not only the winner)
84	- is `[L]` or `[XL]` (architectural; needs design before code)
85	
86	`[S]` items rarely need a backlog doc — a file path in the `TODO.md` line is
87	usually enough. See [`backlog/README.md`](backlog/README.md) for the full
88	template and "when to add a doc" rules.
89	
90	When implementing a `TODO.md` item, in the same commit:
91	
92	1. Run `scripts/promote-todo.sh --title "<substring>" --summary "<what shipped>"`
93	   to move the entry into `## Done` with the dated syntax and re-validate.
94	2. Mark the corresponding `backlog/<slug>.md` (if any) `Status: shipped`
95	   and keep it as a historical record (don't delete — future-you may
96	   revisit adjacent decisions).
97	
98	`backlog/` is excluded from N/A (no packaging — these files stay in the repo) (see N/A); it
99	is repo metadata for maintainers, not user-facing config to deploy.
100	
101	### Past pitfalls → `pitfalls/`
102	
103	When you spend more than ~15 minutes debugging something that wasn't
104	googleable and the fix is non-obvious, write a `pitfalls/<slug>.md`
105	capturing:
106	
107	1. **Verbatim symptom** — copy-paste error messages exactly, do not
108	   paraphrase (preserves grep-ability for future-you / future agent)
109	2. **Root cause** — why this happens (with source / docs / upstream issue link)
110	3. **Workaround** — copy-pasteable commands or config diff
111	4. **Prevention** — how to avoid stepping on this again
112	
113	Title the doc by the **symptom**, not the root cause (you'll search by what
114	you're seeing, not by what you eventually learned). See
115	[`pitfalls/README.md`](pitfalls/README.md) for the full template and
116	when-to-add rules.
117	
118	**Pitfall vs Hard invariant**: a pitfall *graduates* to a Hard invariant in
119	this file when it (a) recurs across machines/agents/sessions despite being
120	documented, (b) silently corrupts state, or (c) the workaround is non-obvious
121	enough that "remember to do X" isn't safe. When graduating, leave the
122	`pitfalls/<slug>.md` as historical record and link to it from the new
123	invariant.
124	
125	`pitfalls/` is excluded from N/A (no packaging — these files stay in the repo) (see N/A) and
126	**not** auto-redacted; review for secrets before committing.
127	<!-- project-knowledge-harness:agent-guidance --> (end)
128
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./TODO.md`

```text
1	# TODO
2	
3	Long-term backlog for translate. See AGENTS.md
4	for the maintenance workflow that agents should follow.
5	
6	> **For agents**: when the user surfaces an idea explicitly **not** being
7	> implemented this session (signals: "maybe later", "nice to have",
8	> "工程量太大需要再評估", "先記下來"), add it here with priority + effort tags.
9	> Do not create new `ROADMAP.md` / `IDEAS.md` / `BACKLOG.md` files —
10	> `TODO.md` is the single backlog index. Long-form research goes in
11	> [`backlog/<slug>.md`](backlog/).
12	
13	<!-- Use the exact section order: P1, P2, P3, P?, Done.
14	     The bundled scripts/todo-kanban.sh validator only inspects top-level
15	     `- [ ]` and `- ✅` items inside these sections. Prose paragraphs,
16	     blockquotes, indented sub-bullets, HTML comments, and `---` rules are
17	     ignored — feel free to add inline guidance like this without breaking
18	     machine readability. -->
19	
20	## P1
21	
22	Likely next batch — items you'd reach for if you sat down to work today.
23	
24	## P2
25	
26	Worth doing, no rush.
27	
28	- [ ] **[S] Align `just install` with `go install` / `~/.local/bin`** — the `install` recipe copies the binary to `~/.dotfiles/bin` (PATH position 11), which **shadows** the `go install` location `~/.local/bin` (position 13); a stale `just install` copy would win silently. Point the recipe at `GOBIN=$HOME/.local/bin go install .` (or drop it) so both paths agree. See [pitfalls/duplicate-translate-on-path-dotfiles-bin-shadows-local-bin.md](pitfalls/duplicate-translate-on-path-dotfiles-bin-shadows-local-bin.md).
29	
30	## P3
31	
32	Someday / nice-to-have.
33	
34	- [ ] **[M] lingua-go detection upgrade behind the `Detector` seam** — whatlanggo is light/fast but weak on short and mixed-script text; lingua-go is more accurate but heavy (embedded n-gram models → bigger binary, slower init). Swap behind the existing `internal/lang` interface only if short-text detection proves insufficient. Detection is mostly a fallback (Google returns the source, LLM returns `DetectedSource`), so keeping it light is defensible.
35	- [ ] **[S] MyMemory fallback engine** — trivial flat JSON (`responseData.translatedText`, `matches[]`); wire as an easy secondary free path after Google. Limits: 5k chars/day anon (50k with `&de=email`), 500 B max per `q`, no source auto-detect.
36	- [ ] **[M] Azure Translator engine behind the engine seam** — the only supported Microsoft path (needs an Azure subscription key). Keyless Bing scraping (`ttranslatev3` + transient `IG`/`IID`/token) is too fragile/ToS-risky for v1. Leave the engine interface ready; add when a key is available.
37	- [ ] **[S] Drop the whole-request `http.Client{Timeout: 60s}` on the streaming path** — in `internal/engine/llm.go`, `NewLLM` sets a client-wide `Timeout` that Go applies to the *entire* request including reading the streamed body, so a long translation is cut at 60s mid-stream. Now surfaced as `⚠ truncated` (see [pitfalls/llm-stream-truncation-silently-rendered-as-complete.md](pitfalls/llm-stream-truncation-silently-rendered-as-complete.md)) instead of silently accepted, but the long output still can't finish. Fix by relying on `ctx` deadlines for the streaming `Do()` (keep the short timeout only for `Available`/`Models` probes).
38	
39	## P?
40	
41	Needs a spike before committing to a real priority. Tag as `[?/Effort]`.
42	
43	- [ ] **[?/L] Ship prebuilt release binaries (goreleaser + GitHub Releases)** — cross-compile per OS/arch (pure-Go, no cgo — trivial) so hosts without a Go toolchain install via chezmoi `.chezmoiexternal` with a templated `{{ .chezmoi.os }}/{{ .chezmoi.arch }}` URL instead of `go install`. Also unlocks shipping the dictionary DB as a release asset. → [research](backlog/release-binaries.md)
44	- [ ] **[?/M] Bundle or prebuild the dictionary vs the 67 MB runtime `dict update`** — evaluate embedding a trimmed DB via `go:embed`, or shipping the built ECDICT sqlite + CC-CEDICT as release assets, so first run isn't a big download/build. Weigh binary-size blowup vs first-run friction. → [research](backlog/dict-bundling.md)
45	
46	## Done
47	
48	- ✅ [2026-07-09] [P?/L] Wire `translate` into chezmoi dotfiles as an auto-installed go tool — go_tools ansible role added to dotfiles (commit 306bfb0): go install → ~/.local/bin, mise-gated, + cat_go upgrade path. Pending chezmoi apply on normal cadence.
49	
50	Recently shipped. When implementing an active item, in the same commit run:
51	
52	```
53	scripts/promote-todo.sh --title "<substring>" --summary "<one-line shipped summary>"
54	```
55	
56	This moves the entry here using the dated `Done` syntax and re-validates.
57	
58	- ✅ [2026-07-10] [P1/M] Publish as a public repo + `go install` — renamed the module `translate` → `github.com/daviddwlee84/translate`, rewrote 22 internal imports, tagged `v0.1.0`, and confirmed `go install github.com/daviddwlee84/translate@latest` installs into `~/.local/bin` (GOBIN-pinned, XDG-clean, stable across mise Go upgrades).
59	
60	<!-- Prune older entries into CHANGELOG.md once prior-year items appear here
61	     or this section grows past ~20 entries. -->
62
```

 `./backlog/README.md`

```text
1	# Backlog Research
2	
3	Long-form research, design notes, and paused troubleshooting for items listed in
4	[`../TODO.md`](../TODO.md). One file per topic, named with a slug that matches
5	the TODO entry.
6	
7	## Why this exists
8	
9	`TODO.md` is the **index** — short titles grouped by priority section with
10	effort tags. This folder
11	is the **knowledge base** — the actual investigation, options considered,
12	benchmarks, error messages, and decisions that informed the TODO entry. The goal
13	is **resume-friendliness**: when you (or an agent) come back to an item three
14	months later, the doc here lets you pick up in 5 minutes instead of re-running
15	30 minutes of investigation.
16	
17	This folder is maintainer-facing repo metadata. It lives in the git repo but
18	never reaches users — `go install` / `go build` compile only Go sources, so it
19	has no effect on the built binary.
20	
21	## When to add a doc here
22	
23	Add a `backlog/<slug>.md` file when **any** of these apply:
24	
25	- The TODO item carries a `P?` tag (it requires evaluation; record what was tried)
26	- You did meaningful troubleshooting but didn't ship a fix (capture the trace
27	  before it evaporates)
28	- Multiple options were considered (record the trade-offs, not just the winner)
29	- An external blocker exists (waiting on upstream release, host availability)
30	- The implementation is `[L]` or `[XL]` (architectural; needs design before code)
31	
32	`[S]` items rarely need a doc unless there's surprising context.
33	
34	## When NOT to add a doc here
35	
36	- Item is `[S]` and obvious — just put the file path in the TODO entry
37	- Already covered by an existing doc page (cross-link instead — that's
38	  user-facing reference, this is maintainer-facing speculation)
39	- Speculation only ("would be cool to...") with no investigation — keep it as a
40	  one-liner in `TODO.md` first; promote to a doc when you actually look into it
41	
42	## File template
43	
44	See [`backlog-doc.md.template`] in the skill's `assets/` for the per-doc
45	template, or copy the structure of an existing entry below.
46	
47	## Index
48	
49	Add new entries here as you create them. Keep alphabetical.
50	
51	| Slug | Status | TODO entry |
52	|---|---|---|
53	| `chezmoi-go-tool-integration` | spike done, paused on scope (2026-07) | P? "Wire `translate` into chezmoi…" |
54	| `dict-bundling` | deferred (2026-07) | P? "Bundle or prebuild the dictionary…" |
55	| `release-binaries` | deferred (2026-07) | P? "Ship prebuilt release binaries…" |
56
```
</details></tool-use>
