<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./pitfalls/README.md`

```text
1	# Pitfalls
2	
3	Past traps we've stepped on. **Symptoms-first** knowledge base — when a
4	problem recurs (on a new machine, after an upgrade, with a new tool combo),
5	grepping the symptom here lands you on the root cause and workaround in
6	seconds, instead of re-debugging from scratch.
7	
8	This folder is maintainer-facing repo metadata. It lives in the git repo but
9	never reaches users — `go install` / `go build` compile only Go sources, so
10	`TODO.md`, `backlog/`, and `pitfalls/` have no effect on the built binary.
11	
12	## Pitfalls vs the rest
13	
14	| Surface | Time direction | Question it answers | Access pattern |
15	|---|---|---|---|
16	| `docs/<tool>.md` | Present | "How does this tool work / how do I configure it?" | Read top to bottom |
17	| `pitfalls/<slug>.md` | **Past** | **"I see error X — has this happened before?"** | **Grep symptoms** |
18	| `backlog/<slug>.md` | Future | "We thought about doing Y — what was the analysis?" | Index in `TODO.md` |
19	| `AGENTS.md` Hard invariants | Present | "What rules MUST agents follow?" | Read top to bottom |
20	
21	A pitfall **graduates** to a Hard invariant when the trap is serious enough
22	that you can't rely on memory or grep — typically when (a) it recurs across
23	machines, (b) it silently corrupts state, or (c) the workaround is non-obvious
24	and easy to undo by accident. When graduating, leave a `pitfalls/<slug>.md`
25	as historical record and link to it from the new invariant.
26	
27	## When to add a pitfall doc
28	
29	Add `pitfalls/<slug>.md` when you've spent more than ~15 minutes on something
30	that wasn't googleable, AND any of:
31	
32	- The symptom is non-obvious from the root cause (silent state, weird side
33	  effect, behaviour change without error)
34	- The fix is "do nothing different but in a specific order"
35	- The same trap could be hit by a new agent / new machine / new contributor
36	- An upstream bug exists with no ETA — workaround needs to outlive memory
37	- A specific tool version is required (or forbidden) and failure at the
38	  wrong version is silent / confusing
39	
40	## When NOT to add a pitfall doc
41	
42	- Trivially googleable (next person solves in 30 seconds)
43	- Already covered in `docs/<tool>.md` — cross-link from this README's
44	  "Cross-referenced pitfalls" table below instead of duplicating
45	- Already a Hard invariant (cross-link only)
46	- One-off transient (network glitch, machine-specific config rot)
47	
48	## File template
49	
50	See [`pitfall-doc.md.template`] in the
51	[`project-knowledge-harness` skill](https://github.com/daviddwlee84/agent-skills/tree/main/skills/local/project-knowledge-harness)
52	for the per-doc template.
53	
54	Key sections (different from `backlog/` template — symptom-first, not
55	context-first):
56	
57	```markdown
58	# <Title describing the SYMPTOM, not the root cause>
59	
60	**Symptoms** (grep this section): <verbatim error messages, observable behaviour>
61	**First seen**: YYYY-MM
62	**Affects**: <tool/version/OS combo>
63	**Status**: workaround documented / fixed upstream in vX.Y / WONTFIX
64	
65	## Symptom
66	
67	Full error messages (verbatim — preserves grep-ability).
68	
69	## Root cause
70	
71	Why this happens, with source/docs/upstream issue link.
72	
73	## Workaround
74	
75	Copy-pasteable commands or config diff.
76	
77	## Prevention
78	
79	How to avoid stepping on this again.
80	
81	## Related
82	
83	Links to docs, sibling pitfalls, TODO entries, upstream issues.
84	```
85	
86	## Index
87	
88	Pitfalls owned by this folder. Keep alphabetical.
89	
90	| Slug | Symptom keywords | Status |
91	|---|---|---|
92	| `duplicate-translate-on-path-dotfiles-bin-shadows-local-bin` | two `translate` on PATH, `command -v -a`, reinstall has no effect, `~/.dotfiles/bin` shadows `~/.local/bin` | workaround; fix in TODO P2 |
93	| `go-install-module-path-mismatch` | `module declares its path as: translate`, `but was required as`, `parsing go.mod` | fixed (module renamed) |
94	| `gobin-points-at-mise-toolchain-dir` | binary vanishes after Go upgrade, `go env GOBIN` = `.../mise/installs/go/<ver>/bin` | workaround (pin GOBIN) |
95	| `llm-stream-truncation-silently-rendered-as-complete` | translation cut mid-word / half output, no error, `detected:` line still shown, streamed result truncated, copilot-proxy SSE dropped | fixed (assert stream completeness) |
96	| `tui-viewport-clips-long-translation-no-softwrap` | TUI translation cut mid-sentence but CLI/curl shows full text, no `⚠`, long/multi-line results clipped, viewport SoftWrap | fixed (SoftWrap=true) |
97	
98	## Cross-referenced pitfalls (still in their original homes)
99	
100	These traps are documented elsewhere and aren't duplicated here — the table
101	exists so grepping `pitfalls/` still finds them. Move into this folder only
102	if their original location stops being a natural reading flow.
103	
104	| Trap | Lives in | Why not here |
105	|---|---|---|
106	| (example: Tool X version Y bug) | `docs/tool-x.md` → "Known issues" | Already part of the tool's normal config narrative |
107
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/engine/engine.go`

```text
1	// Package engine defines the translation backend abstraction and its
2	// implementations (LLM via OpenAI-compatible APIs, free web APIs, dictionaries).
3	//
4	// The engine layer is pure Go with context.Context and imports zero TUI code:
5	// the one-shot CLI and the Bubble Tea TUI drive the identical Engine values and
6	// diverge only at presentation.
7	package engine
8	
9	import "context"
10	
11	// Mode selects what kind of lookup a Request performs.
12	type Mode int
13	
14	const (
15		// ModeTranslate is register-aware, faithful translation (the default).
16		ModeTranslate Mode = iota
17		// ModeDict is a dictionary/definition lookup.
18		ModeDict
19	)
20	
21	// Request is the single input to every engine. A Source of "" or "auto" asks
22	// the engine to detect the source language.
23	type Request struct {
24		Text    string
25		Source  string // BCP-47-ish code or "auto"/""
26		Target  string // e.g. "en", "zh"
27		Mode    Mode
28		MaxAlts int    // cap on Alternatives; 0 => engine default
29		Stream  bool   // caller wants token streaming (LLM only; others ignore)
30		Model   string // optional per-request model override (LLM engines)
31		// ModelProvider scopes Model to one provider by name; other engines ignore
32		// the override (so a copilot model id never leaks to an Ollama fallback).
33		ModelProvider string
34		Preset        string // LLM prompt style: "" => concise (LLM translate only)
35		Extra         string // extra user instructions appended to the system prompt
36	
37		// Pair marks a bidirectional "pair" request: the text is written in one of
38		// two languages (PairHome/PairAway) and must be translated into the OTHER.
39		// The LLM translate prompt uses this to detect-and-route and, critically, to
40		// never echo the input unchanged. Target still carries the caller's routed
41		// best guess (for history/display and non-LLM engines).
42		Pair     bool
43		PairHome string // one pair language (e.g. the home target, "zh-TW")
44		PairAway string // the other pair language (e.g. "en")
45	
46		// Learn marks a language-learning request. It is bidirectional (implies Pair,
47		// with PairHome=native and PairAway=foreign) and adaptive: native-language input
48		// is translated-and-taught (glosses + examples), foreign-language input is
49		// grammar-corrected-and-explained. The LLM engine answers with a strict JSON
50		// object (parsed into TranslateResult.Learn) and, because the output is
51		// structured, always runs NON-STREAMING regardless of Stream.
52		Learn bool
53	}
54	
55	// TranslateResult is the "Marvin-lite" typed result. Every engine fills what it
56	// can; zero-value fields are simply absent. It is also the history record shape.
57	type TranslateResult struct {
58		Translation    string   `json:"translation"`
59		DetectedSource string   `json:"detected_source,omitempty"`
60		Target         string   `json:"target"`
61		Alternatives   []string `json:"alternatives,omitempty"`
62		Notes          string   `json:"notes,omitempty"`
63		Confidence     float64  `json:"confidence,omitempty"`
64	
65		// Warnings records non-fatal problems, e.g. a chain engine that failed before
66		// this (fallback) engine served the result — so a downgrade is never silent.
67		Warnings []string `json:"warnings,omitempty"`
68	
69		// Truncated is set when a streamed result ended before the model finished
70		// (no terminal marker, or stop_reason=="max_tokens"). The partial text is
71		// still returned so the caller can show it, but it must not be cached or
72		// persisted as if complete. Transient: not serialized.
73		Truncated bool `json:"-"`
74	
75		// Provenance — stamped by the producing engine / chain.
76		Engine string `json:"engine,omitempty"`
77		Model  string `json:"model,omitempty"`
78	
79		// Dictionary payload (Mode == ModeDict); nil for translate mode.
80		Dictionary *DictEntry `json:"dictionary,omitempty"`
81		// Learn payload (Request.Learn); nil outside learn mode. Translation still holds
82		// the main foreign-language sentence (the corrected sentence, or the translation)
83		// so copy/history/speak keep working unchanged; the structured extras ride here.
84		Learn *LearnResult `json:"learn,omitempty"`
85		// Suggestions is a ranked "did you mean" list, set by the dictionary engine
86		// when no exact entry was found (Dictionary and Translation stay empty). The
87		// frontend decides how to present/select — the engine no longer auto-picks.
88		Suggestions []string `json:"suggestions,omitempty"`
89	
90		// SuggestDistance is the edit distance of the best entry in Suggestions (English
91		// fuzzy lookups only; 0 when unknown/not applicable, e.g. Chinese prefix matches).
92		// The smart-dict engine reads it to tell a likely typo from a match too far off.
93		// Transient: not serialized.
94		SuggestDistance int `json:"-"`
95	}
96	
97	// DictEntry is a dictionary lookup payload (populated by the dictionary engine).
98	type DictEntry struct {
99		Word      string    `json:"word"`
100		Phonetic  string    `json:"phonetic,omitempty"`
101		Meanings  []Meaning `json:"meanings,omitempty"`
102		SourceURL string    `json:"source_url,omitempty"`
103	}
104	
105	// Meaning groups definitions by part of speech.
106	type Meaning struct {
107		PartOfSpeech string       `json:"part_of_speech"`
108		Definitions  []Definition `json:"definitions"`
109		Synonyms     []string     `json:"synonyms,omitempty"`
110		Antonyms     []string     `json:"antonyms,omitempty"`
111	}
112	
113	// Definition is a single sense of a word.
114	type Definition struct {
115		Text    string `json:"definition"`
116		Example string `json:"example,omitempty"`
117	}
118	
119	// LearnResult is the structured payload for learn/tutor mode (Request.Learn). The
120	// model fills only the fields relevant to the auto-detected direction; all glosses,
121	// explanations, and notes are written in the native (home) language.
122	type LearnResult struct {
123		Direction   string         `json:"direction"`           // "teach" (native→foreign) | "correct" (foreign→native)
124		Original    string         `json:"original"`            // the user's input as received
125		Corrected   string         `json:"corrected,omitempty"` // correct-direction: the fixed foreign sentence
126		Translation string         `json:"translation"`         // teach: foreign translation; correct: native translation of intent
127		Notes       string         `json:"notes,omitempty"`     // short tip/encouragement, in the NATIVE language
128		Issues      []LearnIssue   `json:"issues,omitempty"`    // correct-direction grammar/usage fixes
129		Vocab       []LearnGloss   `json:"vocab,omitempty"`     // teach-direction glosses
130		Examples    []LearnExample `json:"examples,omitempty"`  // teach-direction usage examples
131	}
132	
133	// LearnIssue is one grammar/usage correction (correct-direction).
134	type LearnIssue struct {
135		Span        string `json:"span"`        // the problematic fragment of the input
136		Fix         string `json:"fix"`         // the corrected fragment
137		Explanation string `json:"explanation"` // why — in the NATIVE language
138	}
139	
140	// LearnGloss is one vocabulary explanation (teach-direction).
141	type LearnGloss struct {
142		Term     string `json:"term"`               // foreign word/term
143		Pos      string `json:"pos,omitempty"`      // part of speech
144		Phonetic string `json:"phonetic,omitempty"` // KK/IPA for English, pinyin for Chinese
145		Meaning  string `json:"meaning"`            // meaning in the NATIVE language
146	}
147	
148	// LearnExample is one usage example (teach-direction).
149	type LearnExample struct {
150		Foreign string `json:"foreign"` // example sentence in the foreign language
151		Native  string `json:"native"`  // its native-language translation
152	}
153	
154	// ChunkKind distinguishes streamed tokens from the terminal result/error.
155	type ChunkKind int
156	
157	const (
158		// ChunkToken is one streamed unit of translation text.
159		ChunkToken ChunkKind = iota
160		// ChunkDone carries the final structured result and ends the stream.
161		ChunkDone
162		// ChunkError carries a terminal error and ends the stream.
163		ChunkError
164	)
165	
166	// Chunk is one unit emitted on an engine's result channel.
167	type Chunk struct {
168		Kind   ChunkKind
169		Text   string           // token text (ChunkToken)
170		Result *TranslateResult // final result (ChunkDone)
171		Err    error            // terminal error (ChunkError)
172	}
173	
174	// Engine is the provider abstraction. Every method honors ctx cancellation.
175	//
176	// Translate returns a receive-only channel and MUST close it after sending
177	// exactly one terminal Chunk (ChunkDone or ChunkError). Streaming engines emit
178	// zero or more ChunkToken then one ChunkDone; non-streaming engines emit a
179	// single ChunkDone. This uniform "channel closed == finished" contract lets the
180	// TUI's self-resubscribing reader and the CLI's drain loop share one code path.
181	// The synchronous error return is only for immediate setup failures; all
182	// runtime/network errors flow as a terminal ChunkError.
183	type Engine interface {
184		Name() string
185		Translate(ctx context.Context, req Request) (<-chan Chunk, error)
186		Detect(ctx context.Context, text string) (string, error)
187		Available(ctx context.Context) bool
188		Supports(m Mode) bool
189	}
190	
191	// single is a helper for non-streaming engines: it returns a closed channel
192	// carrying exactly one terminal Chunk.
193	func single(res *TranslateResult, err error) <-chan Chunk {
194		ch := make(chan Chunk, 1)
195		if err != nil {
196			ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: err}
197		} else {
198			ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkDone, Result: res}
199		}
200		close(ch)
201		return ch
202	}
203	
204	// Drain consumes an engine channel to completion, returning the final result.
205	// It streams tokens to onToken (if non-nil) as they arrive. Used by the
206	// one-shot CLI path; the TUI drives the channel directly via its Update loop.
207	func Drain(ch <-chan Chunk, onToken func(string)) (*TranslateResult, error) {
208		for c := range ch {
209			switch c.Kind {
210			case ChunkToken:
211				if onToken != nil {
212					onToken(c.Text)
213				}
214			case ChunkDone:
215				return c.Result, nil
216			case ChunkError:
217				return nil, c.Err
218			}
219		}
220		return nil, ErrNoResult
221	}
222
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-20 05:05:53Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/tui/styles.go`

```text
1	package tui
2	
3	import lg "charm.land/lipgloss/v2"
4	
5	// palette (fixed for now; light/dark adaptivity is a later slice).
6	var (
7		colAccent = lg.Color("#7D56F4")
8		colDim    = lg.Color("#6C6C6C")
9		colErr    = lg.Color("#FF5F87")
10		colWarn   = lg.Color("#E0A500")
11		colOK     = lg.Color("#5FD787")
12		colText   = lg.Color("#D0D0D0")
13	)
14	
15	type styles struct {
16		input    lg.Style
17		inputHi  lg.Style
18		result   lg.Style
19		resultHi lg.Style
20		footer   lg.Style
21		label    lg.Style
22		trans    lg.Style
23		alt      lg.Style
24		notes    lg.Style
25		warn     lg.Style
26		errText  lg.Style
27		dim      lg.Style
28		liveOn   lg.Style
29		liveOff  lg.Style
30	}
31	
32	func newStyles() styles {
33		box := lg.NewStyle().Border(lg.RoundedBorder()).Padding(0, 1)
34		return styles{
35			input:    box.BorderForeground(colDim),
36			inputHi:  box.BorderForeground(colAccent),
37			result:   box.BorderForeground(colDim),
38			resultHi: box.BorderForeground(colAccent),
39			footer:   lg.NewStyle().Foreground(colDim),
40			label:    lg.NewStyle().Foreground(colAccent).Bold(true),
41			trans:    lg.NewStyle().Foreground(colText).Bold(true),
42			alt:      lg.NewStyle().Foreground(colDim),
43			notes:    lg.NewStyle().Foreground(colDim).Italic(true),
44			warn:     lg.NewStyle().Foreground(colWarn),
45			errText:  lg.NewStyle().Foreground(colErr),
46			dim:      lg.NewStyle().Foreground(colDim),
47			liveOn:   lg.NewStyle().Foreground(colOK),
48			liveOff:  lg.NewStyle().Foreground(colDim),
49		}
50	}
51
```

 `./AGENTS.md`

```text
1	
2	## Releasing & versioning
3	
4	`translate --version` is derived from Go build info (`cmd/version.go` →
5	`debug.ReadBuildInfo`), so **the version *is* the git tag** — there is no version
6	constant to edit. `go install …@latest` and the chezmoi/ansible pin both resolve
7	to a published **tag**, not to `main`; a commit on `main` does **not** reach an
8	installed binary until it is pushed **and** tagged.
9	
10	SemVer, pre-1.0 (`0.y.z`):
11	
12	- backward-compatible feature (new mode/flag, e.g. `--learn`) → bump **minor** (`0.1.0 → 0.2.0`)
13	- bug fix only → bump **patch** (`0.2.0 → 0.2.1`)
14	- breaking CLI/flag/config change → still a minor bump while `0.x`; call it out in the tag message
15	- cut **`v1.0.0`** only once the CLI/flags/config surface is considered stable
16	
17	To ship the current `main` to this machine via chezmoi:
18	
19	1. `go test ./...` green, working tree clean.
20	2. `git push origin main`
21	3. `git tag -a vX.Y.Z -m "<highlights>"` && `git push origin vX.Y.Z`
22	4. verify: `go install github.com/daviddwlee84/translate@vX.Y.Z && translate --version` → `vX.Y.Z`
23	5. install on this machine (dotfiles repo): run `just upgrade-go` — it installs each go
24	   tool at `@latest` (= the new tag) into `~/.local/bin`. The `go_tools` pin in
25	   `dot_ansible/roles/go_tools/defaults/main.yml` is only the *fresh-install floor*
26	   (its header says **don't** bump it for upgrades); `chezmoi apply`/ansible is
27	   install-only and won't move an already-installed binary. Make sure no stale copy
28	   shadows `~/.local/bin` earlier on `PATH` (e.g. a hand-built one in `~/.dotfiles/bin`).
29	
30	There is no CHANGELOG; the annotated tag message is the release note. Keep commit
31	subjects in `feat(scope): …` / `fix(scope): …` form so `git log <prev-tag>..<tag>`
32	reads as a coherent changelog.
33	
34	<!-- project-knowledge-harness:agent-guidance -->
35	<!-- Snippet for the project's agent contract file (AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md /
36	     similar). The bundled scripts/init.sh appends this between sentinel
37	     markers; safe to re-run. -->
38	
39	### Long-term backlog → `TODO.md` + `backlog/`
40	
41	When the user surfaces an idea explicitly **not** being implemented this
42	session (signals: "maybe later", "nice to have", "if I'm interested",
43	"工程量太大需要再評估", "先記下來"), add an entry to [`TODO.md`](TODO.md) using
44	the priority + effort tag schema. Do **not** create new `ROADMAP.md` /
45	`IDEAS.md` / `BACKLOG.md` files — `TODO.md` is the single index.
46	
47	The bundled `scripts/todo-kanban.sh` validates the format. Run it
48	(`scripts/todo-kanban.sh --validate-only TODO.md`) after editing so syntax
49	drift is caught immediately.
50	
51	#### Three ways to add a TODO entry (preferred order)
52	
53	1. **Structured CLI — `scripts/add-todo.sh`** (default):
54	
55	   ```
56	   scripts/add-todo.sh --priority P3 --effort M \
57	     --title "Title" --description "Description"
58	   ```
59	
60	   Inserts a canonically-formatted line into the right `## P*` lane and
61	   re-runs the validator. Add `--backlog` to also scaffold
62	   `backlog/<slug>.md` from the bundled template.
63	
64	2. **Quick capture — `backlog/inbox.md`** (when priority/effort unclear):
65	
66	   ```
67	   echo "- maybe add docs versioning with mike" >> backlog/inbox.md
68	   ```
69	
70	   When the user asks "sweep the inbox", run
71	   `scripts/sweep-inbox.sh`. It prompts for the missing fields per loose
72	   line and calls `add-todo.sh`. Use `--batch` for non-interactive runs
73	   that only formalize lines with parseable `key=value` pairs.
74	
75	3. **Direct edit of `TODO.md`** — fine if the format is fresh; run
76	   `scripts/todo-kanban.sh --validate-only` afterwards.
77	
78	Add a `backlog/<slug>.md` companion doc when the item meets any of:
79	
80	- carries a `P?` tag (record what was tried so it doesn't need re-investigation)
81	- captures a paused troubleshooting session that you intend to fix later
82	  (preserve the error trace + root cause analysis before context evaporates)
83	- weighs multiple options (record trade-offs, not only the winner)
84	- is `[L]` or `[XL]` (architectural; needs design before code)
85	
86	`[S]` items rarely need a backlog doc — a file path in the `TODO.md` line is
87	usually enough. See [`backlog/README.md`](backlog/README.md) for the full
88	template and "when to add a doc" rules.
89	
90	When implementing a `TODO.md` item, in the same commit:
91	
92	1. Run `scripts/promote-todo.sh --title "<substring>" --summary "<what shipped>"`
93	   to move the entry into `## Done` with the dated syntax and re-validate.
94	2. Mark the corresponding `backlog/<slug>.md` (if any) `Status: shipped`
95	   and keep it as a historical record (don't delete — future-you may
96	   revisit adjacent decisions).
97	
98	`backlog/` is excluded from N/A (no packaging — these files stay in the repo) (see N/A); it
99	is repo metadata for maintainers, not user-facing config to deploy.
100	
101	### Past pitfalls → `pitfalls/`
102	
103	When you spend more than ~15 minutes debugging something that wasn't
104	googleable and the fix is non-obvious, write a `pitfalls/<slug>.md`
105	capturing:
106	
107	1. **Verbatim symptom** — copy-paste error messages exactly, do not
108	   paraphrase (preserves grep-ability for future-you / future agent)
109	2. **Root cause** — why this happens (with source / docs / upstream issue link)
110	3. **Workaround** — copy-pasteable commands or config diff
111	4. **Prevention** — how to avoid stepping on this again
112	
113	Title the doc by the **symptom**, not the root cause (you'll search by what
114	you're seeing, not by what you eventually learned). See
115	[`pitfalls/README.md`](pitfalls/README.md) for the full template and
116	when-to-add rules.
117	
118	**Pitfall vs Hard invariant**: a pitfall *graduates* to a Hard invariant in
119	this file when it (a) recurs across machines/agents/sessions despite being
120	documented, (b) silently corrupts state, or (c) the workaround is non-obvious
121	enough that "remember to do X" isn't safe. When graduating, leave the
122	`pitfalls/<slug>.md` as historical record and link to it from the new
123	invariant.
124	
125	`pitfalls/` is excluded from N/A (no packaging — these files stay in the repo) (see N/A) and
126	**not** auto-redacted; review for secrets before committing.
127	<!-- project-knowledge-harness:agent-guidance --> (end)
128
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	package engine
2	
3	import (
4		"bufio"
5		"bytes"
6		"context"
7		"encoding/json"
8		"fmt"
9		"io"
10		"net/http"
11		"os"
12		"sort"
13		"strings"
14		"time"
15	
16		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/lang"
17	)
18	
19	// LLMConfig parameterizes an OpenAI-compatible backend (copilot-proxy, Ollama,
20	// OpenRouter, LiteLLM, or a generic base_url+key endpoint).
21	type LLMConfig struct {
22		Name      string        // "copilot", "ollama", "openrouter", ...
23		BaseURL   string        // e.g. "http://localhost:4141/v1"
24		Model     string        // e.g. "claude-sonnet-5"
25		APIKeyEnv string        // env var holding the key; "" => no Authorization header
26		Timeout   time.Duration // per-request timeout (0 => 60s)
27	}
28	
29	// LLMEngine talks to any OpenAI-compatible /chat/completions endpoint.
30	type LLMEngine struct {
31		cfg  LLMConfig
32		key  string
33		http *http.Client
34	}
35	
36	// NewLLM builds an LLM engine, resolving the API key once from the environment.
37	func NewLLM(cfg LLMConfig) *LLMEngine {
38		if cfg.Timeout == 0 {
39			cfg.Timeout = 60 * time.Second
40		}
41		key := ""
42		if cfg.APIKeyEnv != "" {
43			key = strings.TrimSpace(os.Getenv(cfg.APIKeyEnv))
44		}
45		return &LLMEngine{cfg: cfg, key: key, http: &http.Client{Timeout: cfg.Timeout}}
46	}
47	
48	// Name returns the provider name (e.g. "copilot").
49	func (e *LLMEngine) Name() string { return e.cfg.Name }
50	
51	// Supports reports that LLM engines translate. (Dictionary lookups route to the
52	// dedicated dictionary engine in v1, so the chain never sends dict mode here.)
53	func (e *LLMEngine) Supports(m Mode) bool { return m == ModeTranslate }
54	
55	// auth adds the bearer token only when a key is configured. copilot-proxy needs
56	// no Authorization header, so an empty APIKeyEnv yields no header at all.
57	func (e *LLMEngine) auth(r *http.Request) {
58		if e.key != "" {
59			r.Header.Set("Authorization", "Bearer "+e.key)
60		}
61	}
62	
63	// endpoint joins the base URL with a path segment.
64	func (e *LLMEngine) endpoint(path string) string {
65		return strings.TrimRight(e.cfg.BaseURL, "/") + path
66	}
67	
68	// Available is a cheap health probe: GET {BaseURL}/models with a short timeout.
69	func (e *LLMEngine) Available(ctx context.Context) bool {
70		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, 800*time.Millisecond)
71		defer cancel()
72		req, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodGet, e.endpoint("/models"), nil)
73		if err != nil {
74			return false
75		}
76		e.auth(req)
77		resp, err := e.http.Do(req)
78		if err != nil {
79			return false
80		}
81		defer resp.Body.Close()
82		_, _ = io.Copy(io.Discard, resp.Body)
83		return resp.StatusCode == http.StatusOK
84	}
85	
86	// Detect is a no-op for the LLM engine in this slice; offline detection is added
87	// with the free-API slice. Callers fall back to lang.Detect when this returns "".
88	func (e *LLMEngine) Detect(ctx context.Context, text string) (string, error) {
89		return "", nil
90	}
91	
92	// --- OpenAI chat wire types ---
93	
94	type chatMessage struct {
95		Role    string `json:"role"`
96		Content string `json:"content"`
97	}
98	
99	type chatRequest struct {
100		Model    string        `json:"model"`
101		Messages []chatMessage `json:"messages"`
102		Stream   bool          `json:"stream"`
103	}
104	
105	type chatResponse struct {
106		Choices []struct {
107			Message      chatMessage `json:"message"`
108			FinishReason string      `json:"finish_reason"`
109		} `json:"choices"`
110		Error *struct {
111			Message string `json:"message"`
112		} `json:"error"`
113	}
114	
115	// streamDelta is one OpenAI SSE chunk (stream:true). FinishReason is non-nil
116	// only on the terminal chunk ("stop" on a clean finish, "length" on a cap hit).
117	type streamDelta struct {
118		Choices []struct {
119			Delta struct {
120				Content string `json:"content"`
121			} `json:"delta"`
122			FinishReason *string `json:"finish_reason"`
123		} `json:"choices"`
124	}
125	
126	// --- Anthropic Messages API wire types (/v1/messages) ---
127	
128	const anthropicMaxTokens = 4096
129	
130	// learnMaxTokens is a larger cap for learn mode: a gloss-rich structured JSON reply
131	// can exceed the terse-translation budget, and a truncated JSON body fails to parse.
132	const learnMaxTokens = 8192
133	
134	type anthropicRequest struct {
135		Model     string             `json:"model"`
136		MaxTokens int                `json:"max_tokens"`
137		System    string             `json:"system,omitempty"`
138		Messages  []anthropicMessage `json:"messages"`
139		Stream    bool               `json:"stream"`
140	}
141	
142	type anthropicMessage struct {
143		Role    string `json:"role"`
144		Content string `json:"content"`
145	}
146	
147	type anthropicResponse struct {
148		Content []struct {
149			Type string `json:"type"`
150			Text string `json:"text"`
151		} `json:"content"`
152		StopReason string `json:"stop_reason"`
153		Error      *struct {
154			Message string `json:"message"`
155		} `json:"error"`
156	}
157	
158	type anthropicStreamEvent struct {
159		Type  string `json:"type"`
160		Delta struct {
161			Type string `json:"type"`
162			Text string `json:"text"`
163			// StopReason is carried on the terminal "message_delta" event
164			// ("end_turn"/"stop_sequence" on a clean finish, "max_tokens" on a cap
165			// hit); empty on "text_delta" events.
166			StopReason string `json:"stop_reason"`
167		} `json:"delta"`
168		Error *struct {
169			Message string `json:"message"`
170		} `json:"error"`
171	}
172	
173	// isAnthropicModel reports whether a model id must use the Anthropic Messages API
174	// (/v1/messages) rather than /chat/completions. copilot-proxy serves Claude
175	// models ONLY via the messages endpoint — they return HTTP 400
176	// "model_not_supported" on /chat/completions despite being listed in /v1/models.
177	func isAnthropicModel(model string) bool {
178		return strings.HasPrefix(strings.ToLower(model), "claude")
179	}
180	
181	// Translate performs a translation, streaming tokens when req.Stream is set.
182	// It always returns a channel that closes after exactly one terminal chunk, and
183	// dispatches to the Anthropic Messages API for Claude models.
184	func (e *LLMEngine) Translate(ctx context.Context, req Request) (<-chan Chunk, error) {
185		if strings.TrimSpace(req.Text) == "" {
186			return nil, ErrEmptyInput
187		}
188		model := NormalizeModelID(e.cfg.Model)
189		// Apply a per-request model override only when it targets this provider, so a
190		// copilot model id never reaches an Ollama fallback (which would 404).
191		if req.Model != "" && (req.ModelProvider == "" || req.ModelProvider == e.cfg.Name) {
192			model = NormalizeModelID(req.Model)
193		}
194		if isAnthropicModel(model) {
195			return e.translateAnthropic(ctx, req, model)
196		}
197		return e.translateOpenAI(ctx, req, model)
198	}
199	
200	// ModelLister is implemented by engines that can enumerate their models.
201	type ModelLister interface {
202		Models(ctx context.Context) ([]string, error)
203	}
204	
205	type modelsResponse struct {
206		Data []struct {
207			ID                 string   `json:"id"`
208			SupportedEndpoints []string `json:"supported_endpoints"`
209		} `json:"data"`
210	}
211	
212	// Models fetches the provider's model ids, keeping only those usable through the
213	// transports this engine speaks: OpenAI /chat/completions, or (for claude-*) the
214	// Anthropic /v1/messages endpoint. Ids needing only /responses are dropped.
215	func (e *LLMEngine) Models(ctx context.Context) ([]string, error) {
216		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, 4*time.Second)
217		defer cancel()
218		req, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodGet, e.endpoint("/models"), nil)
219		if err != nil {
220			return nil, err
221		}
222		e.auth(req)
223		resp, err := e.http.Do(req)
224		if err != nil {
225			return nil, err
226		}
227		defer resp.Body.Close()
228		if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusOK {
229			return nil, e.httpError(resp)
230		}
231		var mr modelsResponse
232		if err := json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&mr); err != nil {
233			return nil, err
234		}
235		var out []string
236		for _, m := range mr.Data {
237			if usableModel(m.ID, m.SupportedEndpoints) {
238				out = append(out, m.ID)
239			}
240		}
241		sort.Strings(out)
242		return out, nil
243	}
244	
245	// usableModel reports whether a model id can be driven by this engine.
246	func usableModel(id string, endpoints []string) bool {
247		if isAnthropicModel(id) {
248			return true // routed via /v1/messages
249		}
250		for _, ep := range endpoints {
251			if ep == "/chat/completions" {
252				return true
253			}
254		}
255		// No endpoint metadata (e.g. Ollama) => assume chat-usable.
256		return len(endpoints) == 0
257	}
258	
259	// finalize builds the terminal result from the accumulated translation text.
260	func (e *LLMEngine) finalize(full, model string, req Request) *TranslateResult {
261		res := &TranslateResult{
262			Translation: strings.TrimSpace(full),
263			Target:      req.Target,
264			Engine:      e.cfg.Name,
265			Model:       model,
266		}
267		// The plain-text prompt returns only the translation, so fill the detected
268		// source offline when the caller asked to auto-detect.
269		if src := strings.TrimSpace(req.Source); src == "" || src == "auto" {
270			res.DetectedSource = lang.Detect(req.Text)
271		}
272		return res
273	}
274	
275	// promptFor picks the (system, user) prompt pair for a request: the structured
276	// tutor prompt for learn mode, else the plain translate prompt.
277	func promptFor(req Request) (system, user string) {
278		if req.Learn {
279			return buildLearnPrompt(req)
280		}
281		return buildTranslatePrompt(req)
282	}
283	
284	// finalizeResult builds the terminal result, parsing structured learn output when
285	// the request asked for it.
286	func (e *LLMEngine) finalizeResult(full, model string, req Request) *TranslateResult {
287		if req.Learn {
288			return e.finalizeLearn(full, model, req)
289		}
290		return e.finalize(full, model, req)
291	}
292	
293	// finalizeLearn parses the model's structured JSON reply into a LearnResult. It is
294	// defensive — it strips a markdown fence and slices to the outermost object before
295	// unmarshalling — and on any failure falls back to the raw text as the translation
296	// (with a warning), so a malformed reply degrades gracefully instead of erroring.
297	// res.Target is set to the FOREIGN (away) language because res.Translation always
298	// holds the foreign sentence (the corrected sentence, or the translation).
299	func (e *LLMEngine) finalizeLearn(full, model string, req Request) *TranslateResult {
300		res := &TranslateResult{
301			Target: req.PairAway,
302			Engine: e.cfg.Name,
303			Model:  model,
304		}
305		if src := strings.TrimSpace(req.Source); src == "" || src == "auto" {
306			res.DetectedSource = lang.Detect(req.Text)
307		}
308		lr, err := parseLearn(full)
309		if err != nil {
310			res.Translation = strings.TrimSpace(full)
311			res.Warnings = append(res.Warnings, "learn: could not parse structured output — showing the raw reply")
312			return res
313		}
314		lr.Direction = learnDirection(req) // trust offline detection over the model's guess
315		if strings.TrimSpace(lr.Original) == "" {
316			lr.Original = req.Text
317		}
318		res.Learn = lr
319		if lr.Direction == "correct" && strings.TrimSpace(lr.Corrected) != "" {
320			res.Translation = strings.TrimSpace(lr.Corrected)
321		} else {
322			res.Translation = strings.TrimSpace(lr.Translation)
323		}
324		return res
325	}
326	
327	// parseLearn extracts and unmarshals a LearnResult from a model reply, returning an
328	// error when the JSON is absent/invalid or carries no main sentence.
329	func parseLearn(full string) (*LearnResult, error) {
330		var lr LearnResult
331		if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(extractJSON(full)), &lr); err != nil {
332			return nil, err
333		}
334		if strings.TrimSpace(lr.Translation) == "" && strings.TrimSpace(lr.Corrected) == "" {
335			return nil, fmt.Errorf("learn: empty structured result")
336		}
337		return &lr, nil
338	}
339	
340	// extractJSON returns the outermost JSON object in s, tolerating a surrounding
341	// markdown code fence or stray prose around it.
342	func extractJSON(s string) string {
343		s = strings.TrimSpace(s)
344		if strings.HasPrefix(s, "```") { // strip ```json … ``` fence
345			if i := strings.IndexByte(s, '\n'); i >= 0 {
346				s = s[i+1:]
347			}
348			s = strings.TrimSpace(strings.TrimSuffix(strings.TrimSpace(s), "```"))
349		}
350		start := strings.IndexByte(s, '{')
351		end := strings.LastIndexByte(s, '}')
352		if start >= 0 && end > start {
353			return s[start : end+1]
354		}
355		return s
356	}
357	
358	// markTruncated flags a result whose stream ended before the model finished, so
359	// the caller keeps the partial text but never treats it as a complete answer.
360	func markTruncated(res *TranslateResult) {
361		res.Truncated = true
362		res.Warnings = append(res.Warnings,
363			"output was cut off before completion (stream truncated) — press Enter to retry")
364	}
365	
366	// translateOpenAI uses the OpenAI /chat/completions endpoint.
367	func (e *LLMEngine) translateOpenAI(ctx context.Context, req Request, model string) (<-chan Chunk, error) {
368		system, user := promptFor(req)
369		stream := req.Stream && !req.Learn // learn output is structured JSON: parse at done
370		body := chatRequest{
371			Model: model,
372			Messages: []chatMessage{
373				{Role: "system", Content: system},
374				{Role: "user", Content: user},
375			},
376			Stream: stream,
377		}
378		buf, err := json.Marshal(body)
379		if err != nil {
380			return nil, err
381		}
382		httpReq, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodPost, e.endpoint("/chat/completions"), bytes.NewReader(buf))
383		if err != nil {
384			return nil, err
385		}
386		httpReq.Header.Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
387		if stream {
388			httpReq.Header.Set("Accept", "text/event-stream")
389		}
390		e.auth(httpReq)
391	
392		ch := make(chan Chunk, 32) // buffered so a fast stream doesn't block on renders
393		go func() {
394			defer close(ch)
395			resp, err := e.http.Do(httpReq)
396			if err != nil {
397				ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: fmt.Errorf("%s: %w", e.cfg.Name, err)}
398				return
399			}
400			defer resp.Body.Close()
401			if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusOK {
402				ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: e.httpError(resp)}
403				return
404			}
405	
406			var full strings.Builder
407			complete := true
408			if stream {
409				var err error
410				complete, err = e.readSSE(ctx, resp.Body, ch, &full)
411				if err != nil {
412					ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: err}
413					return
414				}
415			} else {
416				var cr chatResponse
417				if err := json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&cr); err != nil {
418					ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: fmt.Errorf("%s: decode response: %w", e.cfg.Name, err)}
419					return
420				}
421				if cr.Error != nil {
422					ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: fmt.Errorf("%s: %s", e.cfg.Name, cr.Error.Message)}
423					return
424				}
425				if len(cr.Choices) == 0 {
426					ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: fmt.Errorf("%s: empty response", e.cfg.Name)}
427					return
428				}
429				full.WriteString(cr.Choices[0].Message.Content)
430				complete = cr.Choices[0].FinishReason != "length" // "length" => hit the cap
431			}
432			res := e.finalizeResult(full.String(), model, req)
433			if !complete {
434				markTruncated(res)
435			}
436			ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkDone, Result: res}
437		}()
438		return ch, nil
439	}
440	
441	// translateAnthropic uses the Anthropic Messages API (/v1/messages).
442	func (e *LLMEngine) translateAnthropic(ctx context.Context, req Request, model string) (<-chan Chunk, error) {
443		system, user := promptFor(req)
444		stream := req.Stream && !req.Learn // learn output is structured JSON: parse at done
445		maxTokens := anthropicMaxTokens
446		if req.Learn {
447			maxTokens = learnMaxTokens
448		}
449		body := anthropicRequest{
450			Model:     model,
451			MaxTokens: maxTokens,
452			System:    system,
453			Messages:  []anthropicMessage{{Role: "user", Content: user}},
454			Stream:    stream,
455		}
456		buf, err := json.Marshal(body)
457		if err != nil {
458			return nil, err
459		}
460		httpReq, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodPost, e.endpoint("/messages"), bytes.NewReader(buf))
461		if err != nil {
462			return nil, err
463		}
464		httpReq.Header.Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
465		httpReq.Header.Set("anthropic-version", "2023-06-01")
466		if stream {
467			httpReq.Header.Set("Accept", "text/event-stream")
468		}
469		e.anthropicAuth(httpReq)
470	
471		ch := make(chan Chunk, 32)
472		go func() {
473			defer close(ch)
474			resp, err := e.http.Do(httpReq)
475			if err != nil {
476				ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: fmt.Errorf("%s: %w", e.cfg.Name, err)}
477				return
478			}
479			defer resp.Body.Close()
480			if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusOK {
481				ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: e.httpError(resp)}
482				return
483			}
484	
485			var full strings.Builder
486			complete := true
487			if stream {
488				var err error
489				complete, err = e.readAnthropicSSE(ctx, resp.Body, ch, &full)
490				if err != nil {
491					ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: err}
492					return
493				}
494			} else {
495				var ar anthropicResponse
496				if err := json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&ar); err != nil {
497					ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: fmt.Errorf("%s: decode response: %w", e.cfg.Name, err)}
498					return
499				}
500				if ar.Error != nil {
501					ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: fmt.Errorf("%s: %s", e.cfg.Name, ar.Error.Message)}
502					return
503				}
504				for _, c := range ar.Content {
505					if c.Type == "text" {
506						full.WriteString(c.Text)
507					}
508				}
509				complete = ar.StopReason != "max_tokens" // "max_tokens" => hit the cap
510			}
511			res := e.finalizeResult(full.String(), model, req)
512			if !complete {
513				markTruncated(res)
514			}
515			ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkDone, Result: res}
516		}()
517		return ch, nil
518	}
519	
520	// anthropicAuth sets the Anthropic auth header (x-api-key, not Bearer). The
521	// copilot-proxy needs none, so an empty key yields no header.
522	func (e *LLMEngine) anthropicAuth(r *http.Request) {
523		if e.key != "" {
524			r.Header.Set("x-api-key", e.key)
525		}
526	}
527	
528	// readSSE parses an OpenAI-style event stream, emitting a ChunkToken per content
529	// delta and accumulating the full text into full. It reports complete=true only
530	// when the stream ended on a terminal marker ([DONE] or finish_reason=="stop");
531	// a stream that closes with no marker, or on finish_reason=="length", is treated
532	// as truncated so the caller can flag it rather than silently accept partial text.
533	func (e *LLMEngine) readSSE(ctx context.Context, r io.Reader, ch chan<- Chunk, full *strings.Builder) (bool, error) {
534		sc := bufio.NewScanner(r)
535		sc.Buffer(make([]byte, 0, 64<<10), 1<<20) // tolerate long SSE lines
536		sawDone := false
537		finish := ""
538		for sc.Scan() {
539			line := sc.Text()
540			if !strings.HasPrefix(line, "data:") {
541				continue
542			}
543			payload := strings.TrimSpace(line[len("data:"):])
544			if payload == "[DONE]" {
545				sawDone = true
546				break
547			}
548			var d streamDelta
549			if json.Unmarshal([]byte(payload), &d) != nil || len(d.Choices) == 0 {
550				continue
551			}
552			if fr := d.Choices[0].FinishReason; fr != nil && *fr != "" {
553				finish = *fr
554			}
555			tok := d.Choices[0].Delta.Content
556			if tok == "" {
557				continue
558			}
559			full.WriteString(tok)
560			select {
561			case ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkToken, Text: tok}:
562			case <-ctx.Done():
563				return false, fmt.Errorf("%s: %w", e.cfg.Name, ctx.Err())
564			}
565		}
566		if err := sc.Err(); err != nil {
567			return false, fmt.Errorf("%s: %w", e.cfg.Name, err)
568		}
569		complete := finish != "length" && (sawDone || finish == "stop")
570		return complete, nil
571	}
572	
573	// readAnthropicSSE parses an Anthropic Messages event stream, emitting a
574	// ChunkToken per text_delta and accumulating the full text into full. It reports
575	// complete=true only when a terminal marker arrived (a message_stop event, or a
576	// message_delta with a natural stop_reason); a stream that closes with neither,
577	// or on stop_reason=="max_tokens", is treated as truncated.
578	func (e *LLMEngine) readAnthropicSSE(ctx context.Context, r io.Reader, ch chan<- Chunk, full *strings.Builder) (bool, error) {
579		sc := bufio.NewScanner(r)
580		sc.Buffer(make([]byte, 0, 64<<10), 1<<20)
581		sawStop := false
582		stopReason := ""
583		for sc.Scan() {
584			line := sc.Text()
585			if !strings.HasPrefix(line, "data:") {
586				continue // ignore "event:" and blank lines
587			}
588			payload := strings.TrimSpace(line[len("data:"):])
589			if payload == "" {
590				continue
591			}
592			var ev anthropicStreamEvent
593			if json.Unmarshal([]byte(payload), &ev) != nil {
594				continue
595			}
596			if ev.Error != nil {
597				return false, fmt.Errorf("%s: %s", e.cfg.Name, ev.Error.Message)
598			}
599			switch ev.Type {
600			case "message_stop":
601				sawStop = true
602				continue
603			case "message_delta":
604				if ev.Delta.StopReason != "" {
605					stopReason = ev.Delta.StopReason
606				}
607				continue
608			}
609			if ev.Type != "content_block_delta" || ev.Delta.Type != "text_delta" || ev.Delta.Text == "" {
610				continue
611			}
612			full.WriteString(ev.Delta.Text)
613			select {
614			case ch <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkToken, Text: ev.Delta.Text}:
615			case <-ctx.Done():
616				return false, fmt.Errorf("%s: %w", e.cfg.Name, ctx.Err())
617			}
618		}
619		if err := sc.Err(); err != nil {
620			return false, fmt.Errorf("%s: %w", e.cfg.Name, err)
621		}
622		complete := stopReason != "max_tokens" &&
623			(sawStop || stopReason == "end_turn" || stopReason == "stop_sequence")
624		return complete, nil
625	}
626	
627	// httpError reads an error body (best effort) into a descriptive error.
628	func (e *LLMEngine) httpError(resp *http.Response) error {
629		b, _ := io.ReadAll(io.LimitReader(resp.Body, 2<<10))
630		msg := strings.TrimSpace(string(b))
631		if msg == "" {
632			msg = resp.Status
633		}
634		return fmt.Errorf("%s: http %d: %s", e.cfg.Name, resp.StatusCode, msg)
635	}
636
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/engine/chain.go`

```text
1	package engine
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"fmt"
6		"sync"
7		"time"
8	
9		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/debug"
10	)
11	
12	// Chain is the AUTO fallback router. It tries engines in configured order and
13	// fails over to the next one when the current engine is known-down or fails
14	// before producing any token. Once tokens have streamed, a mid-stream error is
15	// surfaced rather than restarting on another engine (which would garble output);
16	// the common "provider not running" case fails at connect time, pre-token, and
17	// switches cleanly.
18	type Chain struct {
19		engines []Engine
20		ttl     time.Duration
21	
22		mu    sync.Mutex
23		avail map[string]probeCache
24	}
25	
26	type probeCache struct {
27		ok bool
28		at time.Time
29	}
30	
31	// NewChain builds a chain from engines in priority order. ttl is how long a
32	// health verdict is cached (default 5s).
33	func NewChain(engines []Engine, ttl time.Duration) *Chain {
34		if ttl <= 0 {
35			ttl = 5 * time.Second
36		}
37		return &Chain{engines: engines, ttl: ttl, avail: map[string]probeCache{}}
38	}
39	
40	// Name reports the chain as the "auto" engine.
41	func (c *Chain) Name() string { return "auto" }
42	
43	// Supports reports true if any member supports the mode.
44	func (c *Chain) Supports(m Mode) bool {
45		for _, e := range c.engines {
46			if e.Supports(m) {
47				return true
48			}
49		}
50		return false
51	}
52	
53	// recentlyDown reports whether we have a fresh "down" verdict for e, so we can
54	// skip it without paying a probe on the hot path. Engines are otherwise tried
55	// optimistically (no pre-probe latency on the happy path).
56	func (c *Chain) recentlyDown(e Engine) bool {
57		c.mu.Lock()
58		defer c.mu.Unlock()
59		pc, ok := c.avail[e.Name()]
60		return ok && !pc.ok && time.Since(pc.at) < c.ttl
61	}
62	
63	func (c *Chain) mark(e Engine, ok bool) {
64		c.mu.Lock()
65		c.avail[e.Name()] = probeCache{ok: ok, at: time.Now()}
66		c.mu.Unlock()
67	}
68	
69	func (c *Chain) candidates(m Mode) []Engine {
70		var out []Engine
71		for _, e := range c.engines {
72			if e.Supports(m) {
73				out = append(out, e)
74			}
75		}
76		return out
77	}
78	
79	// Translate proxies the first working engine's stream. Failover happens only
80	// before the first token.
81	func (c *Chain) Translate(ctx context.Context, req Request) (<-chan Chunk, error) {
82		cands := c.candidates(req.Mode)
83		if len(cands) == 0 {
84			return nil, ErrNoEngineForMode
85		}
86		out := make(chan Chunk, 32)
87		go func() {
88			defer close(out)
89			var lastErr error
90			var warns []string // reasons earlier engines were skipped/failed
91			for _, e := range cands {
92				if ctx.Err() != nil { // request abandoned (e.g. user typed more)
93					out <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: ctx.Err()}
94					return
95				}
96				if c.recentlyDown(e) {
97					lastErr = fmt.Errorf("%s: recently unavailable", e.Name())
98					warns = append(warns, e.Name()+" skipped (recently unavailable)")
99					debug.Logf("chain: skip %s (recently unavailable)", e.Name())
100					continue
101				}
102				debug.Logf("chain: try %s", e.Name())
103				sub, err := e.Translate(ctx, req)
104				if err != nil {
105					lastErr = err
106					warns = append(warns, err.Error()) // engine errors are already name-prefixed
107					c.mark(e, false)
108					debug.Logf("chain: %s failed at connect: %v", e.Name(), err)
109					continue
110				}
111	
112				streamed, failed := false, false
113				for ch := range sub {
114					switch ch.Kind {
115					case ChunkToken:
116						streamed = true
117						out <- ch
118					case ChunkDone:
119						c.mark(e, true)
120						debug.Logf("chain: %s served the result", e.Name())
121						if ch.Result != nil && len(warns) > 0 {
122							ch.Result.Warnings = append(ch.Result.Warnings, warns...)
123						}
124						out <- ch
125						return
126					case ChunkError:
127						lastErr = ch.Err
128						failed = true
129						// Marking deferred until after the loop: a cancelled request
130						// (live-mode typing) must NOT be treated as an engine failure.
131					}
132				}
133	
134				if failed {
135					if ctx.Err() != nil { // cancelled: don't mark down, don't fail over
136						out <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: ctx.Err()}
137						return
138					}
139					c.mark(e, false)
140					warns = append(warns, lastErr.Error()) // already name-prefixed
141					if streamed {                          // already showed partial output: surface, don't restart
142						out <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: lastErr}
143						return
144					}
145					continue // clean pre-token failover
146				}
147			}
148			if lastErr == nil {
149				lastErr = ErrAllEnginesFailed
150			}
151			out <- Chunk{Kind: ChunkError, Err: fmt.Errorf("%w: %v", ErrAllEnginesFailed, lastErr)}
152		}()
153		return out, nil
154	}
155	
156	// Detect returns the first available engine's detection, else "".
157	func (c *Chain) Detect(ctx context.Context, text string) (string, error) {
158		for _, e := range c.engines {
159			if c.recentlyDown(e) {
160				continue
161			}
162			if code, err := e.Detect(ctx, text); err == nil && code != "" {
163				return code, nil
164			}
165		}
166		return "", nil
167	}
168	
169	// Available reports whether any member is currently reachable.
170	func (c *Chain) Available(ctx context.Context) bool {
171		for _, e := range c.engines {
172			if e.Available(ctx) {
173				c.mark(e, true)
174				return true
175			}
176			c.mark(e, false)
177		}
178		return false
179	}
180
```

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/engine/smartauto.go`

```text
1	package engine
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"strings"
6		"unicode"
7	
8		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/debug"
9		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/lang"
10	)
11	
12	// SmartAutoEngine is the "smart" default: it treats a single word/term as a
13	// dictionary lookup (delegating to the smart-dict engine, which itself falls back
14	// to the LLM on a miss) and a phrase or sentence as an LLM translation. It is
15	// bidirectional-friendly — the caller sets Pair/PairHome/PairAway and the routed
16	// Target, and both branches honor them.
17	//
18	// Because dictionary word lookups are direction-agnostic (script routes them to
19	// CC-CEDICT vs ECDICT) and phrase translation uses the pair-aware LLM prompt, a
20	// single word like "test" is answered from the dictionary instead of risking an
21	// LLM echo, and a full sentence is translated into the other pair language.
22	type SmartAutoEngine struct {
23		smart Engine // dictionary + LLM fallback (serves ModeDict)
24		llm   Engine // LLM translate, possibly a fallback chain (serves ModeTranslate)
25	}
26	
27	// NewSmartAuto builds a smart-auto engine over a smart-dict engine (word lookups)
28	// and an LLM/translate engine (phrases).
29	func NewSmartAuto(smart, llm Engine) *SmartAutoEngine {
30		return &SmartAutoEngine{smart: smart, llm: llm}
31	}
32	
33	// Name returns "smart-auto".
34	func (e *SmartAutoEngine) Name() string { return "smart-auto" }
35	
36	// Supports translate mode (the main-path request mode); it dispatches to a
37	// dictionary lookup internally when the input looks like a single word.
38	func (e *SmartAutoEngine) Supports(m Mode) bool { return m == ModeTranslate }
39	
40	// Detect is not meaningful here (the LLM/dict sub-engines own detection).
41	func (e *SmartAutoEngine) Detect(ctx context.Context, text string) (string, error) {
42		return "", nil
43	}
44	
45	// Available reports true when either branch can serve.
46	func (e *SmartAutoEngine) Available(ctx context.Context) bool {
47		return e.smart.Available(ctx) || e.llm.Available(ctx)
48	}
49	
50	// Translate routes a single word/term to the dictionary (smart-dict) and a phrase
51	// to the LLM. Streaming is preserved by returning the chosen sub-engine's channel.
52	func (e *SmartAutoEngine) Translate(ctx context.Context, req Request) (<-chan Chunk, error) {
53		if isLookup(req.Text) {
54			debug.Logf("smart-auto: %q → dictionary lookup (smart-dict), target=%s", truncateText(req.Text), req.Target)
55			r := req
56			r.Mode = ModeDict
57			return e.smart.Translate(ctx, r)
58		}
59		debug.Logf("smart-auto: %q → LLM translate, target=%s pair=%v", truncateText(req.Text), req.Target, req.Pair)
60		r := req
61		r.Mode = ModeTranslate
62		return e.llm.Translate(ctx, r)
63	}
64	
65	// isLookup reports whether text should be treated as a dictionary lookup (a
66	// single word or short term) rather than a phrase to translate. Latin/other: one
67	// token of letters (plus '-' / apostrophe), up to 32 runes. CJK: a short run of
68	// Han without spaces (a word or idiom, up to 4 characters). Anything with
69	// internal whitespace is a phrase.
70	func isLookup(text string) bool {
71		t := strings.TrimSpace(text)
72		if t == "" {
73			return false
74		}
75		if strings.ContainsAny(t, " \t\n\r") {
76			return false // multiple tokens → phrase
77		}
78		if lang.IsChinese(t) {
79			return len([]rune(t)) <= 4
80		}
81		for _, r := range t {
82			if !unicode.IsLetter(r) && r != '-' && r != '\'' {
83				return false // digits/punctuation → not a plain word
84			}
85		}
86		return len([]rune(t)) <= 32
87	}
88	
89	// truncateText shortens a string for a single-line debug label.
90	func truncateText(s string) string {
91		s = strings.TrimSpace(s)
92		r := []rune(s)
93		if len(r) > 40 {
94			return string(r[:40]) + "…"
95		}
96		return s
97	}
98
```
