```text
1	package tui
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"strings"
6	
7		"charm.land/bubbles/v2/key"
8		"charm.land/bubbles/v2/spinner"
9		tea "charm.land/bubbletea/v2"
10		"github.com/atotto/clipboard"
11	
12		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/debug"
13		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/engine"
14		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/lang"
15		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/store"
16		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/tts"
17	)
18	
19	// Update handles messages. Value receiver returning the modified copy is the
20	// single convention used throughout (see Model doc).
21	func (m Model) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
22		switch msg := msg.(type) {
23		case tea.WindowSizeMsg:
24			m.width, m.height = msg.Width, msg.Height
25			m.relayout()
26			m.ready = true
27			return m, nil
28	
29		case tea.KeyPressMsg:
30			return m.handleKey(msg)
31	
32		case debounceMsg:
33			if msg.seq != m.seq {
34				return m, nil // a newer keystroke arrived; this tick is stale
35			}
36			return m.launch(false)
37	
38		case historyLoadedMsg:
39			m.hist.SetItems(toListItems(msg.items))
40			return m, nil
41	
42		case modelsLoadedMsg:
43			m.modelsLoading = false
44			m.modelsErr = msg.err
45			m.cachedModels = msg.models
46			m.modelList.SetItems(modelItems(msg.models, m.currentModelID()))
47			return m, nil
48	
49		case tea.MouseWheelMsg:
50			return m.handleMouseWheel(msg)
51	
52		case tea.MouseClickMsg:
53			return m.handleMouseClick(msg)
54	
55		case spinner.TickMsg:
56			var cmd tea.Cmd
57			m.sp, cmd = m.sp.Update(msg)
58			// Animate the placeholder inside the (fixed-height) viewport so the
59			// layout never jumps while waiting for the first token.
60			if m.status == statusTranslating && m.streamBuf == "" {
61				m.vp.SetContent(m.translatingPlaceholder())
62			}
63			return m, cmd
64	
65		case flashClearMsg:
66			m.flash = ""
67			return m, nil
68	
69		case speakDoneMsg:
70			m.stopSpeak() // release the cancel; playback has ended
71			if msg.err != nil && m.base.Err() == nil {
72				m.flash = "speak failed"
73				return m, flashCmd()
74			}
75			m.flash = ""
76			return m, nil
77	
78		case streamMsg:
79			return m.handleStream(msg)
80		}
81	
82		var cmd tea.Cmd
83		m.ta, cmd = m.ta.Update(msg)
84		return m, cmd
85	}
86	
87	// handleStream applies one engine Chunk, dropping anything from a superseded
88	// request. This seq guard is the correctness mechanism: cancelled results can
89	// never render even if a goroutine emits one late.
90	func (m Model) handleStream(msg streamMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
91		if msg.seq != m.inflight {
92			return m, nil
93		}
94		switch msg.chunk.Kind {
95		case engine.ChunkToken:
96			m.retrying = false // real tokens are flowing; drop the "retrying" label
97			m.streamBuf += msg.chunk.Text
98			m.vp.SetContent(m.st.trans.Render(m.streamBuf))
99			m.vp.GotoBottom()
100			return m, waitStream(m.stream, m.inflight) // re-subscribe for the next token
101	
102		case engine.ChunkDone:
103			m.inflight = 0
104			m.status = statusDone
105			m.err = nil
106			m.lastDoneKey = m.pendingKey // remember what was translated (skip re-runs)
107			if msg.chunk.Result != nil {
108				r := *msg.chunk.Result
109				// A truncated stream (rare copilot-proxy drop) is re-fired once
110				// automatically — a fresh request almost always completes. Only after a
111				// second truncation do we settle on the partial text + ⚠.
112				if r.Truncated && m.autoRetryKey != m.pendingKey {
113					m.autoRetryKey = m.pendingKey
114					nm, cmd := m.launch(true) // force a fresh fetch of the same input
115					mm := nm.(Model)
116					mm.retrying = true
117					mm.vp.SetContent(mm.translatingPlaceholder())
118					return mm, cmd
119				}
120				m.autoRetryKey = cacheKey{} // settled (complete, or gave up after the retry)
121				m.retrying = false
122				m.result = msg.chunk.Result
123				// A truncated result keeps its partial text on screen (with a ⚠), but
124				// must not be cached or persisted as if it were the real answer — so a
125				// manual retry (Enter) actually re-fetches instead of replaying it.
126				if !r.Truncated {
127					m.cache[m.pendingKey] = msg.chunk.Result // write-through (force overwrites)
128				}
129				if r.Engine != "" {
130					m.curEngine = r.Engine
131				}
132				if r.Model != "" {
133					m.curModel = r.Model
134				}
135				m.relayout() // engine/model segment width may have changed
136				m.vp.SetContent(m.renderResult(r))
137				m.vp.GotoTop() // show a completed result from its start, not scrolled
138				// Don't persist a truncated result, or a suggestions-only result
139				// (empty translation, no entry).
140				if !r.Truncated && (r.Dictionary != nil || r.Translation != "") {
141					return m, saveHistoryCmd(m.p.Store, m.recordFor(r))
142				}
143				return m, nil
144			}
145			return m, nil
146	
147		case engine.ChunkError:
148			m.inflight = 0
149			m.retrying = false
150			m.autoRetryKey = cacheKey{}
151			m.status = statusError
152			m.err = msg.chunk.Err
153			m.vp.SetContent(m.st.errText.Render("✗ " + msg.chunk.Err.Error()))
154			return m, nil
155		}
156		return m, nil
157	}
158	
159	// isTextInput reports whether a key press produced a printable, non-whitespace
160	// character (Key.Text is set only for printable characters). Space/tab are
161	// treated as navigation so they can page the viewport instead of typing.
162	func isTextInput(msg tea.KeyPressMsg) bool {
163		return msg.Text != "" && strings.TrimSpace(msg.Text) != ""
164	}
165	
166	func (m Model) handleKey(msg tea.KeyPressMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
167		// An open overlay (history / language / model picker) captures keys.
168		if m.overlay != overlayNone {
169			switch msg.String() {
170			case "ctrl+c":
171				m.cancelInflight()
172				return m, tea.Quit
173			case "esc":
174				m.overlay = overlayNone
175				return m, nil
176			case "enter":
177				return m.overlaySelect()
178			}
179			cmd := m.overlayListUpdate(msg)
180			return m, cmd
181		}
182	
183		switch {
184		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.Quit):
185			m.cancelInflight()
186			m.stopSpeak()
187			return m, tea.Quit
188	
189		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.History):
190			m.overlay = overlayHistory
191			return m, loadHistoryCmd(m.p.Store)
192	
193		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.PickLang):
194			m.overlay = overlayLang
195			return m, nil
196	
197		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.PickModel):
198			if !m.learn && m.active().Mode == engine.ModeDict {
199				return m, nil // no model concept in dictionary mode (learn always uses an LLM)
200			}
201			return m.openModelPicker()
202	
203		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.PickPreset):
204			if m.learn || m.active().Mode == engine.ModeDict {
205				return m, nil // presets are LLM translate styles; learn has its own prompt
206			}
207			m.presetList.SetItems(presetItems(m.preset))
208			m.overlay = overlayPreset
209			return m, nil
210	
211		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.ToggleLearn):
212			if m.p.LearnEngine == nil {
213				m.flash = "learn needs an LLM provider"
214				return m, flashCmd()
215			}
216			m.learn = !m.learn
217			if m.learn {
218				// Learn is bidirectional; ensure a distinct "away" (foreign) language.
219				m.pair = true
220				if m.pairWith == "" || strings.EqualFold(m.pairWith, m.target) {
221					if strings.HasPrefix(strings.ToLower(m.target), "en") {
222						m.pairWith = "zh-TW"
223					} else {
224						m.pairWith = "en"
225					}
226				}
227			}
228			m.relayout() // footer content changed
229			if m.live && strings.TrimSpace(m.ta.Value()) != "" {
230				return m.launch(true)
231			}
232			m.clearResult()
233			return m, nil
234	
235		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.TogglePair):
236			m.pair = !m.pair
237			// Pair mode is a no-op when "away" equals the target; pick a distinct away.
238			if m.pair && (m.pairWith == "" || strings.EqualFold(m.pairWith, m.target)) {
239				if strings.HasPrefix(strings.ToLower(m.target), "en") {
240					m.pairWith = "zh-TW"
241				} else {
242					m.pairWith = "en"
243				}
244			}
245			m.relayout() // footer pair segment changed
246			if m.live && strings.TrimSpace(m.ta.Value()) != "" {
247				return m.launch(false)
248			}
249			m.clearResult()
250			return m, nil
251	
252		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.Copy):
253			if txt := m.copyText(); txt != "" {
254				if err := clipboard.WriteAll(txt); err != nil {
255					m.flash = "copy failed"
256				} else {
257					m.flash = "copied ✓"
258				}
259				return m, flashCmd()
260			}
261			return m, nil
262	
263		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.Speak):
264			return m.speak()
265	
266		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.ToggleLive):
267			m.live = !m.live
268			return m, nil
269	
270		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.CycleEngine):
271			if m.learn {
272				m.flash = "learn mode (^n to exit)"
273				return m, flashCmd()
274			}
275			if n := len(m.p.Engines); n > 1 {
276				m.engIdx = (m.engIdx + 1) % n
277				m.curEngine, m.curModel = "", ""
278				m.relayout() // footer content (engine/mode) changed
279				// Only re-translate automatically in live mode; otherwise switch the
280				// engine, clear the now-stale result, and wait for Enter.
281				if m.live && strings.TrimSpace(m.ta.Value()) != "" {
282					return m.launch(true)
283				}
284				m.clearResult()
285			}
286			return m, nil
287	
288		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.Clear):
289			m.cancelInflight()
290			m.stopSpeak()
291			m.seq++
292			m.ta.Reset()
293			m.focus = focusInput
294			m.ta.Focus()
295			m.result = nil
296			m.err = nil
297			m.streamBuf = ""
298			m.status = statusIdle
299			m.vp.SetContent("")
300			return m, nil
301	
302		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.SwitchFocus):
303			// Toggle which pane the keyboard drives. Blurring the textarea also dims its
304			// border (View keys off ta.Focused()); the result box highlights via m.focus.
305			if m.focus == focusInput {
306				m.focus = focusOutput
307				m.ta.Blur()
308				return m, nil
309			}
310			m.focus = focusInput
311			return m, m.ta.Focus()
312	
313		case key.Matches(msg, m.keys.Translate):
314			// After a dictionary miss the input still equals the missed word, so a
315			// second Enter means "help me choose" — open the ranked suggestions.
316			if m.result != nil && len(m.result.Suggestions) > 0 &&
317				strings.TrimSpace(m.ta.Value()) == m.lastInput {
318				m.suggestList.SetItems(suggestItems(m.result.Suggestions))
319				m.overlay = overlaySuggest
320				return m, nil
321			}
322			return m.launch(true)
323		}
324	
325		// Output pane focused: the keyboard scrolls the result viewport. A printable
326		// character (not whitespace) snaps focus back to the input and is typed there,
327		// so the user can just start typing without pressing Tab first.
328		if m.focus == focusOutput {
329			if isTextInput(msg) {
330				m.focus = focusInput
331				cmd := m.ta.Focus()
332				var tcmd tea.Cmd
333				m.ta, tcmd = m.ta.Update(msg)
334				return m, tea.Batch(cmd, tcmd)
335			}
336			var cmd tea.Cmd
337			m.vp, cmd = m.vp.Update(msg)
338			return m, cmd
339		}
340	
341		// Ordinary keystroke: update the textarea, then (re)arm the debounce.
342		var cmd tea.Cmd
343		m.ta, cmd = m.ta.Update(msg)
344	
345		if strings.TrimSpace(m.ta.Value()) == "" {
346			// Input cleared: cancel in-flight work and clear the result pane.
347			m.cancelInflight()
348			m.seq++
349			m.result = nil
350			m.streamBuf = ""
351			m.status = statusIdle
352			m.vp.SetContent("")
353			return m, cmd
354		}
355	
356		if m.live {
357			m.seq++            // invalidate any pending debounce tick
358			m.cancelInflight() // stop any in-flight request; its results become stale
359			m.status = statusTyping
360			return m, tea.Batch(cmd, armDebounce(m.seq, m.debounce))
361		}
362		return m, cmd
363	}
364	
365	// translatingPlaceholder renders the fixed-height "translating…" placeholder,
366	// upgraded to a "retrying" label while an auto-retry after a truncation is
367	// in flight so the user sees why a second request fired.
368	func (m Model) translatingPlaceholder() string {
369		label := "translating…"
370		if m.retrying {
371			label = "translating… · retrying (stream truncated)"
372		}
373		return m.sp.View() + " " + m.st.dim.Render(label)
374	}
375	
376	// launch starts a streaming translation for the current input, cancelling any
377	// in-flight request first. It is the single place a translation begins.
378	// When force is false (the live/debounce path) it skips re-translating text that
379	// was already translated, to avoid redundant LLM/API calls.
380	func (m Model) launch(force bool) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
381		text := strings.TrimSpace(m.ta.Value())
382		if text == "" {
383			m.status = statusIdle
384			return m, nil
385		}
386		key := m.cacheKeyFor(text)
387		if !force && key == m.lastDoneKey && m.status == statusDone {
388			return m, nil // already showing this exact result
389		}
390		// Cache hit on the live/debounce path: render instantly with NO API call.
391		// Enter (force) always re-fetches and overwrites.
392		if !force {
393			if cached, ok := m.cache[key]; ok {
394				m.cancelInflight()
395				m.status = statusDone
396				m.err = nil
397				m.result = cached
398				if cached.Engine != "" {
399					m.curEngine = cached.Engine
400				}
401				if cached.Model != "" {
402					m.curModel = cached.Model
403				}
404				m.streamBuf = ""
405				m.lastInput = text
406				m.lastDoneKey = key
407				m.vp.SetContent(m.renderResult(*cached))
408				return m, nil
409			}
410		}
411		m.seq++
412		if m.cancel != nil {
413			m.cancel()
414		}
415		ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(m.base)
416		m.cancel = cancel
417		m.inflight = m.seq
418		m.status = statusTranslating
419		m.streamBuf = ""
420		m.retrying = false // a fresh launch clears any stale "retrying" label
421		m.err = nil
422		m.result = nil
423		m.lastInput = text
424		m.pendingKey = key
425		m.vp.SetContent(m.translatingPlaceholder()) // fixed-height placeholder
426	
427		seq := m.seq
428		ne := m.active()
429		eng := ne.Engine
430		target := m.target
431		pairOn := m.pair && m.pairWith != "" && ne.Mode == engine.ModeTranslate
432		if pairOn {
433			target = lang.PairTarget(m.target, m.pairWith, text) // home-lang input → away, else home
434			debug.Logf("tui pair route: home=%s away=%s → target=%s", m.target, m.pairWith, target)
435		}
436		req := engine.Request{
437			Text:          text,
438			Source:        m.source,
439			Target:        target,
440			Mode:          ne.Mode,
441			Stream:        true, // ignored by non-streaming engines (google/dict)
442			Model:         m.modelOverride,
443			ModelProvider: m.p.ModelProvider,
444			Preset:        m.preset,
445			Extra:         m.p.Instructions,
446			Pair:          pairOn,
447			PairHome:      m.target,
448			PairAway:      m.pairWith,
449		}
450		// Learn mode overrides the selected engine with a bare LLM engine and asks for a
451		// structured, non-streaming tutor reply routed by pair direction.
452		if m.learn && m.p.LearnEngine != nil {
453			eng = m.p.LearnEngine
454			req.Learn = true
455			req.Mode = engine.ModeTranslate
456			req.Stream = false
457			req.Preset = ""
458			req.Pair = true
459			req.PairHome = m.target
460			req.PairAway = m.pairWith
461			req.Target = lang.PairTarget(m.target, m.pairWith, text) // for display/history; engine sets its own
462		}
463		debug.Logf("tui launch: engine=%s mode=%d target=%s preset=%s learn=%v", eng.Name(), req.Mode, req.Target, m.preset, m.learn)
464		ch, err := eng.Translate(ctx, req)
465		if err != nil {
466			e := err
467			return m, func() tea.Msg {
468				return streamMsg{seq: seq, chunk: engine.Chunk{Kind: engine.ChunkError, Err: e}}
469			}
470		}
471		m.stream = ch
472		return m, waitStream(ch, seq)
473	}
474	
475	// cancelInflight cancels the current request (if any) and marks it as no longer
476	// in flight so late chunks are dropped.
477	func (m *Model) cancelInflight() {
478		if m.cancel != nil {
479			m.cancel()
480			m.cancel = nil
481		}
482		m.inflight = 0
483	}
484	
485	// clearResult drops the shown result. Used when a setting (engine/lang/model/
486	// style/pair) changes without an immediate re-translate, so the pane never shows
487	// output that's stale relative to the (now different) footer.
488	func (m *Model) clearResult() {
489		m.cancelInflight()
490		m.result = nil
491		m.streamBuf = ""
492		m.err = nil
493		m.status = statusIdle
494		m.vp.SetContent("")
495	}
496	
497	// copyText returns the text to place on the clipboard for the current result.
498	func (m Model) copyText() string {
499		if m.result == nil {
500			return ""
501		}
502		return strings.TrimSpace(m.result.Translation)
503	}
504	
505	// speak plays the foreign side of the current lookup/translation. When the output
506	// pane is focused it speaks the result explicitly; otherwise it uses the
507	// foreign-language ("副") preference. Playback runs off the UI thread.
508	func (m Model) speak() (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
509		if m.p.Speaker == nil {
510			m.flash = "tts disabled"
511			return m, flashCmd()
512		}
513		forced := tts.SideAuto
514		if m.focus == focusOutput {
515			forced = tts.SideResult // explicit output focus → speak the result pane
516		}
517		resText, resLang := "", m.target
518		if m.result != nil {
519			resText = strings.TrimSpace(m.result.Translation)
520			if resText == "" && m.result.Dictionary != nil {
521				resText = m.result.Dictionary.Word
522			}
523			if m.result.Target != "" {
524				resLang = m.result.Target
525			}
526		}
527		ch, ok := tts.Select(tts.SelectInput{
528			SourceText: m.ta.Value(),
529			SourceLang: m.source,
530			ResultText: resText,
531			ResultLang: resLang,
532			Foreign:    m.foreignPref(),
533			Forced:     forced,
534		})
535		if !ok {
536			m.flash = "nothing to speak"
537			return m, flashCmd()
538		}
539		m.stopSpeak() // debounce: cancel any playback still in flight
540		ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(m.base)
541		m.speakCancel = cancel
542		m.flash = "speaking…"
543		return m, tea.Batch(flashCmd(), speakCmd(m.p.Speaker, ctx, ch))
544	}
545	
546	// foreignPref is the preferred foreign/副 language: the configured value, else
547	// the pair-mode away language ("" => Select derives it).
548	func (m Model) foreignPref() string {
549		if m.p.Foreign != "" {
550			return m.p.Foreign
551		}
552		if m.pair && m.pairWith != "" {
553			return m.pairWith
554		}
555		return ""
556	}
557	
558	// stopSpeak cancels any in-flight TTS playback.
559	func (m *Model) stopSpeak() {
560		if m.speakCancel != nil {
561			m.speakCancel()
562			m.speakCancel = nil
563		}
564	}
565	
566	// currentModelID is the model id currently in effect (for the ✓ marker).
567	func (m Model) currentModelID() string {
568		if m.modelOverride != "" {
569			return m.modelOverride
570		}
571		return m.curModel
572	}
573	
574	// openModelPicker opens the model overlay, fetching the model list once per session.
575	func (m Model) openModelPicker() (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
576		m.overlay = overlayModel
577		if m.cachedModels == nil && !m.modelsLoading && m.modelsErr == nil {
578			m.modelsLoading = true
579			return m, fetchModelsCmd(m.p.ModelSource)
580		}
581		return m, nil
582	}
583	
584	// overlayListUpdate forwards a message to the active overlay's list.
585	func (m *Model) overlayListUpdate(msg tea.Msg) tea.Cmd {
586		var cmd tea.Cmd
587		switch m.overlay {
588		case overlayHistory:
589			m.hist, cmd = m.hist.Update(msg)
590		case overlayLang:
591			m.langList, cmd = m.langList.Update(msg)
592		case overlayModel:
593			m.modelList, cmd = m.modelList.Update(msg)
594		case overlaySuggest:
595			m.suggestList, cmd = m.suggestList.Update(msg)
596		case overlayPreset:
597			m.presetList, cmd = m.presetList.Update(msg)
598		}
599		return cmd
600	}
601	
602	// overlaySelect applies the highlighted overlay item and closes the overlay.
603	func (m Model) overlaySelect() (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
604		switch m.overlay {
605		case overlayHistory:
606			// Recall is an explicit "show me this" action: always translate.
607			if it, ok := m.hist.SelectedItem().(histItem); ok {
608				m.ta.SetValue(it.rec.Input)
609				m.ta.MoveToEnd()
610				m.overlay = overlayNone
611				return m.launch(true)
612			}
613		case overlayLang:
614			if it, ok := m.langList.SelectedItem().(langItem); ok {
615				m.target = it.code
616				m.overlay = overlayNone
617				m.relayout() // pair segment width changed
618				if m.live && strings.TrimSpace(m.ta.Value()) != "" {
619					return m.launch(true)
620				}
621				m.clearResult()
622				return m, nil
623			}
624		case overlayModel:
625			if it, ok := m.modelList.SelectedItem().(modelItem); ok {
626				m.modelOverride = it.id
627				m.overlay = overlayNone
628				if m.live && strings.TrimSpace(m.ta.Value()) != "" {
629					return m.launch(true)
630				}
631				m.clearResult()
632				return m, nil
633			}
634		case overlaySuggest:
635			// Picking a suggestion updates the input and looks it up.
636			if it, ok := m.suggestList.SelectedItem().(suggestItem); ok {
637				m.ta.SetValue(it.word)
638				m.ta.MoveToEnd()
639				m.overlay = overlayNone
640				return m.launch(true)
641			}
642		case overlayPreset:
643			if it, ok := m.presetList.SelectedItem().(presetItem); ok {
644				m.preset = it.id
645				m.overlay = overlayNone
646				m.relayout() // style segment width changed
647				if m.live && strings.TrimSpace(m.ta.Value()) != "" {
648					return m.launch(false)
649				}
650				m.clearResult()
651				return m, nil
652			}
653		}
654		m.overlay = overlayNone
655		return m, nil
656	}
657	
658	// handleMouseWheel scrolls the active overlay list, or the result viewport.
659	func (m Model) handleMouseWheel(msg tea.MouseWheelMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
660		if m.overlay != overlayNone {
661			cmd := m.overlayListUpdate(msg)
662			return m, cmd
663		}
664		var cmd tea.Cmd
665		m.vp, cmd = m.vp.Update(msg)
666		return m, cmd
667	}
668	
669	// handleMouseClick routes clicks. In an overlay it forwards to the list; in
670	// normal mode a click focuses the pane it lands in (so the keyboard scrolls it),
671	// except a click on the footer row opens the language picker.
672	func (m Model) handleMouseClick(msg tea.MouseClickMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
673		if m.overlay != overlayNone {
674			cmd := m.overlayListUpdate(msg)
675			return m, cmd
676		}
677		if m.height > 0 && msg.Y == m.height-1 {
678			m.overlay = overlayLang
679			return m, nil
680		}
681		// The input box occupies the first inputH+2 rows (border + inputH + border);
682		// everything below it (down to the footer) is the result box.
683		if msg.Y < inputH+2 {
684			if m.focus != focusInput {
685				m.focus = focusInput
686				return m, m.ta.Focus()
687			}
688			return m, nil
689		}
690		if m.focus != focusOutput {
691			m.focus = focusOutput
692			m.ta.Blur()
693		}
694		return m, nil
695	}
696	
697	// recordFor builds a history record for a completed result.
698	func (m Model) recordFor(res engine.TranslateResult) store.Record {
699		src := m.source
700		if src == "auto" && res.DetectedSource != "" {
701			src = res.DetectedSource
702		}
703		target := m.target
704		if res.Target != "" {
705			target = res.Target // effective target (may differ in pair mode)
706		}
707		return store.Record{
708			SourceLang:   src,
709			TargetLang:   target,
710			Engine:       res.Engine,
711			Model:        res.Model,
712			Input:        m.lastInput,
713			Output:       res.Translation,
714			Alternatives: res.Alternatives,
715			Notes:        res.Notes,
716		}
717	}
718	
719	// relayout recomputes component sizes for the current window. The input/result
720	// boxes are rendered at .Width(m.width-2); lipgloss treats that as the TOTAL
721	// width, so the inner content area is that minus the box frame (border 2 +
722	// padding 2 = 4) = m.width-6. Wrapping content to anything wider makes each line
723	// overflow the box and get re-wrapped, orphaning a character per line.
724	func (m *Model) relayout() {
725		if m.width == 0 || m.height == 0 {
726			return
727		}
728		footerH := m.footerHeight() // wraps to width; may be >1 on narrow terminals
729		compW := m.width - 6
730		if compW < 1 {
731			compW = 1
732		}
733		resultH := m.height - footerH - (inputH + 2) - 2
734		if resultH < 3 {
735			resultH = 3
736		}
737		m.ta.SetWidth(compW)
738		m.ta.SetHeight(inputH)
739		m.vp.SetWidth(compW)
740		m.vp.SetHeight(resultH)
741		m.resultH = resultH
742		overlayH := m.height - 2 // overlays use a single-line footer
743		if overlayH < 1 {
744			overlayH = 1
745		}
746		m.hist.SetSize(m.width-2, overlayH)
747		m.langList.SetSize(m.width-2, overlayH)
748		m.modelList.SetSize(m.width-2, overlayH)
749		m.suggestList.SetSize(m.width-2, overlayH)
750		m.presetList.SetSize(m.width-2, overlayH)
751	}
752
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./internal/engine/google.go`

```text
1	package engine
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"encoding/json"
6		"fmt"
7		"io"
8		"net/http"
9		"net/url"
10		"strings"
11		"time"
12	
13		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/lang"
14	)
15	
16	// GoogleConfig configures the free, unofficial Google translate_a/single endpoint.
17	type GoogleConfig struct {
18		Endpoint  string
19		ExtraDT   []string // extra dt slots, e.g. "bd" (bilingual dict), "at" (alternates)
20		UserAgent string
21		Timeout   time.Duration
22	}
23	
24	// GoogleEngine is a keyless translation engine using Google's web endpoint.
25	// It is unofficial and best-effort: it can rate-limit (HTTP 429) or change shape
26	// without notice, so it lives at the end of the fallback chain.
27	type GoogleEngine struct {
28		cfg  GoogleConfig
29		http *http.Client
30	}
31	
32	// NewGoogle builds a Google engine with sensible defaults.
33	func NewGoogle(cfg GoogleConfig) *GoogleEngine {
34		if cfg.Endpoint == "" {
35			cfg.Endpoint = "https://translate.googleapis.com/translate_a/single"
36		}
37		if cfg.Timeout == 0 {
38			cfg.Timeout = 4 * time.Second
39		}
40		if cfg.UserAgent == "" {
41			cfg.UserAgent = "Mozilla/5.0 translate-cli"
42		}
43		return &GoogleEngine{cfg: cfg, http: &http.Client{Timeout: cfg.Timeout}}
44	}
45	
46	// Name returns "google".
47	func (e *GoogleEngine) Name() string { return "google" }
48	
49	// Supports reports that Google handles translation only (not dictionary).
50	func (e *GoogleEngine) Supports(m Mode) bool { return m == ModeTranslate }
51	
52	func (e *GoogleEngine) buildURL(req Request) string {
53		sl := req.Source
54		if sl == "" {
55			sl = "auto"
56		}
57		q := url.Values{}
58		q.Set("client", "gtx")
59		q.Set("sl", sl)
60		q.Set("tl", req.Target)
61		q.Set("dt", "t")
62		for _, dt := range e.cfg.ExtraDT {
63			q.Add("dt", dt)
64		}
65		q.Set("q", req.Text)
66		return e.cfg.Endpoint + "?" + q.Encode()
67	}
68	
69	// Translate performs one keyless translation (non-streaming).
70	func (e *GoogleEngine) Translate(ctx context.Context, req Request) (<-chan Chunk, error) {
71		if strings.TrimSpace(req.Text) == "" {
72			return nil, ErrEmptyInput
73		}
74		httpReq, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodGet, e.buildURL(req), nil)
75		if err != nil {
76			return nil, err
77		}
78		httpReq.Header.Set("User-Agent", e.cfg.UserAgent)
79	
80		resp, err := e.http.Do(httpReq)
81		if err != nil {
82			return single(nil, fmt.Errorf("google: %w", err)), nil
83		}
84		defer resp.Body.Close()
85		if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusOK {
86			body, _ := io.ReadAll(io.LimitReader(resp.Body, 512))
87			return single(nil, fmt.Errorf("google: http %d: %s", resp.StatusCode, strings.TrimSpace(string(body)))), nil
88		}
89	
90		var data []json.RawMessage
91		if err := json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&data); err != nil {
92			return single(nil, fmt.Errorf("google: decode: %w", err)), nil
93		}
94		res, err := parseGoogle(data, req)
95		if err != nil {
96			return single(nil, err), nil
97		}
98		res.Engine = e.Name()
99		return single(res, nil), nil
100	}
101	
102	// parseGoogle defensively walks the nested-array response. Positions: data[0] is
103	// an array of [translated, original, ...] segments (long text splits into many);
104	// data[2] is the detected source language.
105	func parseGoogle(data []json.RawMessage, req Request) (*TranslateResult, error) {
106		if len(data) < 1 {
107			return nil, fmt.Errorf("google: empty payload")
108		}
109		var segs []json.RawMessage
110		if err := json.Unmarshal(data[0], &segs); err != nil {
111			return nil, fmt.Errorf("google: bad segments: %w", err)
112		}
113		var b strings.Builder
114		for _, s := range segs {
115			var parts []json.RawMessage
116			if json.Unmarshal(s, &parts) != nil || len(parts) == 0 {
117				continue
118			}
119			var txt string
120			if json.Unmarshal(parts[0], &txt) == nil {
121				b.WriteString(txt)
122			}
123		}
124		res := &TranslateResult{Translation: strings.TrimSpace(b.String()), Target: req.Target}
125		if res.Translation == "" {
126			return nil, fmt.Errorf("google: no translation in response")
127		}
128		if len(data) > 2 {
129			var det string
130			if json.Unmarshal(data[2], &det) == nil {
131				res.DetectedSource = det
132			}
133		}
134		return res, nil
135	}
136	
137	// Detect uses the offline detector (cheap, no network round-trip).
138	func (e *GoogleEngine) Detect(ctx context.Context, text string) (string, error) {
139		return lang.Detect(text), nil
140	}
141	
142	// Available probes the endpoint with a tiny translation request.
143	func (e *GoogleEngine) Available(ctx context.Context) bool {
144		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, 1500*time.Millisecond)
145		defer cancel()
146		u := e.buildURL(Request{Text: "a", Source: "auto", Target: "en"})
147		req, err := http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, http.MethodGet, u, nil)
148		if err != nil {
149			return false
150		}
151		req.Header.Set("User-Agent", e.cfg.UserAgent)
152		resp, err := e.http.Do(req)
153		if err != nil {
154			return false
155		}
156		defer resp.Body.Close()
157		_, _ = io.Copy(io.Discard, resp.Body)
158		return resp.StatusCode == http.StatusOK
159	}
160
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	package engine
2	
3	import (
4		"strings"
5		"testing"
6	
7		"github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/internal/lang"
8	)
9	
10	func TestBuildTranslatePromptPairDirective(t *testing.T) {
11		req := Request{
12			Text:     "test",
13			Source:   "auto",
14			Target:   "zh-TW",
15			Preset:   PresetConcise,
16			Pair:     true,
17			PairHome: "zh-TW",
18			PairAway: "en",
19		}
20		sys, _ := buildTranslatePrompt(req)
21	
22		// The pair directive must name both languages so the model can detect + route.
23		for _, code := range []string{"zh-TW", "en"} {
24			if name := lang.Name(code); !strings.Contains(sys, name) {
25				t.Errorf("pair system prompt missing language %q (%s)", code, name)
26			}
27		}
28		// And it must forbid echoing the input unchanged (the "test → test" bug).
29		if !strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(sys), "never return the text unchanged") {
30			t.Errorf("pair system prompt is missing the no-echo instruction:\n%s", sys)
31		}
32	}
33	
34	func TestBuildTranslatePromptNoDirectiveWhenNotPair(t *testing.T) {
35		req := Request{Text: "test", Source: "auto", Target: "zh-TW", Preset: PresetConcise}
36		sys, _ := buildTranslatePrompt(req)
37		if strings.Contains(sys, "Bidirectional mode") {
38			t.Errorf("non-pair prompt should not include the pair directive:\n%s", sys)
39		}
40	}
41	
42	func TestConcisePromptForbidsEcho(t *testing.T) {
43		// Even outside pair mode, the concise prompt must not tell the model to echo a
44		// word in a different language.
45		if strings.Contains(translateSystemPromptConcise, "return the text unchanged") &&
46			!strings.Contains(translateSystemPromptConcise, "ALREADY in the target language") {
47			t.Errorf("concise prompt still has a bare echo escape hatch:\n%s", translateSystemPromptConcise)
48		}
49	}
50
```

```text
1	package config

5	func strptr(s string) *string { return &s }

7	// A [cli] / [tui] overlay overrides [general] for its front-end only; an unset
8	// overlay field inherits [general].
9	func TestResolvePerModeOverlay(t *testing.T) {
10		c := Default()
11		c.General.Preset = "contextual"
12		c.General.Tier = "fast"
13		c.CLI = &Overlay{Preset: strptr("concise"), Tier: strptr("max")}
14		c.TUI = &Overlay{Preset: strptr("dictionary")} // no tier → inherits "fast"
15	
16		cli := c.Resolve(Overrides{}, ModeCLI)
17		if cli.Preset != "concise" {
18			t.Errorf("CLI preset = %q, want concise", cli.Preset)
19		}
20		if cli.Tier != "max" {
21			t.Errorf("CLI tier = %q, want max", cli.Tier)
22		}
23	
24		tui := c.Resolve(Overrides{}, ModeTUI)
25		if tui.Preset != "dictionary" {
26			t.Errorf("TUI preset = %q, want dictionary", tui.Preset)
27		}
28		if tui.Tier != "fast" {
29			t.Errorf("TUI tier = %q, want fast (inherited from [general])", tui.Tier)
30		}
31	}

36		c.General.Preset = "contextual"
```

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
I'm piecing together how the styling system works—the `styles` struct gets initialized through `newStyles()` and attached to the TUI model, with the `dim` style specifically using a gray color value defined as a constant.

The CLI path doesn't use lipgloss at all, just plain output functions. There's no color profile management or ANSI stripping in the codebase itself, though those are available as indirect dependencies. For stdin, the code reads everything and trims whitespace without any ANSI processing. The output logic in `oneShot` checks whether stdout is a TTY to decide between streaming or buffered output, with support for bilingual hooks at key points.
</details></think>

The dict engines use a single non-streaming call per request, and Google's implementation already handles splitting long text into segments server-side—which is crucial for understanding question 4. The LLM prompt outputs only the translation without guaranteeing line count preservation.

<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Confirm errgroup usage, read errors, check dict engine

`grep -rn "errgroup\|x/sync" --include="*.go" . ; echo "---ERRORS---"; cat internal/engine/errors.go; echo "---DICT Translate---"; grep -n "func.*Translate\|single(\|Supports" internal/engine/dict.go`

```text
---ERRORS---
package engine

import "errors"

// Sentinel errors returned across the engine layer.
var (
	// ErrEmptyInput is returned when a request has no text to translate.
	ErrEmptyInput = errors.New("engine: empty input")
	// ErrNoResult is returned when a channel closed without a terminal result.
	ErrNoResult = errors.New("engine: stream closed without a result")
	// ErrNoEngineForMode is returned by the chain when no engine supports the mode.
	ErrNoEngineForMode = errors.New("engine: no engine supports this mode")
	// ErrAllEnginesFailed is returned by the chain when every candidate failed.
	ErrAllEnginesFailed = errors.New("engine: all engines failed")
	// ErrNoDictEntry is returned when a dictionary lookup finds nothing.
	ErrNoDictEntry = errors.New("engine: no dictionary entry")
)
---DICT Translate---
59:// Supports reports that this engine handles dictionary lookups only.
60:func (e *DictEngine) Supports(m Mode) bool { return m == ModeDict }
74:func (e *DictEngine) Translate(ctx context.Context, req Request) (<-chan Chunk, error) {
82:		return single(e.result(entry, req), nil), nil
```

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-20 05:06:35Z)**_

I have a complete picture. Here are my findings.

## Findings: one-shot CLI output & styling map

### 1. The pipe branch and `oneShot` output flow

**Pipe branch** — `cmd/root.go:204-222`:
- `root.go:204` `case !term.IsTerminal(int(os.Stdin.Fd())):` — the piped-stdin dispatch arm.
- `root.go:205` `b, err := io.ReadAll(os.Stdin)` reads all stdin.
- `root.go:209` `text := strings.TrimSpace(string(b))` — the only transform on the input.
- `root.go:214` calls `oneShot(ctx, oneShotEng, text, src, effTgt, res.Stream, ...)`. Note the args branch (`root.go:191-202`) calls the identical `oneShot` — both paths funnel through it, so a bilingual branch inside `oneShot` covers both.

**Output writing in `oneShot`** — `cmd/root.go:414-472`:
- `root.go:415` `stdoutTTY := term.IsTerminal(int(os.Stdout.Fd()))` — the stdout-TTY probe.
- `root.go:416` `stream := streamPref && stdoutTTY && !flagJSON && !learn` — **the streaming vs non-streaming decision**. Streaming is gated on stdout being a TTY, so `translate x | pbcopy` never streams (goes to the non-stream `default` arm).
- `root.go:436-439` streaming token callback: `onTok = func(t string) { fmt.Print(t) }` — raw tokens, no styling.
- `root.go:440` `res, err := engine.Drain(ch, onTok)` drains the channel.
- Final output switch, `root.go:457-470`:
  - `root.go:458-463` `flagJSON`: `json.NewEncoder(os.Stdout)` full structured result.
  - `root.go:464-465` `res.Learn != nil`: `fmt.Print(renderLearnCLI(res))`.
  - `root.go:466-467` `stream`: just `fmt.Println()` (tokens already printed).
  - `root.go:468-469` `default`: `fmt.Println(res.Translation)` — **the plain non-streaming pipe output**, the single line most piped runs hit.

**Where a bilingual branch hooks cleanly:** the `default`/`stream` arms of the `root.go:457` switch, where both the original (`text` param) and `res.Translation` are in scope. For the pipe/immersive case specifically, `stream` is already `false` on a pipe, so the `default` arm at `root.go:468-469` is the natural single insertion point. `oneShot` receives `text` (original) as a parameter (`root.go:414`), so no plumbing is needed to print original + translation.

### 2. lipgloss usage — TUI-only

- **No lipgloss on the CLI path.** The only real import is `internal/tui/styles.go:3` (`import lg "charm.land/lipgloss/v2"`) and `internal/tui/view.go:8`. The two hits in `update.go:720` / `view.go:102` are just comments. CLI output is all `fmt.Print`/`fmt.Println`.
- **The dim style** — `internal/tui/styles.go:46`: `dim: lg.NewStyle().Foreground(colDim)`, where `colDim = lg.Color("#6C6C6C")` (`styles.go:8`). Note: it does **NOT** use `.Faint(true)` anywhere — "dim" is a hardcoded grey hex foreground. The related `notes` style adds italics: `styles.go:43` `notes: lg.NewStyle().Foreground(colDim).Italic(true)`.
- **Coupling:** the `styles` struct, `newStyles()`, and the `dim` field are all package-private to `tui` (`model.go:87` `st styles`, `model.go:214` `st: newStyles()`, consumed via `m.st.dim.Render(...)` in `view.go`/`update.go`). So the helper itself is TUI-coupled and not importable as-is, but the underlying primitive is a one-liner (`lg.NewStyle().Foreground(lg.Color("#6C6C6C"))`) that's trivially reproducible for CLI stdout. There is no shared/exported style helper today.

### 3. Color-capability detection for stdout — essentially none

- **No `colorprofile`, `NO_COLOR`, or `termenv` usage anywhere in source** (all three greps returned zero hits in `.go` files).
- **No `SetColorProfile` / `NewRenderer` / `SetDefaultRenderer`** anywhere — lipgloss is used with its default global renderer inside the TUI only.
- `github.com/charmbracelet/colorprofile v0.4.3 // indirect` in `go.mod` is a transitive dep of bubbletea/lipgloss, not referenced directly.
- The only terminal detection is the three `term.IsTerminal` calls (golang.org/x/term): `root.go:120` (stdin, mode selection), `root.go:204` (stdin, pipe dispatch), `root.go:415` (stdout, stream gate). None of these consult `NO_COLOR` or a color profile — stdout styling capability is not detected at all today.

### 4. stdin read + no ANSI strip — confirmed

- `cmd/root.go:205` `b, err := io.ReadAll(os.Stdin)` — the exact read.
- `cmd/root.go:209` `text := strings.TrimSpace(string(b))` — the exact trim, and the **only** processing.
- **No ANSI strip on stdin (or anywhere):** the `ansi.` / `x/ansi` / `Strip` grep returned zero source hits. `github.com/charmbracelet/x/ansi v0.11.7 // indirect` is available as a transitive dep (it exposes `ansi.Strip`), but is not imported. The trimmed `text` is passed straight into `oneShot` → `engine.Request.Text`, so any ANSI in piped input reaches the LLM verbatim.

### 5. Existing "original + translation" pair renderers — reuse candidates

Both return a plain-text (no-ANSI) `string` built with `strings.Builder`, printed via `fmt.Print`:

- **`renderLearnCLI`** — `cmd/root.go:485-544`. Comment `root.go:484`: "formats a learn-mode result as plain text (no ANSI, pipe-safe)." Shape: emits a primary line then indented children. Notably it already renders **foreign + native pairs**: `root.go:533-538` writes `"  ✎ " + ex.Foreign` then `"    ↳ " + ex.Native`, and the correct-direction branch does `"✔ " + corrected` then `"  ↳ " + translation` (`root.go:496-499`). This is the closest existing "original-then-translation-beneath" pattern, using a `↳` continuation glyph and 2-space indent — but it is unstyled plain ASCII/Unicode, no dim.
- **`renderDict`** — `cmd/define.go:84-111`. Comment `define.go:83`: "formats a dictionary result as plain text (no ANSI, pipe-safe)." Shape: headword (+ phonetic) line, then indented part-of-speech / bulleted definitions (`"    • " + def.Text`) with quoted examples. Called at `define.go:62` `fmt.Print(renderDict(res2))`.

Both establish the project's convention: CLI renderers build a `strings.Builder`, use leading-space indentation + glyphs (`•`, `↳`, `✎`, `ⓘ`), append `\n`, and are explicitly documented as ANSI-free/pipe-safe. Neither applies any color/dim styling today — styling on the CLI path would be net-new.

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-20 05:07:08Z)**_

I have everything I need. Here are my findings.

## Findings: engine capabilities & concurrency for a batch/bilingual pipe mode

### 1. `internal/engine/engine.go` — core types

**Engine interface** (`engine.go:183-189`):
```go
type Engine interface {
	Name() string
	Translate(ctx context.Context, req Request) (<-chan Chunk, error)
	Detect(ctx context.Context, text string) (string, error)
	Available(ctx context.Context) bool
	Supports(m Mode) bool
}
```
`Translate` returns a receive-only channel that MUST close after exactly one terminal `Chunk` (`ChunkDone` or `ChunkError`). Non-streaming engines emit a single `ChunkDone`; streaming engines emit zero+ `ChunkToken` then one `ChunkDone` (`engine.go:174-189`). "Channel closed == finished" is the shared contract.

**Request struct** (`engine.go:23-53`) — fields: `Text`, `Source`, `Target`, `Mode`, `MaxAlts int`, `Stream bool`, `Model string`, `ModelProvider string`, `Preset string`, `Extra string`, `Pair bool`, `PairHome string`, `PairAway string`, `Learn bool`. There is NO batch/multi-text field — one `Request` carries one `Text`.

**TranslateResult struct** (`engine.go:57-95`) — fields: `Translation string`, `DetectedSource string`, `Target string`, `Alternatives []string`, `Notes string`, `Confidence float64`, `Warnings []string`, `Truncated bool` (transient), `Engine string`, `Model string`, `Dictionary *DictEntry`, `Learn *LearnResult`, `Suggestions []string`, `SuggestDistance int` (transient). Single `Translation` string — no per-line array.

**Streaming channel/chunk types** (`engine.go:154-172`): `ChunkKind` enum `ChunkToken`/`ChunkDone`/`ChunkError`; `Chunk{ Kind, Text string, Result *TranslateResult, Err error }`. Helper `single(res, err)` (`engine.go:193-202`) returns a closed 1-buffered channel with one terminal chunk — used by all non-streaming engines.

**Drain** (`engine.go:207-221`): loops over the channel; forwards `ChunkToken` text to `onToken` if non-nil; returns `c.Result` on `ChunkDone`, `c.Err` on `ChunkError`, else `ErrNoResult`. It blocks until the channel closes.

### 2. Single synchronous translation — CONFIRMED

`cmd/root.go oneShot` (`root.go:414-472`) is the model: it builds a `Request`, calls `ch, err := eng.Translate(ctx, req)` (`root.go:431`), then `res, err := engine.Drain(ch, onTok)` (`root.go:440`). `onTok` is nil unless streaming to a TTY (`root.go:436-439`).

**Yes** — you can call `eng.Translate(ctx, Request{..., Stream:false})` and `engine.Drain(ch, nil)` to get one blocking `*TranslateResult` whose `.Translation` is the string. With `Stream:false`, LLM engines take the non-streaming JSON branch (`llm.go:369`, `408-431`) and emit a single `ChunkDone`; `Drain` with nil `onToken` just returns that result. Non-streaming engines (google/dict) ignore `Stream` entirely.

### 3. Engines & existing batching/concurrency

Engines: **LLM** (`llm.go`, OpenAI `/chat/completions` + Anthropic `/v1/messages`, streaming), **Google** (`google.go`, keyless, non-streaming), **Dict** (`dict.go`, `Supports(ModeDict)` only, non-streaming via `single()`), **Chain** (`chain.go`, "auto" fallback router), **SmartAuto** (`smartauto.go`, routes single word→dict, phrase→LLM), plus smartdict/localdict.

**No batch API exists** — every engine translates exactly one `req.Text`. To translate N lines you call `Translate` N times.

**One notable exception for cost**: the Google engine already returns multiple segments — `parseGoogle` walks `data[0]`, an array of `[translated, original, ...]` segments ("long text splits into many", `google.go:102-123`), and concatenates them. Google splits on its own server-side; it does not give you a clean 1-input-line→1-output-line mapping, it re-joins into one `Translation` string.

**Concurrency patterns in the repo**: the only goroutine pattern is the per-engine `make(chan Chunk, 32); go func(){ defer close(ch); ... }()` producer (`llm.go:392-393`, `llm.go:471-472`, `chain.go:86-87`, `smartdict.go:69-70`). `Chain` uses a `sync.Mutex` to guard its availability cache (`chain.go:22-23,56-67`).

**There is NO `errgroup` / `sync.WaitGroup` / fan-out anywhere** — grep for `errgroup`/`x/sync` in `*.go` returns nothing (`golang.org/x/sync v0.20.0` is only an *indirect* dep via bubbletea, `go.mod`). The chain iterates engines **serially** (`chain.go:91`), never concurrently.

**The TUI never fires concurrent engine calls.** `launch` (`update.go:380-473`) is "the single place a translation begins" and it cancels any in-flight request before starting the next: `m.cancelInflight()` / `m.cancel()` then a fresh `context.WithCancel` (`update.go:412-416`). It fires exactly ONE `eng.Translate` per launch (`update.go:464`) and reads it via a self-resubscribing `waitStream(m.stream, seq)` loop in `handleStream` (`update.go:90-100`). Stale results are dropped by an `m.inflight`/`seq` guard (`update.go:91`). So there is no existing goroutine-fan-out utility you can reuse — you would introduce the first one (a plain `sync.WaitGroup` or `golang.org/x/sync/errgroup`, already in the module graph, over N `Translate`+`Drain` calls).

### 4. `buildTranslatePrompt` & line-count preservation

`buildTranslatePrompt` (`prompt.go:93-112`) builds a system+user pair for a single `req.Text`. The concise system prompt (`prompt.go:21-33`) says **"Output ONLY the translated text… No quotes, no explanations."** It says nothing about preserving line count or line breaks — so if you concatenate N lines into one `Text`, **nothing guarantees N output lines**: the model may merge, reorder, drop blank lines, or add/remove newlines, and there is no delimiter/index contract. The contextual and dictionary presets actively reshape output into their own multi-line formats (`prompt.go:36-63`), which would corrupt a per-line mapping.

**No existing structured/multi-line translate prompt exists.** The only structured prompt is learn mode: `buildLearnPrompt` (`prompt.go:183-195`) → strict single-JSON-object teach/correct schemas (`prompt.go:127-173`), parsed by `parseLearn`/`extractJSON` (`llm.go:329-356`) into `LearnResult`. Learn mode forces **non-streaming** and a larger token cap (`req.Stream=false`, `llm.go:369,444`; `learnMaxTokens=8192`, `llm.go:132,446-448`). That JSON pattern is the only precedent if you wanted one call returning N structured items — but it's built for one input, not a batch array.

Net: the cheapest reliable "N lines → N translations" is **N independent `Translate` calls** (one line each, `Stream:false`, `Drain(ch,nil)`), optionally concurrent. A single concatenated call is cheaper on requests but unreliable for line-count fidelity with today's prompts.

### 5. Context / timeout constraints you must respect

- Every engine method honors `ctx` cancellation (`engine.go:174`); the SSE readers select on `ctx.Done()` per token (`llm.go:560-564`, `613-617`). For N concurrent calls, derive per-call contexts (as the TUI does with `context.WithCancel(m.base)`, `update.go:415`) so you can cancel cleanly.
- **The `http.Client` timeout footgun (from TODO.md P2):** `NewLLM` sets a client-wide `http.Client{Timeout: cfg.Timeout}` defaulting to **60s** (`llm.go:37-46`). Go applies this to the *entire* request including reading the streamed body, so a long translation is cut at 60s mid-stream — surfaced as `⚠ truncated` (`markTruncated`, `llm.go:360-364`; `TranslateResult.Truncated`, `engine.go:73`). TODO.md P2 recommends dropping the whole-request timeout on the streaming path and relying on `ctx` deadlines. For a batch of N lines this matters two ways: (a) the 60s wall-clock cap is **per HTTP request, shared across concurrent calls only if they share a client** — actually each `LLMEngine` has one `*http.Client` (`llm.go:33`), so all concurrent `Translate` calls from one engine share that client but each `Do` gets its own 60s; individual calls are short so truncation is unlikely per-line; (b) if you instead do ONE big concatenated call, you're far more likely to hit the 60s cap. The `Available`/`Models` probes use their own short sub-contexts (`800ms` at `llm.go:70`, `4s` at `llm.go:216`) and should keep a short timeout.
- Google engine has its own 4s client timeout (`google.go:37-39`) and can rate-limit (HTTP 429) under burst — relevant if you fan out many concurrent Google calls; it lives at the end of the chain for this reason (`google.go:24-26`).

**Summary for your bilingual pipe mode:** there's no batch primitive and no existing fan-out utility — you'd loop `Translate`+`Drain` per line (the `oneShot` pattern minus printing), your choice of serial or a new `errgroup`/`WaitGroup` fan-out. Use `Stream:false` + `Drain(ch, nil)` per line for a clean blocking string. Google is the only engine that already multi-segments, but it re-joins to one string. A single concatenated LLM call is cheapest on request count but has no line-count guarantee under the current concise prompt and risks the 60s client-timeout truncation.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
I'm looking at the AGENTS.md file, which appears to start with a "Releasing & versioning" section. The Read operation returned 127 lines, though I'm not entirely sure if that's the complete file or just a portion of it.

Now I'm grounding myself in what I've learned: the engine interface has a `Drain` method that returns a single blocking result, and there's a `single()` helper. For translating multiple lines, I'd call `engine.Translate` once per line and drain each result, though there's no existing batching mechanism in place yet.

The key design question is whether to translate line-by-line or send everything in one call. Per-line translation is more robust because it preserves the exact line-to-translation mapping and avoids the fragility of trying to maintain line counts across a multi-line request. The tradeoff is that it makes N calls instead of one, which could add latency and cost, but for something like a tldr with ~20 non-blank lines, that's manageable with bounded concurrency. The downside is losing cross-line context—wrapped prose across multiple lines translates worse when handled individually—but for most tldr content where each logical item fits on one line, this limitation is acceptable for a v1 opt-in feature.

I'm reconsidering whether a hybrid approach might work better here. The key insight is that for tldr specifically, skipping code lines matters because translating command examples produces garbage—the real value is in translating the prose descriptions. So the heuristic complexity is justified for this use case, even if it adds some implementation overhead. I'll split the input into blocks separated by blank lines, then for each block check if it's code or prose. Prose blocks get stripped of ANSI codes and translated as a whole unit (one engine call per block), then I'll display the original with color followed by a dimmed translation beneath. This mirrors Immersive Translate's approach—keeps the engine call count low, preserves context within each block, and skips code entirely. For the dimmed styling in the CLI, I can either define a small style inline or reuse an existing color utility.

For the dimmed output, I need to respect TTY detection and NO_COLOR—lipgloss v2 handles color profile detection automatically, but I should only emit ANSI dim codes when stdout is actually a TTY. If output is piped, I'll skip the dim styling and use a plain prefix like "  ↳ " to mark the translation line instead, keeping the output clean for downstream processing.

On the ANSI stripping side, I need to strip color codes from piped input before building the request text—this applies to both bilingual and regular pipe modes. The tricky part is preserving the original colored lines for display in bilingual mode while feeding clean text to the LLM.

For the flag, `--bilingual` feels like the clearest name since it directly describes what the feature does, though I could mention `--immersive` as a potential alias to echo the Immersive Translate brand.

On scope, I should limit bilingual mode to stdin input in v1 — that's where the multi-line colored output really shines. For single arguments it's less compelling but wouldn't hurt. As for config, the reviewer was clear about not making this a pipe default, so I'll keep it flag-only for now and skip adding a config option to reduce complexity; we can always add that later if needed.

For documentation, the user wants supporting analysis docs covering the implementation approach and how this compares to competitors like Immersive Translate. Following the project conventions, this belongs in a design record under `backlog/` — something like `backlog/bilingual-immersive-pipe.md` — since we're actually shipping this and it involves weighing multiple options and competitor analysis. I'll mark the status appropriately and make sure the README reflects this feature. The design doc will cover implementation details, competitive analysis, rejected alternatives, and prior art from standards like XLIFF and tools like DeepL. For the README, I need to add user-facing documentation for the `--bilingual` flag and the ANSI-stripping behavior. Then I'll set up unit tests for the helper functions like `stripANSI`, `splitBlocks`, and the code block detection heuristic.

For the package structure, I'm thinking a new `internal/bitext` module makes sense—it would hold the block splitting logic, code detection, and a render function that takes the original block, translation, and TTY flag to produce the final output. This keeps the logic pure and testable while keeping `cmd/root.go` thin as the orchestration layer.

On the engine side, I need to refactor how translation works. Right now `oneShot` handles printing, history, and warnings all together. I should extract a lower-level helper—something like `translateOnce`—that just does the translation and returns the result string without printing, so both the existing `oneShot` flow and the new bilingual renderer can use it. That's a minimal refactor that avoids duplication.

For history tracking with bilingual output, I'm still deciding whether to record each block separately or skip history altogether.  Bilingual mode is more of a view-only feature, so I'll skip per-block history to avoid clutter—maybe just record the full concatenated translation if needed at all.

Streaming doesn't apply here since bilingual needs the complete block before rendering it beneath the original. On concurrency, translating blocks sequentially could add latency across multiple LLM calls, but adding a worker pool introduces ordering complexity. I'll keep it sequential for v1 and note concurrency as a future optimization.

Actually, for a typical tldr page with 8-12 prose blocks, sequential translation means 8-12 round-trips to the LLM, which could stretch to 10-30 seconds—that's rough UX. Bounded concurrency with a small pool (4-6 workers) is worth the effort; it's just a semaphore channel collecting results into an ordered slice. I'll include it in the design but keep the implementation straightforward.

I also need to handle the context timeout carefully: each LLM call is independent, so the 60-second HTTP client timeout applies per call, which is fine for short blocks. For edge cases, soft-wrapped prose (consecutive non-blank lines) gets treated as a single block and joined for translation, which actually handles that well. Hard-wrapped sections with blank lines between them will lose some context, but that's acceptable. Tables and columns are handled as blocks too.

I'm waiting for the three Explore agents to finish running so I can incorporate their findings—especially around flag-to-config wiring conventions and concurrency patterns—before finalizing the plan.
</details></think>

There are two distinct patterns for boolean flags: Pattern A flows through the config resolution pipeline (flag → overrides → config.Overrides → Resolve → Resolved → engine), requiring coordinated wiring across multiple files with matching field names, TOML tags, and default values; Pattern B appears to be a standalone flag approach that's still being detailed. Enabled && (flagSpeak || cfg.TTS.AutoSpeak)` — so the standalone flag gets OR'd with a config default, all evaluated directly at the call site without going through the Resolve chain. The [cli]/[tui] overlay system does exist and applies per-mode overrides through the Overlay struct, with Resolve handling the application of those overlays to the General config section.

The invocation mode (CLI vs TUI) is determined early and passed to Resolve, which means a "bilingual" default could follow the same pattern as Learn — adding a General bool field, an Overlay pointer, and an applyOverlay clause, with precedence documented as flag > env > [cli]/[tui] > [general] > default. The Resolve function already handles this OR-pattern for Pair and Learn, where Learn implies Pair.

For documentation, the README follows a structure with top-level ## sections (Install, Build & run, Usage, Engines & fallback, etc.) and feature-specific ### subsections like "### Pair mode" — so a bilingual/immersive mode would get its own ### subsection there, parallel to existing features.

Competitor analysis and design rationale belong in a backlog/<slug>.md file since this involves weighing multiple options and trade-offs. When a feature ships during this session, the TODO entry moves to Done via a promotion script, but the backlog document preserves the implementation and competitor analysis with a "shipped" status. Since this is a new mode or flag, it's a backward-compatible addition that needs versioning documentation alongside the analysis.

The formatting step uses `gofmt`, vetting uses `go vet`, and there's also `go mod tidy` for dependency management. The commit convention follows the pattern of `feat(scope):` for new features and `fix(scope):` for fixes like the ANSI-strip correction.

```text
Findings below. I did not write any files (read-only exploration); this is the convention map with file:line references.

## 1. FLAG wiring — two distinct patterns

Persistent flags are declared in `NewRootCmd` off `f := root.PersistentFlags()` (`cmd/root.go:67`), using `f.BoolVar`/`f.StringVar`/`f.BoolVarP`/`f.StringVarP` (`cmd/root.go:68-83`), backed by package-level vars (`cmd/root.go:30-47`).

There are **two ways** a bool flag is wired, and a new mode flag can use either:

**Pattern A — routed through config resolution (use if it should be configurable/env-overridable/overlay-defaulted).** `flagLearn` end-to-end:
- declared `cmd/root.go:41`; registered `cmd/root.go:78`
- mapped in `overrides()`: `Learn: flagLearn` (`cmd/root.go:108`)
- `Overrides.Learn` field (`internal/config/resolve.go:20`)
- merged in `Resolve`: `Learn: o.Learn || g.Learn || envVal("TRANSLATE_LEARN") != ""` (`internal/config/resolve.go:150`)
- `Resolved.Learn` field (`internal/config/resolve.go:45`), consumed in `runRoot` and threaded into `oneShot(... res.Learn)` (`cmd/root.go:194`) → `engine.Request.Learn` (`cmd/root.go:429`, struct field `internal/engine/engine.go:52`).

Pattern A requires **all** of: `General` field + toml tag (`internal/config/config.go:56`), an `Overlay` pointer field (`internal/config/config.go:43`) + an `applyOverlay` clause (`internal/config/resolve.go:104-106`), `Overrides` field, `Resolved` field, the `Resolve` OR-line, and optionally a `Default()` seed (`internal/config/config.go:158-237`). So Pattern A is heavier.

**Pattern B — standalone flag, read directly, NOT through Overrides/Resolve.** A flag does **not** need a Config field. `flagJSON`, `flagNoHistory`, `flagSpeak`, `flagSpeakLang` are read at their use sites:
- `flagJSON` → `cmd/root.go:416,458`, `cmd/history.go:72`, `cmd/define.go:53`
- `flagNoHistory` → `openStore`, `cmd/root.go:286`
- `flagSpeak` → `shouldSpeak`, `cmd/speak.go:56`: `return cfg.TTS.Enabled && (flagSpeak || cfg.TTS.AutoSpeak)`
- `flagSpeakLang` → `cmd/speak.go:92,107,110,131`

The **`--speak` flags are the closest precedent for an opt-in per-call mode with a config default**: a standalone flag var ORed against a config field read directly at the call site (`flagSpeak || cfg.TTS.AutoSpeak`, `cmd/speak.go:56`) — no Overrides/Resolve plumbing. `--json`/`--no-history` are pure standalone bools.

## 2. Per-mode overlay + where a "bilingual" default could live

Yes — there is a `[cli]`/`[tui]` overlay. `Config.CLI`/`Config.TUI *Overlay` (`internal/config/config.go:19-20`), `Overlay` all-pointer struct (`internal/config/config.go:33-48`). Applied in `Resolve`:

```
g := c.General                    // resolve.go:126
ov := overlayFor(c, mode)         // resolve.go:127
applyOverlay(&g, ov)              // resolve.go:128
```
(`overlayFor` `internal/config/resolve.go:64-72`; `applyOverlay` `internal/config/resolve.go:76-119`). The mode is chosen by `invocationMode(args)` (`cmd/root.go:116-124`) and passed to `Resolve` at `cmd/root.go:137`.

A "bilingual" default **could** live in config exactly like `Learn`/`Pair`: the boolean-mode merge pattern is `internal/config/resolve.go:148-156`, e.g.
```
Pair:  o.Pair || g.Pair || envVal("TRANSLATE_PAIR") != "",   // resolve.go:148
Learn: o.Learn || g.Learn || envVal("TRANSLATE_LEARN") != "", // resolve.go:150
```
with the "learn implies pair" post-step at `internal/config/resolve.go:160-162`. Precedence is stated at `internal/config/resolve.go:58-60` and `internal/config/config.go:32`: **flag > env > `[cli]`/`[tui]` > `[general]` > default**.

## 3. Tests — style and the ANSI/splitter question

- **No `cmd/` tests exist** (confirmed: no `cmd/*_test.go`). All tests are under `internal/*`. Consequence: helpers you want covered should live in `internal/`, not `cmd/`. Note `renderLearnCLI` (`cmd/root.go:485-544`) is a pure string builder but is **untested** precisely because it sits in `cmd/`.
- **Table-driven** is the norm for engine tests: `internal/engine/llm_test.go:212-239` (`TestNonStreamCompleteness`, `cases []struct{...}` + `t.Run`).
- **Config tests** are focused unit tests: build `Default()`, mutate, call `Resolve`, assert — `internal/config/resolve_test.go`, `internal/config/learn_test.go` (the learn flag→Resolve pattern, `learn_test.go:7-30`), `internal/config/tts_test.go`. Env precedence uses `t.Setenv` (`resolve_test.go:60`).
- **Pure prompt-directive tests** are the model for a new mode's prompt logic: `internal/engine/prompt_test.go:10-49` asserts substrings of `buildTranslatePrompt` output and prompt constants.
- **Pipe/stdin/oneShot**: no test drives cmd's actual stdin branch (`cmd/root.go:204-222`). The closest is the engine-level `runOneShot` helper (`internal/engine/llm_test.go:193-210`), which spins an `httptest.NewServer` and drives a non-streaming `Translate` — its comment labels this the "piped CLI / --json" path (`llm_test.go:191`). External-process code is tested via injected interfaces (`fakeRunner`, `internal/tts/tts_test.go:18-30`).
- **ANSI-strip + line-splitter**: convention says make them **pure helpers in `internal/`** (e.g. alongside `internal/engine/prompt.go`), then table-drive them like `llm_test.go` / `prompt_test.go`. Putting them in `cmd/` would leave them untestable given the no-`cmd`-test convention.

## 4. DOCS conventions — where the competitor/impl-analysis doc goes

There is **no `docs/` dir** (the `docs/<tool>.md` rows in the READMEs are hypothetical templates from the knowledge-harness skill, not an existing tree).

**User-facing feature docs = a new `###` subsection in README.md**, parallel to `### Pair mode (bidirectional)` (`README.md:107`) and `### Smart auto` (`README.md:99`), under a `##` section. README top-level `##` order: Install, Build & run, Usage, Per-front-end defaults, TUI keys, Engines & fallback, Dictionary mode, Developer note — Charm v2, Architecture, Roadmap & lessons learned. Flag also gets a row in the Usage table / flags line (`README.md:39`).

**Competitor analysis (Immersive Translate) + implementation/design analysis = `backlog/<slug>.md`**, indexed from `TODO.md`. This is dictated by:
- `AGENTS.md:39-45`: "### Long-term backlog → `TODO.md` + `backlog/` … Do **not** create new `ROADMAP.md` / `IDEAS.md` / `BACKLOG.md` files — `TODO.md` is the single index."
- `AGENTS.md:78-84`: add a `backlog/<slug>.md` companion when the item "**weighs multiple options (record trade-offs, not only the winner)**" or is `[L]`/`[XL]` — competitor analysis is exactly option-weighing.
- `backlog/README.md:22-30`: "Add a `backlog/<slug>.md` file when any of these apply: … Multiple options were considered (record the trade-offs…)".
- `TODO.md:10-11`: "Long-form research goes in `backlog/<slug>.md`."
- Direction table `pitfalls/README.md:18`: `backlog/<slug>.md` = **Future / "We thought about doing Y — what was the analysis?" / Index in `TODO.md`** — i.e. design rationale + competitor comparison. (Contrast: `pitfalls/` is Past/symptom-grep, not this.)

So: create `backlog/bilingual-immersive-mode.md` (or similar slug), add the alphabetical row to the Index table in `backlog/README.md:51-55`, and reference it from a `TODO.md` entry. Do **not** spin up a new `docs/` dir or top-level `*.md`.

Process notes that also govern this work:
- If the feature ships this session, move its `TODO.md` entry to `## Done` via `scripts/promote-todo.sh --title ... --summary ...` in the same commit, and mark the backlog doc `Status: shipped` (don't delete) — `AGENTS.md:90-96`, `TODO.md:50-56`. If it's explicitly *not* being built now, add it to `TODO.md` with `P#/[Effort]` tags (schema/lanes: P1/P2/P3/P?/Done, effort S/M/L/XL, e.g. `TODO.md:28,34-37,43-44`).
- Versioning: a new mode/flag is a **minor** bump — `AGENTS.md:12` literally cites "new mode/flag, e.g. `--learn`"; the ANSI-strip fix alone would be a patch (`AGENTS.md:13`).
- Commit style: `feat(scope): …` / `fix(scope): …` (`AGENTS.md:31-32`), confirmed in `git log`: `feat(learn): …`, `feat(tts): …`, `fix(cli): …`, `docs(agents): …`. So expect e.g. `feat(cli): bilingual/immersive pipe mode` + a `fix` for ANSI strip + `docs:`.

## 5. Justfile recipes for the verification section

From `Justfile`:
- build (`Justfile:7-9`): `go build -o translate .`
- test (`Justfile:43-44`): `go test ./...`
- check — the composite gate (`Justfile:28-29`): `check: fmt vet build`, where fmt (`Justfile:31-32`) = `gofmt -w cmd internal main.go` and vet (`Justfile:35-36`) = `go vet ./...`
- run (`Justfile:12-14`): `go run . {{ARGS}}`; tui (`Justfile:16-18`): `go run .`; tidy (`Justfile:39-40`): `go mod tidy`

There is **no dedicated lint recipe** beyond `just vet`. The release/verify gate in `AGENTS.md:19` is "`go test ./...` green, working tree clean." So a verification section should reference `just check` (fmt+vet+build) and `just test`.

### Quick answer to your embedded question
A new mode flag does **not** require a `Config` field + `Resolve` wiring — `--json`/`--no-history`/`--speak` prove a bool flag can be standalone (read directly at the call site). Use the full Pattern-A chain (`--learn`) only if you want the mode settable via `config.toml`, `TRANSLATE_*` env, or a `[cli]`/`[tui]` overlay/default. For an "opt-in per-call flag with an optional config default," the `--speak`/`cfg.TTS.AutoSpeak` hybrid (`cmd/speak.go:56`) is the cleanest precedent.
</output>
```

- merged in `Resolve`: `Learn: o.Learn || g.Learn || envVal("TRANSLATE_LEARN") != ""` (`internal/config/resolve.go:150`)
- `Resolved.Learn` field (`internal/config/resolve.go:45`), consumed in `runRoot` and threaded into `oneShot(... res.Learn)` (`cmd/root.go:194`) → `engine.Request.Learn` (`cmd/root.go:429`, struct field `internal/engine/engine.go:52`).

Pattern A requires **all** of: `General` field + toml tag (`internal/config/config.go:56`), an `Overlay` pointer field (`internal/config/config.go:43`) + an `applyOverlay` clause (`internal/config/resolve.go:104-106`), `Overrides` field, `Resolved` field, the `Resolve` OR-line, and optionally a `Default()` seed (`internal/config/config.go:158-237`). So Pattern A is heavier.

**Pattern B — standalone flag, read directly, NOT through Overrides/Resolve.** A flag does **not** need a Config field. `flagJSON`, `flagNoHistory`, `flagSpeak`, `flagSpeakLang` are read at their use sites:
- `flagJSON` → `cmd/root.go:416,458`, `cmd/history.go:72`, `cmd/define.go:53`
- `flagNoHistory` → `openStore`, `cmd/root.go:286`
- `flagSpeak` → `shouldSpeak`, `cmd/speak.go:56`: `return cfg.TTS.Enabled && (flagSpeak || cfg.TTS.AutoSpeak)`
- `flagSpeakLang` → `cmd/speak.go:92,107,110,131`

The **`--speak` flags are the closest precedent for an opt-in per-call mode with a config default**: a standalone flag var ORed against a config field read directly at the call site (`flagSpeak || cfg.TTS.AutoSpeak`, `cmd/speak.go:56`) — no Overrides/Resolve plumbing. `--json`/`--no-history` are pure standalone bools.

## 2. Per-mode overlay + where a "bilingual" default could live

Yes — there is a `[cli]`/`[tui]` overlay. `Config.CLI`/`Config.TUI *Overlay` (`internal/config/config.go:19-20`), `Overlay` all-pointer struct (`internal/config/config.go:33-48`). Applied in `Resolve`:

```
g := c.General                    // resolve.go:126
ov := overlayFor(c, mode)         // resolve.go:127
applyOverlay(&g, ov)              // resolve.go:128
```
(`overlayFor` `internal/config/resolve.go:64-72`; `applyOverlay` `internal/config/resolve.go:76-119`). The mode is chosen by `invocationMode(args)` (`cmd/root.go:116-124`) and passed to `Resolve` at `cmd/root.go:137`.

A "bilingual" default **could** live in config exactly like `Learn`/`Pair`: the boolean-mode merge pattern is `internal/config/resolve.go:148-156`, e.g.
```
Pair:  o.Pair || g.Pair || envVal("TRANSLATE_PAIR") != "",   // resolve.go:148
Learn: o.Learn || g.Learn || envVal("TRANSLATE_LEARN") != "", // resolve.go:150
```
with the "learn implies pair" post-step at `internal/config/resolve.go:160-162`. Precedence is stated at `internal/config/resolve.go:58-60` and `internal/config/config.go:32`: **flag > env > `[cli]`/`[tui]` > `[general]` > default**.

## 3. Tests — style and the ANSI/splitter question

- **No `cmd/` tests exist** (confirmed: no `cmd/*_test.go`). All tests are under `internal/*`. Consequence: helpers you want covered should live in `internal/`, not `cmd/`. Note `renderLearnCLI` (`cmd/root.go:485-544`) is a pure string builder but is **untested** precisely because it sits in `cmd/`.
- **Table-driven** is the norm for engine tests: `internal/engine/llm_test.go:212-239` (`TestNonStreamCompleteness`, `cases []struct{...}` + `t.Run`).
- **Config tests** are focused unit tests: build `Default()`, mutate, call `Resolve`, assert — `internal/config/resolve_test.go`, `internal/config/learn_test.go` (the learn flag→Resolve pattern, `learn_test.go:7-30`), `internal/config/tts_test.go`. Env precedence uses `t.Setenv` (`resolve_test.go:60`).
- **Pure prompt-directive tests** are the model for a new mode's prompt logic: `internal/engine/prompt_test.go:10-49` asserts substrings of `buildTranslatePrompt` output and prompt constants.
- **Pipe/stdin/oneShot**: no test drives cmd's actual stdin branch (`cmd/root.go:204-222`). The closest is the engine-level `runOneShot` helper (`internal/engine/llm_test.go:193-210`), which spins an `httptest.NewServer` and drives a non-streaming `Translate` — its comment labels this the "piped CLI / --json" path (`llm_test.go:191`). External-process code is tested via injected interfaces (`fakeRunner`, `internal/tts/tts_test.go:18-30`).
- **ANSI-strip + line-splitter**: convention says make them **pure helpers in `internal/`** (e.g. alongside `internal/engine/prompt.go`), then table-drive them like `llm_test.go` / `prompt_test.go`. Putting them in `cmd/` would leave them untestable given the no-`cmd`-test convention.

## 4. DOCS conventions — where the competitor/impl-analysis doc goes

There is **no `docs/` dir** (the `docs/<tool>.md` rows in the READMEs are hypothetical templates from the knowledge-harness skill, not an existing tree).

**User-facing feature docs = a new `###` subsection in README.md**, parallel to `### Pair mode (bidirectional)` (`README.md:107`) and `### Smart auto` (`README.md:99`), under a `##` section. README top-level `##` order: Install, Build & run, Usage, Per-front-end defaults, TUI keys, Engines & fallback, Dictionary mode, Developer note — Charm v2, Architecture, Roadmap & lessons learned. Flag also gets a row in the Usage table / flags line (`README.md:39`).

**Competitor analysis (Immersive Translate) + implementation/design analysis = `backlog/<slug>.md`**, indexed from `TODO.md`. This is dictated by:
- `AGENTS.md:39-45`: "### Long-term backlog → `TODO.md` + `backlog/` … Do **not** create new `ROADMAP.md` / `IDEAS.md` / `BACKLOG.md` files — `TODO.md` is the single index."
- `AGENTS.md:78-84`: add a `backlog/<slug>.md` companion when the item "**weighs multiple options (record trade-offs, not only the winner)**" or is `[L]`/`[XL]` — competitor analysis is exactly option-weighing.
- `backlog/README.md:22-30`: "Add a `backlog/<slug>.md` file when any of these apply: … Multiple options were considered (record the trade-offs…)".
- `TODO.md:10-11`: "Long-form research goes in `backlog/<slug>.md`."
- Direction table `pitfalls/README.md:18`: `backlog/<slug>.md` = **Future / "We thought about doing Y — what was the analysis?" / Index in `TODO.md`** — i.e. design rationale + competitor comparison. (Contrast: `pitfalls/` is Past/symptom-grep, not this.)

So: create `backlog/bilingual-immersive-mode.md` (or similar slug), add the alphabetical row to the Index table in `backlog/README.md:51-55`, and reference it from a `TODO.md` entry. Do **not** spin up a new `docs/` dir or top-level `*.md`.

Process notes that also govern this work:
- If the feature ships this session, move its `TODO.md` entry to `## Done` via `scripts/promote-todo.sh --title ... --summary ...` in the same commit, and mark the backlog doc `Status: shipped` (don't delete) — `AGENTS.md:90-96`, `TODO.md:50-56`. If it's explicitly *not* being built now, add it to `TODO.md` with `P#/[Effort]` tags (schema/lanes: P1/P2/P3/P?/Done, effort S/M/L/XL, e.g. `TODO.md:28,34-37,43-44`).
- Versioning: a new mode/flag is a **minor** bump — `AGENTS.md:12` literally cites "new mode/flag, e.g. `--learn`"; the ANSI-strip fix alone would be a patch (`AGENTS.md:13`).
- Commit style: `feat(scope): …` / `fix(scope): …` (`AGENTS.md:31-32`), confirmed in `git log`: `feat(learn): …`, `feat(tts): …`, `fix(cli): …`, `docs(agents): …`. So expect e.g. `feat(cli): bilingual/immersive pipe mode` + a `fix` for ANSI strip + `docs:`.

## 5. Justfile recipes for the verification section

From `Justfile`:
- build (`Justfile:7-9`): `go build -o translate .`
- test (`Justfile:43-44`): `go test ./...`
- check — the composite gate (`Justfile:28-29`): `check: fmt vet build`, where fmt (`Justfile:31-32`) = `gofmt -w cmd internal main.go` and vet (`Justfile:35-36`) = `go vet ./...`
- run (`Justfile:12-14`): `go run . {{ARGS}}`; tui (`Justfile:16-18`): `go run .`; tidy (`Justfile:39-40`): `go mod tidy`

There is **no dedicated lint recipe** beyond `just vet`. The release/verify gate in `AGENTS.md:19` is "`go test ./...` green, working tree clean." So a verification section should reference `just check` (fmt+vet+build) and `just test`.

### Quick answer to your embedded question
A new mode flag does **not** require a `Config` field + `Resolve` wiring — `--json`/`--no-history`/`--speak` prove a bool flag can be standalone (read directly at the call site). Use the full Pattern-A chain (`--learn`) only if you want the mode settable via `config.toml`, `TRANSLATE_*` env, or a `[cli]`/`[tui]` overlay/default. For an "opt-in per-call flag with an optional config default," the `--speak`/`cfg.TTS.AutoSpeak` hybrid (`cmd/speak.go:56`) is the cleanest precedent.

<tool-use data-tool-type="generic" data-tool-name="AskUserQuestion"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **AskUserQuestion**</summary>
**What should the opt-in flag be called for this bilingual pipe mode?**

Options:
- **--bilingual (+ -2)** - Descriptive; says exactly what it does (shows both languages). Short alias -2. Clearest for `--help`.
- **--immersive** - Echoes the Immersive Translate philosophy you referenced. Brandier, slightly less self-explanatory.
- **Both as aliases** - --bilingual as primary, --immersive as a hidden/secondary alias. Covers both mental models at a tiny cost.

**In mixed prose+code input (tldr, man, --help), how should code/command example lines be handled?**

Options:
- **Skip code, translate prose** - Detect indented command/code blocks (e.g. `rg pattern …`) and echo them verbatim with NO translation beneath; only translate prose descriptions. Best for the tldr use case, but relies on a heuristic that can misfire.
- **Translate everything** - Simpler and predictable: translate every non-blank block including command examples. But `rg pattern` → nonsense for code lines.

**Should bilingual mode be flag-only, or also settable as a config default?**

Options:
- **Flag-only (opt-in per call)** - Standalone flag like --json/--no-history. Never a pipe default — protects the Unix-filter contract (`cmd | translate | less/grep/wc` stays plain single-output). Simplest surface.
- **Flag + [cli] config default** - Also add a config toggle (default off) following the --speak / TTS.AutoSpeak precedent, so power users can set bilingual=true for the CLI front-end. More wiring (config/resolve/overlay).


**Answer:** --bilingual (+ -2), Skip code, translate prose" selected preview:
rg

Ripgrep, a recursive line-oriented search tool.
  ↳ Ripgrep,一個以行為導向的遞迴搜尋工具。

Recursively search current directory for a pattern:
  ↳ 遞迴搜尋當前目錄以尋找模式:

    rg pattern        ← code line: kept as-is, not translated, Flag-only (opt-in per call)
</details></tool-use>
