        "snippets": [
          "MINIMAL Go diff — cmd/version.go (package cmd). Add the var + early return; keep the rest of buildVersion() unchanged:\n\npackage cmd\n\nimport (\n\t\"runtime/debug\"\n\t\"strings\"\n)\n\n// version is injected at release/package time via, e.g.:\n//   -ldflags \"-X github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/cmd.version=v0.3.0\"\n// It stays empty for a plain `go build`/`go install`, in which case we fall\n// back to the Go build info (module version or VCS revision).\nvar version string\n\nfunc buildVersion() string {\n\tif version != \"\" {\n\t\treturn version // release/packaged build: trust the injected tag\n\t}\n\tinfo, ok := debug.ReadBuildInfo()\n\tif !ok {\n\t\treturn \"(unknown; no build info)\"\n\t}\n\t// ... existing logic unchanged: reads info.Main.Version + vcs.* settings ...\n}",
          "Homebrew formula (var in package cmd — note v#{version} because Homebrew version has no leading v):\n\ndef install\n  ldflags = \"-s -w -X github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/cmd.version=v#{version}\"\n  system \"go\", \"build\", *std_go_args(ldflags:)\nend\n\n# std_go_args(ldflags:) expands to: [\"-trimpath\", \"-o=#{bin}/translate\", \"-ldflags=-s -w -X github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/cmd.version=v0.3.0\"]",
          "goreleaser .goreleaser.yaml — override the default (which targets main.version and strips the v) to hit the cmd package and keep the v via {{.Tag}}:\n\nbuilds:\n  - id: translate\n    main: .            # main package is at repo root\n    env:\n      - CGO_ENABLED=0   # modernc.org/sqlite is pure Go\n    goos: [linux, darwin, windows]\n    goarch: [amd64, arm64]\n    flags:\n      - -trimpath\n    ldflags:\n      - -s -w -X github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/cmd.version={{.Tag}}",
          "goreleaser DEFAULT ldflags (documented), for reference if you instead name the var main.version in package main:\n-s -w -X main.version={{.Version}} -X main.commit={{.Commit}} -X main.date={{.Date}}\n# {{.Version}} = tag with leading v stripped (\"0.3.0\"); {{.Tag}} = raw \"v0.3.0\".\n# goreleaser's own repo additionally sets -X main.builtBy=goreleaser -X main.treeState={{.IsGitDirty}} but those are NOT part of the built-in default."

        "summary": "Pure-Go cross-compile is trivial (CGO_ENABLED=0, goos darwin/linux × goarch amd64/arm64). For Homebrew: GoReleaser DEPRECATED `brews:`/homebrew_formulas in v2.10 — the current, non-deprecated key is `homebrew_casks:`. There is no CLI-vs-GUI split anymore; a CLI ships as a cask via the `binaries:` stanza. Big gotcha: Homebrew removed the `--no-quarantine` bypass (Nov 2025), so an UNSIGNED cask binary triggers \"translate is damaged and cannot be opened\" unless you add a post-install `xattr -dr com.apple.quarantine` hook. The tap is targeted via `repository.{owner,name,branch,token}`; token must be a PAT with repo scope on the tap repo (convention: HOMEBREW_TAP_GITHUB_TOKEN) because the default GITHUB_TOKEN cannot push to a second repo. Default ldflags stamp `main.version/commit/date` — but this project reads runtime/debug.ReadBuildInfo, so nothing gets stamped; add `var version` and prefer it, falling back to ReadBuildInfo. GoReleaser's default archive names (darwin/linux + amd64/arm64) line up exactly with chezmoi `.chezmoi.os`/`.chezmoi.arch`, so `.chezmoiexternal` archive-file consumption needs no arch mapping.",
        "steps": [
          "Add a stampable version var to main.go (repo root) so goreleaser's default ldflags have a target; keep ReadBuildInfo as fallback. Snippet #3. This fixes the '(devel)' problem for release builds while leaving `go install @latest` working.",
          "Create the tap repo github.com/daviddwlee84/homebrew-tap (empty; goreleaser creates Casks/translate.rb on first release). Users then `brew install daviddwlee84/tap/translate`.",
          "Generate a classic PAT with `repo` scope (or fine-grained PAT with Contents:read-write on homebrew-tap) and add it as repo secret HOMEBREW_TAP_GITHUB_TOKEN in the translate repo. The built-in GITHUB_TOKEN stays for the release itself but CANNOT push to the tap repo.",
          "Add .goreleaser.yaml at repo root (Snippet #1). Note `version: 2`, `formats: [tar.gz]` (v2 renamed `format`→`formats`), CGO_ENABLED=0, homebrew_casks with the quarantine-strip post-install hook.",
          "Add .github/workflows/release.yml (Snippet #2): trigger on `v*` tag push, checkout fetch-depth:0, setup-go via go-version-file: go.mod (go 1.26.4), goreleaser-action@v7 with `version: \"~> v2\"` and both tokens in env.",
          "Validate locally before tagging: `go install github.com/goreleaser/goreleaser/v2@latest` then `goreleaser release --snapshot --clean` (builds all 4 archives, renders the cask, no publish).",
          "Tag and push: `git tag -a v0.3.1 -m ... && git push origin v0.3.1`. CI builds archives, creates the GitHub Release, and opens/commits the cask to the tap.",
          "For Go-less chezmoi hosts, add an archive-file external in the dotfiles .chezmoiexternal.toml.tmpl (Snippet #4) pointing at the release tarball; ties off backlog/release-binaries.md option B."

        "gotchas": [
          "`brews:` is DEPRECATED as of GoReleaser v2.10 (message: 'Deprecated in v2.10. Homebrew Casks should be used instead.'). It still works today but is on borrowed time — use `homebrew_casks:`. There is no separate maintained 'formula' publisher anymore.",
          "CASK QUARANTINE: Homebrew casks intentionally set com.apple.quarantine, and Homebrew removed the `--no-quarantine` escape hatch (~Nov 2025). An unsigned/un-notarized binary → macOS shows 'translate is damaged and cannot be opened'. You MUST add a post-install hook running `xattr -dr com.apple.quarantine` (Snippet #1) or sign+notarize (needs a paid Apple Developer ID — overkill for a single maintainer).",
          "Trade-off note: classic Homebrew *formulas* do NOT apply the quarantine bit, so an unsigned formula binary runs with no xattr hack. That's the one ergonomic reason some still use the deprecated `brews:`. If you value zero-Gatekeeper-friction over future-proofing, `brews:` is the pragmatic short-term choice; otherwise `homebrew_casks:` + the hook is the 2026 answer.",
          "ReadBuildInfo interaction: GoReleaser's default ldflags target `main.version/main.commit/main.date`. This project has none of those and reads info.Main.Version instead, so goreleaser builds would report '(devel)'. `-X` on a nonexistent symbol is silently ignored. Fix = add `var version` and prefer it (Snippet #3).",
          "Version string mismatch in URLs: the git TAG is `v0.3.0` (used in the release download PATH) but goreleaser strips the leading `v` from `.Version`, so the archive FILENAME is `translate_0.3.0_...`. chezmoi/curl consumers need `v{{version}}` in the path and `{{version}}` (no v) in the filename.",
          "Token: `repository.token` must reference a PAT (`{{ .Env.HOMEBREW_TAP_GITHUB_TOKEN }}`), NOT the workflow's GITHUB_TOKEN — the auto token is scoped to the translate repo only and will 403 when pushing the cask to homebrew-tap.",
          "v2 archive key change: use `formats: [tar.gz]` (list), not the deprecated singular `format:`. Default `wrap_in_directory` is false, so the binary sits at the archive root → chezmoi `path = \"translate\"`.",
          "Arch/OS mapping is a non-issue here: goreleaser defaults emit `darwin`/`linux` and `amd64`/`arm64`, which are byte-for-byte what chezmoi's `.chezmoi.os` and `.chezmoi.arch` return. Do NOT retitle Os to `Darwin`/`macOS` or you'll break the chezmoi template.",
          "workflow permissions: `contents: write` is required for the goreleaser-action step to create the Release.",
          "setup-go: prefer `go-version-file: go.mod` over `go-version: stable` since go.mod pins go 1.26.4 and `stable` could lag/lead the toolchain."
        ],
        "snippets": [
          "# Snippet #1 — .goreleaser.yaml (repo root)\nversion: 2\nproject_name: translate\n\nbefore:\n  hooks:\n    - go mod tidy\n\nbuilds:\n  - id: translate\n    main: .            # main package at repo root\n    binary: translate\n    env:\n      - CGO_ENABLED=0  # pure Go (modernc.org/sqlite) → static, cross-compiles freely\n    goos: [darwin, linux]\n    goarch: [amd64, arm64]\n    ldflags:\n      - -s -w\n      - -X main.version={{.Version}}\n      - -X main.commit={{.Commit}}\n      - -X main.date={{.CommitDate}}\n    mod_timestamp: '{{ .CommitTimestamp }}'  # reproducible builds\n\narchives:\n  - id: translate\n    ids: [translate]\n    formats: [tar.gz]          # v2: plural key (was 'format')\n    name_template: \"{{ .ProjectName }}_{{ .Version }}_{{ .Os }}_{{ .Arch }}\"\n    files: [LICENSE*, README*]\n\nchecksum:\n  name_template: 'checksums.txt'\n\nhomebrew_casks:\n  - name: translate\n    binaries: [translate]\n    repository:\n      owner: daviddwlee84\n      name: homebrew-tap          # repo: github.com/daviddwlee84/homebrew-tap\n      branch: main\n      token: \"{{ .Env.HOMEBREW_TAP_GITHUB_TOKEN }}\"\n    homepage: \"https://github.com/daviddwlee84/translate\"\n    description: \"Terminal translation CLI/TUI\"\n    # REQUIRED for unsigned binaries: strip quarantine or macOS says 'damaged'.\n    hooks:\n      post:\n        install: |\n          if OS.mac?\n            system_command \"/usr/bin/xattr\",\n                           args: [\"-dr\", \"com.apple.quarantine\", \"#{staged_path}/translate\"]\n          end\n\nrelease:\n  github:\n    owner: daviddwlee84\n    name: translate",
          "# Snippet #2 — .github/workflows/release.yml\nname: release\non:\n  push:\n    tags: ['v*']\npermissions:\n  contents: write            # needed to create the GitHub Release\njobs:\n  goreleaser:\n    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n    steps:\n      - uses: actions/checkout@v4\n        with:\n          fetch-depth: 0     # full history/tags for changelog + version\n      - uses: actions/setup-go@v5\n        with:\n          go-version-file: go.mod   # honors go 1.26.4\n      - uses: goreleaser/goreleaser-action@v7\n        with:\n          distribution: goreleaser\n          version: \"~> v2\"\n          args: release --clean\n        env:\n          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}                 # this repo's release\n          HOMEBREW_TAP_GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.HOMEBREW_TAP_GITHUB_TOKEN }}  # PAT, pushes cask to tap",
          "// Snippet #3 — main.go: hybrid version so BOTH goreleaser and `go install @latest` work\npackage main\n\nimport \"runtime/debug\"\n\n// Stamped by goreleaser default ldflags (-X main.version=...). Empty otherwise.\nvar (\n\tversion = \"\"\n\tcommit  = \"\"\n\tdate    = \"\"\n)\n\nfunc buildVersion() string {\n\tif version != \"\" {\n\t\treturn version // goreleaser release build → \"0.3.0\"\n\t}\n\tif info, ok := debug.ReadBuildInfo(); ok && info.Main.Version != \"\" {\n\t\treturn info.Main.Version // `go install ...@v0.3.0` → \"v0.3.0\"; local build → \"(devel)\"\n\t}\n\treturn \"dev\"\n}",
          "# Snippet #4 — dotfiles .chezmoiexternal.toml.tmpl (Go-less hosts, backlog option B)\n# NOTE tag path keeps the leading 'v'; goreleaser strips it from the filename.\n{{ $v := \"0.3.0\" -}}\n[\".local/bin/translate\"]\n    type = \"archive-file\"\n    url = \"https://github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/releases/download/v{{ $v }}/translate_{{ $v }}_{{ .chezmoi.os }}_{{ .chezmoi.arch }}.tar.gz\"\n    path = \"translate\"          # file inside the (unwrapped) archive\n    executable = true\n    refreshPeriod = \"168h\"\n# .chezmoi.os → darwin|linux, .chezmoi.arch → amd64|arm64 — match goreleaser defaults exactly."

        "Per new tag with A: edit two lines (url version) + recompute sha256. The formula `version` auto-derives from the url, so the -X updates itself. Keep a `test do assert_match \"v#{version}\", shell_output(\"#{bin}/translate --version\") end`.",
        "AUTOMATE A when tagging cadence rises (this, not B, is what makes the hand-bump obsolete): add a tiny on-tag GitHub Action (e.g. dawidd6/action-homebrew-bump-formula, or a ~15-line shasum+sed+commit-push) that rewrites url+sha256 in the tap repo.         "EMPIRICALLY CONFIRMED on this repo (go1.26.4): building from an extracted tag tarball (no .git) prints `translate version (devel)`, AND `-ldflags -X github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/cmd.version=v0.3.0` changes nothing — still `(devel)` — because cmd/version.go has no `var version` and buildVersion() only reads info.Main.Version. The formula/goreleaser -X trick is DEAD until the source change lands. This is the single most important prerequisite.",
        "Version stamping needs the var for A, B, AND C — every non-`go install` path builds from a tarball/checkout that yields `(devel)`. Only `go install path@vX.Y.Z` (proxy-resolved) and a clean git checkout sitting exactly on a v0.x/v1.x tag stamp Main.Version correctly. So `go install @latest` (the current primary path) already reports the right version; Homebrew build-from-source does NOT.",
        "ADVERSARIAL POINT AGAINST B (the supposedly-modern path): goreleaser deprecated `brews:` in v2.10 → you must use `homebrew_casks:`, and Homebrew removed the `--no-quarantine` bypass (~Nov 2025). An unsigned prebuilt cask binary triggers macOS 'translate is damaged and cannot be opened' unless you add an `xattr -dr com.apple.quarantine` post-install hook (or notarize with a paid Apple Developer ID).         "Prebuilt binaries are low-value here: a personal tap does NOT get official homebrew bottles (only homebrew-core CI builds those).         "A GH Action does NOT make Option A obsolete — it makes the manual url+sha256 bump obsolete while keeping build-from-source.         "Homebrew's `#{version}` strips the leading v: url `…/v0.3.0.tar.gz` → `version` == `0.3.0`. Inject `v#{version}` to keep the binary printing `v0.3.0` (matching today's Main.Version formatting); keep the `test do assert_match` consistent with the choice.",
        "`--HEAD` guard: for a `head` block, `#{version}` becomes `HEAD`, so `-X …=v#{version}` injects the literal `vHEAD`. Guard the ldflags for head builds or omit `head`.",
        "MAJOR-VERSION TRAP (safe today at v0.x, bites at v2): a tag whose major is >=2 is invalid for the unsuffixed module path, so even a clean checkout falls back to a pseudo-version. Adding `var version` immunizes all future paths against this.",
        "License: repo currently has NO LICENSE file (README only cites ECDICT's MIT for bundled data). `license \"MIT\"` won't block a personal-tap install but WILL fail `brew audit --strict`, and the absence is a hard blocker for homebrew-core (C). Add a real LICENSE or omit the stanza.",
        "go.mod pins `go 1.26.4`, so the brew `go` build dep must be >= 1.26.4.         "Repo/tap naming: `brew tap daviddwlee84/tap` maps to github.com/daviddwlee84/homebrew-tap; the `homebrew-` prefix is REQUIRED for the shorthand (docs say 'recommended', but without it users must tap by explicit git URL). Install command: `brew install daviddwlee84/tap/translate`.",
        "Existing install paths are untouched: the tap is a fully independent 3rd path. `go install @latest` + the chezmoi go_tools ansible role continue to work; no version-var change breaks them (for `@version` installs `version` is empty → falls back to Main.Version, still correct)."

      "snippets": [
        "cmd/version.go — add the injectable var and prefer it (THE prerequisite):\n\n// version is set at build time via\n//   -ldflags \"-X github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/cmd.version=v0.3.0\"\n// (Homebrew formula / goreleaser). Empty for a plain `go build`/`go install`,\n// in which case we fall back to module + VCS build info below.\nvar version string\n\nfunc buildVersion() string {\n\tinfo, ok := debug.ReadBuildInfo()\n\tif !ok {\n\t\tif version != \"\" {\n\t\t\treturn version\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn \"(unknown; no build info)\"\n\t}\n\tver := info.Main.Version // \"(devel)\" for tarball builds\n\tif version != \"\" {       // linker-injected wins over (devel)\n\t\tver = version\n\t}\n\t// … existing vcs.revision/time/modified handling unchanged …\n}",
        "Formula/translate.rb (Option A — build-from-source personal tap):\n\nclass Translate < Formula\n  desc \"Terminal translator / dictionary CLI + TUI\"\n  homepage \"https://github.com/daviddwlee84/translate\"\n  url \"https://github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/archive/refs/tags/v0.3.0.tar.gz\"\n  sha256 \"<shasum -a 256 of that tarball>\"\n  license \"MIT\" # only if you add a LICENSE file; otherwise omit\n\n  depends_on \"go\" => :build\n\n  def install\n    ldflags = \"-X github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/cmd.version=v#{version}\"\n    system \"go\", \"build\", *std_go_args(ldflags:)\n  end\n\n  test do\n    assert_match \"v#{version}\", shell_output(\"#{bin}/translate --version\")\n  end\nend",
        ".goreleaser.yaml (Option B — only when the chezmoi/Go-less/dict-asset driver exists; note casks + quarantine hook):\n\nbuilds:\n  - env: [CGO_ENABLED=0]\n    goos: [darwin, linux]\n    goarch: [amd64, arm64]\n    ldflags:\n      - -s -w -X github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/cmd.version={{ .Tag }}\narchives:\n  - formats: [tar.gz]   # v2 list form; wrap_in_directory defaults false → binary at archive root\nhomebrew_casks:\n  - repository:\n      owner: daviddwlee84\n      name: homebrew-tap\n      branch: main\n      token: \"{{ .Env.HOMEBREW_TAP_GITHUB_TOKEN }}\"  # PAT, NOT the workflow GITHUB_TOKEN\n    binaries: [translate]\n    hooks:\n      post:\n        install: |\n          system_command \"/usr/bin/xattr\",\n                         args: [\"-dr\", \"com.apple.quarantine\", \"#{staged_path}/translate\"]",
        "GitHub Action to automate Option A's hand-bump (keeps build-from-source; makes the manual step obsolete without goreleaser). On tag push, in the translate repo:\n\n- uses: dawidd6/action-homebrew-bump-formula@v3\n  with:\n    token: ${{ secrets.HOMEBREW_TAP_GITHUB_TOKEN }}  # cross-repo PAT\n    tap: daviddwlee84/homebrew-tap\n    formula: translate\n    tag: ${{ github.ref_name }}\n# (or a ~15-line shasum+sed+commit-push script against Formula/translate.rb)"

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-8 2026-07-20 06:47:54Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
So the current implementation pulls the version from Go's build info rather than using a linker-settable variable, which means tarball builds show `(devel)`. The project has three pre-1.0 releases tagged so far: v0.1.0, v0.2.0, and v0.3.0.

The codebase is pure Go with no cgo dependencies, uses Go 1.26.4, and has no LICENSE file (only citing ECDICT's MIT for bundled data). There's no CI pipeline yet, and the ~22MB binary doesn't embed the dictionary—it's downloaded at runtime via `dict update`. Current distribution relies on `go install @latest` and some ansible/chezmoi tooling, with goreleaser deferred as a backlog item since the maintainer (working solo on a low-star project) felt `go install` covered all current needs.

Now I'm thinking through the tradeoffs between the three distribution approaches—assessing the recommended path adversarially against the alternatives and considering what actually makes sense for this project's current state and maintenance capacity.

Option C (homebrew-core submission) would face immediate blockers: the project doesn't meet homebrew-core's notability threshold (roughly 30+ forks/watchers or 75+ stars for a single-maintainer tool), requires stable versioned releases, and would fail the license audit since there's no LICENSE file at the root. Homebrew-core also expects ongoing maintenance burden that doesn't fit a low-capacity solo maintainer. So for a personal low-star CLI, a manual tap makes more sense — I'd create my own homebrew repository with a formula that builds from source using Go, but I'd hit the version-stamping problem: without the git history in the tarball, it defaults to `(devel)` instead of showing the actual release version. I'd need to inject the version string via ldflags during the build.

The good news is that Go's standard build args already support ldflags, so adding a version variable and passing it through is minimal overhead — it's not strictly required to make the formula work, but it's the right call to avoid that regression and keep the version meaningful. The tradeoff is that each release means manually updating the formula with the new URL, recomputing the sha256, and if I'm injecting the version, updating that too.

A GitHub Action could automate this entirely — there are existing actions that bump Homebrew formulas on tag push, or I could write a simple script that updates the URL and sha256 automatically. That would eliminate the manual work, though it adds a small amount of CI complexity.

The key insight is that automation and prebuilt binaries are separate decisions. I could go with Option A plus a GH Action to handle the bumping, keeping the simplicity of building from source while removing the tedious manual step.

Option B takes a different approach: goreleaser handles cross-compilation, uploads binaries to GitHub Releases, and automatically pushes the formula and cask to the tap. But there's a 2026 gotcha — goreleaser deprecated the `brews:` configuration in v2.10 in favor of `homebrew`

_casks:`, and Homebrew itself removed the `--no-quarantine` flag in November 2025. This means unsigned cask binaries trigger macOS Gatekeeper warnings unless I add a post-install hook to strip the quarantine attribute. Proper signing and notarizing requires a paid Apple Developer ID ($99/yr), which feels excessive for a solo maintainer. The deprecated `brews:` path avoids this quarantine issue but is essentially on borrowed time, while the modern casks approach ships unsigned binaries that create friction for users.

Build-from-source (option A) sidesteps the quarantine problem entirely since the binary compiles locally rather than being downloaded. For a pure-Go project, the build dependency is just Go itself, which Homebrew installs automatically if needed. Compiling a 22MB binary takes maybe 30-60 seconds for module downloads and compilation. The question becomes whether saving that minute on first install justifies the complexity of maintaining prebuilt bottles for a low-visibility personal CLI.

A personal tap doesn't get official Homebrew bottles anyway—option B would mean distributing goreleaser archives via GitHub Releases and consuming them through a cask. The version-stamping problem persists with B as well: goreleaser targets `main.version` by default, but this project reads `info.Main.Version` instead, so the binary would still report `(devel)` unless I adjust the ldflags configuration.

Option B requires more infrastructure—a `.goreleaser.yaml` file, a GitHub Actions workflow with write permissions, a personal access token scoped to the tap repository, and the cask quarantine hook—but once set up, it's fully automated on each tag and also enables consumption through chezmoi's external archive mechanism for systems without Go installed.

My recommendation is to start with option A (manual builds to a personal tap), which has the lowest overhead and avoids the PAT and quarantine complications. The critical piece that all paths depend on is adding a linker-settable version variable in the cmd package and having buildVersion() check for it first. The version variable injection is the load-bearing change that makes `--version` work correctly across all build paths, so that's my top prerequisite; LICENSE is a close second since it's required for strict audits and blocks the C path entirely. I should escalate to B only when releases become frequent or a non-Go environment joins the fleet, since B's real value is the chezmoi multi-arch story and dictionary asset shipping—Homebrew's modern cask path actually makes B worse than A due to unsigned binary quarantine hooks. C isn't worth pursuing for homebrew-core given the low visibility, single maintainer, missing LICENSE blocker, and loss of formula control, plus it still needs the same version variable fix anyway.

A GitHub Action that bumps the formula on tag release—using something like dawidd6's action or a simple 15-line shasum-and-push script—gives me A's simplicity with B's automation, skipping prebuilt binaries and goreleaser entirely. This "A+" hybrid becomes the sweet spot once tagging cadence picks up; full B is only justified when I also want prebuilt artifacts.

The bump Action still needs a PAT to push cross-repo to the tap, same token surface as B, but without goreleaser or quarantine concerns. Now I need to verify the ldflags path for the version injection—it should be `github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/cmd.version` since the var lives in the cmd package, and buildVersion() should prefer the injected value. Let me draft the version.go snippet...

Drafting version injection code... Writing the Homebrew formula... Writing goreleaser config...
</details></think>

Now I'm weighing the distribution options. Option A—a personal build-from-source tap—looks like the right call because it sidesteps the macOS Gatekeeper quarantine problem that plagues Option B (prebuilt binaries via goreleaser cask). Homebrew dropped the `--no-quarantine` workaround in November 2025, so unsigned cask binaries trigger the "damaged" warning unless you notarize them or add an `xattr` hook—both expensive or fragile. A source-built formula compiles locally on the user's machine, so no quarantine bit ever gets set. Plus it's the lowest infrastructure overhead: no PAT, no CI pipeline, no goreleaser complexity. The trade-off is users need Go installed, but that's already a build dependency anyway, and for a low-star CLI, prebuilt bottles don't justify the hassle.

Before shipping this, I need to add a `var version` to cmd/version.go and make sure the build-time injection via `-X` actually works—the current code might be ignoring it. The automation piece is clean: a GitHub Action (dawidd6/action-homebrew-bump-formula) can auto-bump the formula's url and sha256 on every tag, keeping the build-from-source approach alive without touching goreleaser or casks. The repo would be named `homebrew-tap`, so users install with `brew install daviddwlee84/tap/translate`. One detail: Homebrew's `#{version}` macro strips the leading `v`, so I'll inject `v#{version}` in the formula to print `v0.3.0` correctly.

# Homebrew distribution — installability via `brew`

**Status**: researched (2026-07-20), not yet implemented — spike done
**Effort**: M
**Related**: `TODO.md` P3 · `cmd/version.go` (version prerequisite) · [release-binaries.md](release-binaries.md) (goreleaser/prebuilt path) · dotfiles `go_tools` role + `.chezmoiexternal`

## Context

`translate` installs today via `go install github.com/daviddwlee84/translate@latest`
(into `~/.local/bin`, also driven by the dotfiles `go_tools` ansible role). The ask:
also make it `brew install`-able, and document the whole path as the basis for
shipping.

**Verdict: feasible and low-effort** — but two in-repo prerequisites must land first
(both verified empirically on this repo's go1.26.4), and the "modern" prebuilt-cask
path has a new macOS Gatekeeper trap that makes the *simplest* path also the *best*
one for a single-maintainer CLI.

## Prerequisites (do these first — they block every option)

### 1. Add a linker-injectable `version` var (REQUIRED)

`cmd/version.go` derives the version **only** from `debug.ReadBuildInfo()`
(`info.Main.Version`). That value is synthesized by the Go toolchain **only** for
`go install path@version`; it is **not** a linker symbol. Empirically confirmed here:

\```
# extract the tag tarball (exactly what Homebrew builds from) and build:
git archive v0.3.0 | tar -x -C /tmp/t && cd /tmp/t && go build -o tb .
./tb --version                     # → "translate version (devel)"
# and -X is a SILENT no-op today, because there is no symbol to set:
go build -ldflags "-X github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/cmd.version=v0.3.0" -o tb2 .
./tb2 --version                    # → STILL "translate version (devel)"
\```

So any build that is **not** `go install …@version` (Homebrew tarball build,
goreleaser, plain `go build`) reports `(devel)`. Fix — add a package-level var and
prefer it, keeping the existing ReadBuildInfo logic as the fallback:

\```go
// cmd/version.go  (package cmd)
// version is injected at package/release time via
//   -ldflags "-X github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/cmd.version=v0.3.0"
// Empty for a plain `go build`/`go install`, where we fall back to build info.
var version string

func buildVersion() string {
	if version != "" {
		return version // Homebrew/goreleaser build → the injected tag
	}
	info, ok := debug.ReadBuildInfo()
	// … existing logic unchanged: info.Main.Version + vcs.revision/time/modified …
}
\```

Note the `-X` path is the **full package path** `github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/cmd.version`
(the var lives in package `cmd`, not `main`); a wrong path fails silently.
`go install …@vX.Y.Z` keeps working unchanged (var empty → ReadBuildInfo → correct).

### 2. Add a `LICENSE` file (recommended)

The repo has **no** `LICENSE` today (README only cites ECDICT's MIT for the bundled
*dictionary data*). A personal tap will install without one, but `brew audit --strict`
complains and homebrew-core hard-requires it. Add a real license (e.g. MIT) so the
formula's `license "MIT"` stanza is truthful.

## Options

| Option | What | For this project |
|---|---|---|
| **A. Personal build-from-source tap** | a `homebrew-tap` repo with `Formula/translate.rb` → tag tarball + sha256, `depends_on "go" => :build`, `go build` | **Recommended now.** Lowest infra (no CI/PAT), and the source-built binary carries **no macOS quarantine bit** (see B's trap). |
| **B. goreleaser + prebuilt archives + auto-published cask** | cross-compiled binaries on GitHub Releases; goreleaser pushes a cask to the tap on each tag | **Defer.** Adds CI + a cross-repo PAT + the quarantine hook. Only worth it alongside the *other* driver (chezmoi Go-less hosts / ship dict DB) — see [release-binaries.md](release-binaries.md). |
| **C. Submit to homebrew-core** | the central tap; `brew install translate` | **Skip.** Notability bar (stars/usage), cedes formula control (autobumps), same prereqs, LICENSE hard-required. |

### Why not the "modern" prebuilt path (B) yet — the Gatekeeper trap

goreleaser **deprecated `brews:` (formulae) in v2.10**; the current key is
`homebrew_casks:`. But Homebrew **removed the `--no-quarantine` bypass (~Nov 2025)**,
so an **unsigned** prebuilt cask binary triggers *"translate is damaged and cannot be
opened"* on macOS unless you either add a post-install `xattr -dr com.apple.quarantine`
hook or notarize with a paid Apple Developer ID ($99/yr). Option A sidesteps this
entirely — the binary is compiled **locally**, so no quarantine bit is ever set. For a
low-star CLI where Go is a build dep either way, prebuilt bottles buy only ~30–60 s of
first-install build time and are not worth the pipeline.

## Option A — concrete setup (recommended)

1. **Prereqs above** land in a tagged release (e.g. cut `v0.3.1` after adding
   `var version` + `LICENSE`).
2. **Create repo** `github.com/daviddwlee84/homebrew-tap` (the `homebrew-` prefix is
   mandatory for the `brew tap daviddwlee84/tap` shorthand).
3. **Add `Formula/translate.rb`** (filename → class `Translate`):

\```ruby
class Translate < Formula
  desc "Fast terminal translation tool (CLI + TUI)"
  homepage "https://github.com/daviddwlee84/translate"
  url "https://github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/archive/refs/tags/v0.3.0.tar.gz"
  sha256 "479b6d6059948ef3bb2a6f2a861f785438926437019ea5f7539abcb4246c9f2c"
  license "MIT" # only once a LICENSE file exists; else drop this line
  head "https://github.com/daviddwlee84/translate.git", branch: "main"

  depends_on "go" => :build

  def install
    # ReadBuildInfo yields "(devel)" from a tarball, so inject the tag via -X.
    # Homebrew's #{version} strips the leading v (0.3.0), so re-add it: v#{version}.
    ldflags = "-s -w -X github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/cmd.version=v#{version}"
    system "go", "build", *std_go_args(ldflags:)   # std_go_args adds -trimpath + -o bin/translate
  end

  test do
    assert_match "v#{version}", shell_output("#{bin}/translate --version")
  end
end
\```

4. **Compute the sha256** for a new tag:
   `curl -fsSL https://github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/archive/refs/tags/vX.Y.Z.tar.gz | shasum -a 256`
   (GitHub auto-tarballs are stable per tag, but always pin the value you computed).
5. **Validate:** `brew install --build-from-source daviddwlee84/tap/translate`,
   `brew test …`, `brew audit --strict --online daviddwlee84/tap/translate`.
6. **Users install:** `brew install daviddwlee84/tap/translate` (auto-taps).

Notes: no `vendor/` needed — Homebrew's sandbox blocks filesystem writes, **not**
network, so `go build` fetches modules from the proxy at install time (same as
homebrew-core `fzf`/`gh`). `go.mod` pins `go 1.26.4`, so brew's `go` must be ≥ that.

### Maintenance / upgrade flow (how a new tag reaches brew users)

Per release: bump `url` to the new tag + replace `sha256` in `Formula/translate.rb`,
push the tap repo. `version` (and the injected `-X`) derive from the url automatically.
Users get it via `brew update && brew upgrade translate`.

**Automation sweet spot ("A + Action"):** an on-tag GitHub Action in the *translate*
repo that rewrites url+sha256 in the tap — keeps build-from-source (no goreleaser, no
cask, no quarantine hook) while making the manual bump zero-touch:

\```yaml
# .github/workflows/brew-bump.yml (in the translate repo), on push tags: ['v*']
- uses: dawidd6/action-homebrew-bump-formula@v3
  with:
    token: ${{ secrets.HOMEBREW_TAP_GITHUB_TOKEN }}  # PAT with repo scope on homebrew-tap
    tap: daviddwlee84/homebrew-tap
    formula: translate
    tag: ${{ github.ref_name }}
\```

(The default `GITHUB_TOKEN` can't push to a second repo, hence the PAT.)

## Option B — sketch (only when the chezmoi/Go-less/dict-asset driver appears)

goreleaser `version: 2`, `builds` with `CGO_ENABLED=0`, `goos: [darwin, linux]` ×
`goarch: [amd64, arm64]`, `ldflags: -s -w -X github.com/daviddwlee84/translate/cmd.version={{ .Tag }}`,
`archives.formats: [tar.gz]` (v2 renamed `format`→`formats`), and `homebrew_casks:`
with a `repository.token` PAT and a **post-install `xattr -dr com.apple.quarantine`
hook**. Release via `goreleaser/goreleaser-action@v7` (`version: "~> v2"`) on tag push
with `contents: write`. The default archive names (`darwin`/`linux`, `amd64`/`arm64`)
match chezmoi `.chezmoi.os`/`.chezmoi.arch` exactly, so a `.chezmoiexternal`
archive-file external needs no arch mapping — which is the real reason to adopt B (see
[release-binaries.md](release-binaries.md)). Full config in that doc when pursued.

## References

- Homebrew: [Taps](https://docs.brew.sh/Taps) · [Formula Cookbook](https://docs.brew.sh/Formula-Cookbook) · [`std_go_args`](https://rubydoc.brew.sh/Formula.html#std_go_args-instance_method)
- Quarantine change: Homebrew removed `--no-quarantine` (Nov 2025) — brew discussion #6537 / issue #20755
- goreleaser: [`homebrew_casks`](https://goreleaser.com/customization/homebrew_casks/) · [`brews` deprecation (v2.10)](https://goreleaser.com/deprecations/#brews) · [main.version cookbook](https://goreleaser.com/resources/cookbooks/using-main.version/)
- Go: [`runtime/debug.ReadBuildInfo`](https://pkg.go.dev/runtime/debug#ReadBuildInfo) — `Main.Version` is not linker-settable
- Auto-bump Action: [dawidd6/action-homebrew-bump-formula](https://github.com/dawidd6/action-homebrew-bump-formula)

Both done. Here's the wrap-up.

## 1. Tag shipped
- `v0.2.0` turned out to be **already published** (2026-07-15, the learn+tts+smart-auto release), so the bilingual work became **`v0.3.0`** (minor bump — new flag).
- Pushed: `main` → `5cfaaad`, tag **`v0.3.0`** → `d64a45f`. Verified `go install …@v0.3.0` resolves and reports `translate version v0.3.0`.
- **To roll it out**: `just upgrade-go` on each host (or via `fleet`) — `chezmoi apply` won't, it's install-only.

## 2. Homebrew — yes, feasible; documented in `backlog/homebrew-distribution.md`

**Recommendation: Option A — a personal build-from-source tap** (`brew install daviddwlee84/tap/translate`). It's the lowest-infra path and, importantly, **avoids a macOS Gatekeeper trap** the "modern" prebuilt route now has: Homebrew dropped `--no-quarantine` (Nov 2025) and goreleaser deprecated `brews:` (v2.10 → `homebrew_casks:`), so an unsigned prebuilt cask shows *"translate is damaged"* unless you add an `xattr` hook or notarize ($99/yr). A source-built formula binary is compiled locally → no quarantine bit.

**Two in-repo prerequisites first** (both I verified empirically on your go1.26.4):
1. **Add `var version` to `cmd/version.go`** — because version comes only from `ReadBuildInfo`, any non-`go install` build (tarball/goreleaser) reports `(devel)`, and `-X …/cmd.version=…` is a **silent no-op** without the var. Fix is ~6 lines (prefer the injected var, keep ReadBuildInfo as fallback).
2. **Add a `LICENSE` file** — the repo has none today (README only cites ECDICT's MIT for the *data*); needed for `brew audit --strict` / homebrew-core.

The doc has a complete ready-to-use formula (with the real v0.3.0 tarball sha256), an **"A + GitHub Action"** auto-bump variant (zero-touch releases, still build-from-source), and a deferred goreleaser/cask sketch — to adopt only when the *other* driver appears (chezmoi Go-less hosts / shipping the dictionary as a release asset, per `release-binaries.md`). Skip homebrew-core (notability bar).

All committed on main (`5cfaaad`), TODO P3 + backlog index updated.

---

Two optional follow-ups I can do now if you want:
- **Land the prerequisites** (add `var version` + a MIT `LICENSE`) and cut `v0.3.1` — that makes the project genuinely Homebrew-ready; the tap repo itself is the only external step left for you.
- **Commit the two orphaned specstory transcripts** (07-14, 07-17) that are still sitting untracked from earlier sessions.
